      <tr>
        <td class="docs">

  <a class="pilcrow" href="#section-1" id="section-1"></a>
</div>

        </td>
        <td class="code highlight">
          <pre class="json">{
  <span class="hljs-attr">"groups"</span>: {
    <span class="hljs-attr">"logarithms"</span>: [
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Product Rule for Logarithms"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Express $\\log_{4}\\big(k^2 t\\big) + \\log_{4}\\big(t^2\\big) + \\log_4 k$ as a single logarithm."</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"First, let's recall the product rule for logarithms:\n\\[\n\\log_n a + \\log_n b = \\log_n(ab)\n\\]\nUsing the product rule for logarithms, we can combine the first two terms as follows:\n\\begin{align}\n\\log_{4}\\big(k^2 t\\big) + \\log_{4}\\big(t^2\\big) + \\log_4 k\n&amp; = \\bigl( \\log_{4}\\big(k^2 t\\big) + \\log_{4}\\big(t^2\\big) \\bigr) + \\log_4 k \\\\[5pt]\n&amp; = \\log_{4}\\big(k^2 t t^2\\big) + \\log_4 k\\\\[5pt]\n\\end{align}\nUsing the product rule for logarithms once more, we obtain\n\\[\n\\log_{4}\\big(k^2 t t^2\\big) + \\log_4 k = \\log_{4}\\big(k^2 t t^2 k\\big).\n\\]\nFinally, if we use the commutative property of real numbers and the addition law for exponents, we get\n\\begin{align}\n\\log_{4}\\big(k^2 t t^2 k \\big) &amp; = \\log_{4}\\big(k^2 k t t^2\\big)\\\\[5pt]\n&amp; = \\log_{4}\\big(k^{2+1} t^{1+2}\\big)\\\\[5pt]\n&amp; = \\log_{4}\\big(k^3 t^3\\big).\n\\end{align}\nTherefore,\n\\[\n\\log_{4}(k^2 t) + \\log_{4}(t^2) + \\log_4(k) = \\log_{4}\\big(k^3 t^3\\big).\n\\]"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\log_{4}\\left(k^3 t^3\\right)$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\log_{12}\\left(k^2 t + t^2 + k\\right)$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\log_{4}\\left(k^2 t + t^2 + k\\right)$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\log_{12}\\left(k^2 t^2\\right)$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\log_{4}\\left(k^2 t^2\\right)$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"H"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Product Rule for Logarithms"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Express $\\log_{3}\\big(p^3\\big) + \\log_{3}\\big(q^2\\big) + \\log_3(pq)$ as a single logarithm."</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"First, let's recall the product rule for logarithms:\n\\[\n\\log_n a + \\log_n b = \\log_n(ab)\n\\]\nUsing the product rule for logarithms, we can combine the first two terms as follows:\n\\begin{align}\n\\log_{3}\\big(p^3\\big) + \\log_{3}\\big(q^2\\big) + \\log_3(pq)\n&amp; = \\bigl( \\log_{3}\\big(p^3\\big) + \\log_{3}\\big(q^2\\big) \\bigr) + \\log_3(pq)\\\\[5pt]\n&amp; = \\log_{3}\\big(p^3 q^2\\big) + \\log_3(pq)\\\\[5pt]\n\\end{align}\nUsing the product rule for logarithms once more, we obtain\n\\[\n\\log_{3}\\big(p^3 q^2\\big) + \\log_3(pq)= \\log_{3}\\big(p^3 q^2 pq\\big).\n\\]\nFinally, if we use the commutative property of real numbers and the addition law for exponents, we get\n\\begin{align}\n\\log_{3} \\big(p^3 q^2 pq\\big)\n&amp; = \\log_{3} \\big(p^3p q^2 q \\big)\\\\[5pt]\n&amp; = \\log_{3} \\big(p^{3+1} q^{2+1} \\big)\\\\[5pt]\n&amp; = \\log_{3}\\big(p^4 q^3\\big).\n\\end{align}\nTherefore,\n\\[\n\\log_{3}\\big(p^3\\big) + \\log_{3}\\big(q^2\\big) + \\log_3(pq) = \\log_{3}\\big(p^4 q^3\\big).\n\\]"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\log_{3}\\big(p^4 q^3\\big)$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\log_{27}\\big(p^4 q^3\\big)$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\log_{9}\\big(p^3+q^2+pq\\big)$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\log_{3}\\big(p^3 q^2\\big)$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\log_{3}\\big(p^3+q^2+pq \\big)$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"H"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Product Rule for Logarithms"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Express $\\log(x+2) + \\log(5x)$ as a single logarithm."</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"First, let's recall the product rule for logarithms:\n\\[\n\\log_n a + \\log_n b = \\log_n(ab)\n\\]\nUsing the product rule for logarithms, the given expression can be rewritten as\n\\begin{align}\n\\log(x+2) + \\log(5x) &amp; = \\log\\big( (x+2)\\cdot 5x \\big)\\\\[5pt]\n&amp; = \\log \\left(5x^2 + 10x \\right).\n\\end{align}"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\log \\left(5x^2 + 10x \\right)$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\log \\left(5x^2 + 10 \\right)$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\log \\left(6x + 2 \\right)$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\log \\left(7x+2 \\right)$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\log \\left(5x^2 + 5x \\right)$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"M"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Product Rule for Logarithms"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Express $4\\log 2+4\\log 4 + 4\\log 8$ as a single logarithm."</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"First, let's recall the product rule for logarithms:\n\\[\n\\log_n a + \\log_n b = \\log_n(ab)\n\\]\nIf we factor the given expression and use the product rule for logarithms, we get\n\\begin{align}\n4\\log 2+4\\log 4+4 \\log 8\n&amp;= 4 \\left(\\log 2+4\\log 4 + \\log 8\\right) \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= 4\\log (2 \\cdot 4 \\cdot 8) \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= 4\\log 64.\n\\end{align}\nTherefore,\n\\[\n4\\log 2+4\\log 4 + 4\\log 8 = 4\\log 64.\n\\]"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$4\\log 64$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$4\\log 14$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\log 64$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$12\\log 14$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$64\\log 64$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"M"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Product Rule for Logarithms"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"$\\log_2(8 p) = $"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"First, let's recall the product rule for logarithms:\n\\[\n\\log_n a + \\log_n b = \\log_n(ab)\n\\]\nUsing the product rule, the given expression can be rewritten as\n\\begin{align}\n\\log_2(8 p) = \\log_2 8 + \\log_2 p.\n\\end{align}\nSince $8 = 2^3,$ we have\n\\[\n\\log_2 8 = \\log_2\\left( 2^3 \\right) = 3.\n\\]\nTherefore,\n\\begin{align}\n\\log_2(8 p) &amp; = \\log_2 8 + \\log_2 p\\\\[5pt]\n&amp; = 3 + \\log_2 p.\n\\end{align}"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$3 + \\log_2 p$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$8 + \\log_2 p$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$3\\log_2 p$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$1 + \\log_2(2p)$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$3 \\log_2(4p)$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"H"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Product Rule for Logarithms"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"$\\log_8(3z)=$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"First, let's recall the product rule for logarithms:\n\\[\n\\log_n a + \\log_n b = \\log_n(ab)\n\\]\nUsing the product rule, the given expression can be rewritten as\n\\begin{align}\n\\log_8 (3z )\n&amp; = \\log_8 (3 \\cdot z )\\\\[5pt]\n&amp; = \\log_8 3 + \\log_8 z.\n\\end{align}"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\log_8 3+\\log_8 z$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\log_8 3\\cdot\\log_8 z$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\log_8 3-\\log_8 z$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{\\log_8 3}{\\log_8 z}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$3\\log_8 z$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Product Rule for Logarithms"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"If $\\log 16 = x$ and $\\log 4=y$, then $2\\log 64=$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"First, let's recall the product rule for logarithms:\n\\[\n\\log_n a + \\log_n b= \\log_n(ab)\n\\]\nUsing the product rule for logarithms and the information given, we get\n\\begin{align}\n2\\log 64 &amp; = 2\\log(16\\cdot 4)\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=2\\bigl( \\log 16 + \\log 4 \\bigr) \\\\[5pt]\n&amp; = 2(x + y).\n\\end{align}"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$2(x+y)$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$2xy$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$4(x+y)$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$x^2 + y^2$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$x+y+2xy$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"H"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Product Rule for Logarithms"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"If $\\ln x =10$ and $\\ln y = 2$, then $\\ln (xy)=$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"First, let's recall the product rule for logarithms:\n\\[\n\\log_n a + \\log_n b= \\log_n(ab)\n\\]\nUsing the product rule, we have\n\\begin{align}\n\\ln (xy) &amp; = \\ln x + \\ln y\\\\[5pt]\n&amp; = 10 + 2\\\\[5pt]\n&amp; =12.\n\\end{align}"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$12$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$20$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$10x+2y$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$20xy$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$x^{10}y^2$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"M"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Solving Logarithmic Equations Containing the Natural Logarithm"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"If $\\ln \\left( {3-7x} \\right) = 6$, then $x=$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"To remove the logarithm, we exponentiate both sides of the equation. The logarithm is a natural logarithm, which means its base is $e.$ So, we use a base of $e$ for the exponentiation as well:\n\\begin{align*}\n\\ln \\left( {3-7x} \\right) &amp;= 6 \\\\[3pt]\ne^{\\color{blue}\\ln \\left( {3-7x} \\right)} &amp;=e^{\\color{blue}6}\n\\end{align*}\nSince exponentiation is the inverse function of the logarithm, we get $e^{\\ln(3-7x)} = 3-7x.$\nTherefore,\n\\begin{align*}\n3-7x &amp; = e^6 \\\\[3pt]\n- 7x &amp;= e^6 - 3 \\\\[3pt]\nx &amp;= \\dfrac{e^6 - 3}{ - 7} \\\\[3pt]\nx &amp;= \\dfrac{3 -e^6}{7}.\n\\end{align*}"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{3 -e^6}{7}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{3 +e^6}{7}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{3 -e^7}{6}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{6 -e^6}{7}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{6+e^7}{7}$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"M"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Solving Logarithmic Equations Containing the Natural Logarithm"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Find the exact solution to $\n\\ln \\left( {4x} \\right) = 2\n.$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"To remove the logarithm, we exponentiate both sides of the equation. The logarithm is a natural logarithm, which means its base is $e.$ So, we use a base of $e$ for the exponentiation as well:\n\\begin{align*}\n\\ln\\left( {4x} \\right) &amp;= 2 \\\\[3pt]\ne^{\\color{blue}\\ln\\left( {4x} \\right)} &amp;=e^{\\color{blue}2}\n\\end{align*}\nSince exponentiation is the inverse function of the logarithm, we get $e^{\\ln (4x)} = 4x.$\nTherefore,\n\\begin{align*}\n4x &amp;= e^{2} \\\\[5pt]\nx &amp;= \\dfrac{1}{4}e^{2}\\\\[5pt]\nx &amp;= \\dfrac{e^{2}}{4}.\n\\end{align*}"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$x = \\dfrac{e^2}{4}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$x =\\dfrac{2-e^{2}}{4}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$x = \\dfrac{{4 - e}}{2}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$x = \\dfrac{{ {e^4}}}{2}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$x =\\dfrac{1}{2}$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Solving Logarithmic Equations Containing the Natural Logarithm"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Find the exact solution to $5\\ln (7-2x) +13 = 38.$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"First, we rearrange our equation to isolate the logarithmic term, as follows:\n\\begin{align*}\n5\\ln (7-2x) +13 &amp;= 38 \\\\[3pt]\n5\\ln (7-2x) &amp;= 25 \\\\[3pt]\n\\ln (7-2x) &amp;= 5\n\\end{align*}\nTo remove the logarithm, we exponentiate both sides of the equation. The logarithm is a natural logarithm, which means its base is $e.$ So, we use a base of $e$ for the exponentiation as well:\n\\begin{align*}\n\\ln{(7-2x)} &amp;= 5 \\\\[3pt]\ne^{\\color{blue}\\ln{(7-2x)}} &amp;=e^{\\color{blue}5}\n\\end{align*}\nSince exponentiation is the inverse function of the logarithm, we get $e^{\\ln (7-2x)} = 7-2x.$\nTherefore,\n\\begin{align*}\n7-2x &amp;= e^5 \\\\[5pt]\n-2x &amp;= e^5-7 \\\\[3pt]\nx &amp;= \\dfrac{e^5-7}{-2} \\\\[3pt]\nx &amp;= \\dfrac{7-e^5}{2}.\n\\end{align*}"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{7-e^5}{2}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{7-e^2}{2}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{7+e^5}{5}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{7-e^2}{5}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{2+e^5}{7}$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"M"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Solving Logarithmic Equations Containing the Natural Logarithm"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Find the exact solution to $2\\ln (1-5x) +3 = 9.$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"First, we rearrange our equation to isolate the logarithmic term, as follows:\n\\begin{align*}\n2\\ln (1-5x) +3 &amp;= 9 \\\\[3pt]\n2\\ln (1-5x) &amp;= 6 \\\\[3pt]\n\\ln (1-5x) &amp;= 3\n\\end{align*}\nTo remove the logarithm, we exponentiate both sides of the equation. The logarithm is a natural logarithm, which means its base is $e.$ So, we use a base of $e$ for the exponentiation as well:\n\\begin{align*}\n\\ln{(1-5x)} &amp;= 3 \\\\[3pt]\ne^{\\color{blue}\\ln{(1-5x)}} &amp;=e^{\\color{blue}3}\n\\end{align*}\nSince exponentiation is the inverse function of the logarithm, we get $e^{\\ln (1-5x)} = 1-5x.$\nTherefore,\n\\begin{align*}\n1-5x &amp;= e^3 \\\\[5pt]\n-5x &amp;= e^3-1 \\\\[3pt]\nx &amp;= \\dfrac{e^3-1}{-5} \\\\[3pt]\nx &amp;= \\dfrac{1-e^3}{5}.\n\\end{align*}"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{1-e^3}{5}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{e^3+1}{5}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{1+e^3}{3}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{2-e^3}{3}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{3+e^3}{5}$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"M"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Solving Logarithmic Equations Containing the Natural Logarithm"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Find the values of $x$ such that $\\ln(x-1) - 3\\ln(x-1) = 2.$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"First, we simplify our equation by collecting the like logarithmic terms, and isolating the logarithm, as follows:\n\\begin{align*}\n\\ln(x-1) - 3\\ln(x-1) &amp;= 2 \\\\[5pt]\n(1-3)\\ln(x-1) &amp;=2 \\\\[5pt]\n-2\\ln(x-1) &amp;= 2 \\\\[5pt]\n\\ln(x-1) &amp;= -1\n\\end{align*}\nTo remove the logarithm, we exponentiate both sides of the equation. The logarithm is a natural logarithm, which means its base is $e.$ So, we use a base of $e$ for the exponentiation as well:\n\\begin{align*}\n\\ln(x-1) &amp;= -1 \\\\[5pt]\ne^{\\color{blue}\\ln(x-1)} &amp;= e^{\\color{blue}-1}\n\\end{align*}\nSince exponentiation is the inverse function of the logarithm, we get $e^{\\ln (x-1)} = x-1.$\nTherefore,\n\\begin{align*}\nx-1 &amp;= e^{-1} \\\\[5pt]\nx-1 &amp;= \\dfrac{1}{e} \\\\[5pt]\nx &amp; = \\dfrac{1}{e} + 1.\n\\end{align*}"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$ \\dfrac{1}{e} + 1$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$e + 1$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$ e^4 + 1$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$ \\dfrac{1}{e^4} + 1$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\sqrt{e}+1$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"H"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Solving Logarithmic Equations Containing the Natural Logarithm"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"If $2\\ln{x} + 3\\ln{x}=20$, then what is the value of $x?$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"First, we simplify our equation by collecting the like logarithmic terms, and isolating the logarithm, as follows:\n\\begin{align*}\n2\\ln{x} + 3\\ln{x} &amp;= 20 \\\\[5pt]\n(2+3)\\ln x &amp;= 20 \\\\[5pt]\n5\\ln x &amp;= 20 \\\\[5pt]\n\\ln x &amp;= \\dfrac{20}{5}\\\\[5pt]\n\\ln x &amp;=4.\n\\end{align*}\nTo remove the logarithm, we exponentiate both sides of the equation. The logarithm is a natural logarithm, which means its base is $e.$ So, we use a base of $e$ for the exponentiation as well:\n\\begin{align*}\n\\ln x &amp;= 4 \\\\[5pt]\ne^{\\color{blue}\\ln x} &amp;= e^{\\color{blue}4}\n\\end{align*}\nSince exponentiation is the inverse function of the logarithm, we get $e^{\\ln x} = x.$\nTherefore, $ x = e^{4}. $"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$e^4$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{e^{2}}{4}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\sqrt e$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\sqrt[3]e$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{e^{20}}{5}$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"M"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Differentiating Logarithmic Functions"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Given that $y=4\\pi\\ln x$, find $\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}y}{\\textrm{d}x}.$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The derivative of the natural logarithm is the reciprocal function:\n\\[\n\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{ \\textrm{d} x}\\left( \\ln x \\right)= \\dfrac{1}{x}\n\\]\nWe apply our formula and get\n\\begin{align}\n\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}y}{\\textrm{d}x}&amp;=\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}\\left(4\\pi\\ln x\\right)\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=4\\pi\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}\\left(\\ln x\\right)\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=4\\pi\\cdot\\dfrac{1}{x}\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=\\dfrac{4\\pi}{x}.\n\\end{align}"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{4\\pi}{x}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{4}{\\pi x}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{\\pi}{4x}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{4}{x+\\pi}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{x+\\pi}{4}$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Differentiating Logarithmic Functions"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Given that $y=7\\pi\\ln x$, find $\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}y}{\\textrm{d}x}.$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The derivative of the natural logarithm is the reciprocal function:\n\\[\n\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{ \\textrm{d} x}\\left( \\ln x \\right)= \\dfrac{1}{x}\n\\]\nWe apply our formula and get\n\\begin{align}\n\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}y}{\\textrm{d}x}&amp;=\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}\\left(7\\pi\\ln x\\right)\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=7\\pi\\cdot \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}\\left(\\ln x\\right)\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=7\\pi\\cdot\\dfrac{1}{x}\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=\\dfrac{7\\pi}{x}.\n\\end{align}"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{7\\pi}{x}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{7}{\\pi x}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{\\pi}{7x}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{7}{x+\\pi}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{x+\\pi}{7}$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Differentiating Logarithmic Functions"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Given that $y=\\ln x +e^x$, find the instantaneous rate of change at $x=1.$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"To find the instantaneous rate of change at a given point, we have to find $\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}y} {\\textrm{d}x}$ at that point.\nTaking the derivative, we have\n\\begin{align}\n\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}y}{\\textrm{d}x}&amp;=\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}\\left(\\ln x +e^x\\right)\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=\\dfrac{1}{x}+e^x \\,.\n\\end{align}\nTherefore, the instantaneous rate of change at $x=1$ is\n\\[\n\\eqalign{\n\\left.\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}y} {\\textrm{d}x}\\right|_{x=1}\n&amp;=\\left.\\left(\\dfrac{1}{x}+e^x \\right)\\,\\right|_{x=1}\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=\\dfrac{1}{1}+e^1\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=1+e \\,.\n}\n\\]"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$1+e$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$e$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$1$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{e}{1+e}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{1+e}{e}$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Differentiating Logarithmic Functions"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Find $f'(x)$ for $f(x) = 2\\ln x - 5e^x.$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Taking the derivative, we have\n\\begin{eqnarray}\n\\eqalign{\nf'(x) &amp; = \\dfrac {\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x} \\left(2\\ln x - 5e^x\\right) \\\\[5pt]\n&amp; = 2\\left(\\dfrac {1}{x}\\right) - 5e^x \\\\[5pt]\n&amp; = \\dfrac {2}{x} - 5e^x.\n}\n\\end{eqnarray}"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac {2}{x} - 5e^x $"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac {2}{x} - e^x$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac {1}{x} - 5e^x$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac {2x}{3}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$ 5e^x$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Differentiating Logarithmic Functions"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Given that $y=5\\log_7 x -5x+1$, find $\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}y}{\\textrm{d}x}.$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"To differentiate a logarithm with base $a,$ we use the formula\n\\[\n\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{ \\textrm{d} x}\\left( \\log_a x \\right)= \\dfrac{1}{x\\ln a}.\n\\]\nTherefore,\n\\[\n\\eqalign{\n\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}y}{\\textrm{d}x}&amp;=\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}\\left(5\\log_7 x -5x+1\\right)\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=5\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}\\left(\\log_7 x\\right)-5\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(x)-\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(1)\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=5\\cdot\\dfrac{1}{x \\ln 7}-5\\cdot 1+0\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=\\dfrac{5}{x \\ln 7}-5.\n}\n\\]"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{5}{x \\ln 7}-5$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{1}{x \\ln 7}-5$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{5}{x \\ln 7}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{5}{x \\ln 7}-5x+1$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{1}{x \\ln 7}-5x+1$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Differentiating Logarithmic Functions"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}} {\\textrm{d}x} \\left(2^{x} + \\log_2 x\\right) = $"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"To differentiate a logarithm with base $a,$ we use the formula\n\\[\n\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{ \\textrm{d} x}\\left( \\log_a x \\right)= \\dfrac{1}{x\\ln a}.\n\\]\nTherefore,\n\\begin{eqnarray}\n\\eqalign{\n\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}} {\\textrm{d}x} \\left(2^{x} + \\log_2 x\\right)\n&amp; = 2^x \\cdot \\ln 2 + \\dfrac {1}{x \\cdot \\ln 2} \\\\[5pt]\n&amp; = 2^x \\ln 2 + \\dfrac {1}{x \\cdot \\ln 2}.\n}\n\\end{eqnarray}"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$2^x\\ \\ln 2 + \\dfrac {1}{x\\ln 2} $"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$2^x + \\dfrac {1}{x\\ln 2} $"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$2^x + \\dfrac {1}{x} $"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$2^x\\ \\ln 2 + \\dfrac {1}{x} $"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$2^x\\ \\ln 2 + \\dfrac {1}{\\ln 2} $"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Differentiating Logarithmic Functions"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Given that $y=5\\log_4 x$, find $\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}y}{\\textrm{d}x}.$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"To differentiate a logarithm with base $a,$ we use the formula\n\\[\n\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{ \\textrm{d} x}\\left( \\log_a x \\right)= \\dfrac{1}{x\\ln a}.\n\\]\nTherefore,\n\\[\n\\eqalign{\n\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}y}{\\textrm{d}x}&amp;=\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}\\left(5\\log_4 x\\right)\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=5\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}\\left(\\log_4 x\\right)\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=5\\cdot\\dfrac{1}{x \\ln 4}\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=\\dfrac{5}{x \\ln 4}.\n}\n\\]"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{5}{x \\ln 4}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{5}{x \\ln 5}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{1}{x \\ln 4}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{5}{x}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{5}{4x}$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Differentiating Logarithmic Functions"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Given that $y= 3 x- 5 + 8\\log_2 x$, find the slope of the tangent at $x=\\dfrac{1}{\\ln 2}.$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"To find the slope of the tangent at a given point, we have to find $\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}y} {\\textrm{d}x}$ at that point.\nTaking the derivative, we have\n\\begin{align}\n\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}y}{\\textrm{d}x}\n&amp;= \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}\\left( 3 x- 5 + 8\\log_2 x \\right)\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(3 x)\n+ \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}\\left(-5 \\right)\n+ \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}\\left(8\\log_2 x\\right)\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= 3\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}( x)\n- \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}\\left(5\\right)\n+ 8\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}\\left(\\log_2 x\\right)\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= 3 - 0 + 8\\cdot \\dfrac{1}{x \\ln 2} \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= 3 + \\dfrac{8}{x \\ln 2} \\,.\n\\end{align}\nTherefore, the slope of the tangent at $x=\\dfrac{1}{\\ln 2}$ is\n\\begin{align}\n\\left.\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}y} {\\textrm{d}x}\\right|_{x=1/\\ln 2}\n&amp;=\\left. \\left(3 + \\dfrac{8}{x \\ln 2} \\right)\\right|_{x=1/\\ln 2}\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=3 + \\dfrac{8}{\\left( \\dfrac{1}{\\ln 2}\\cdot \\ln 2\\right) }\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=3+8\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=11 \\,.\n\\end{align}"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$11$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$6$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$3+8\\ln 2$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$8\\ln 2 - 2$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$4$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Differentiating Logarithmic Functions"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Given that $y= 6\\log_{2}x $, find the slope of the tangent at $x=3.$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"To find the slope of the tangent at a given point, we have to find $\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}y} {\\textrm{d}x}$ at that point.\nTaking the derivative, we have\n\\[\n\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}y}{\\textrm{d}x}=\\dfrac{6}{x\\ln 2} \\,.\n\\]\nTherefore, the slope of the tangent at $x=3$ is\n\\begin{align}\n\\left.\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}y} {\\textrm{d}x}\\right|_{x=3}\n&amp;=\\left.\\left(\\dfrac{6}{x\\ln 2} \\right)\\right|_{x=3}\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=\\dfrac{6}{3\\ln 2}\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=\\dfrac{2}{\\ln 2} \\,.\n\\end{align}"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{2}{\\ln 2}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{1}{\\ln 2}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{1}{2\\ln 2}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{1}{3\\ln 2}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{3}{2\\ln 2}$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
      }
    ],
    <span class="hljs-attr">"unknown"</span>: [
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Average of the Roots Formula"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Find the axis of symmetry for the parabola sketched above."</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"From the graph, we deduce that the roots are $x_1=-5$ and $x_2=1.$\nTo find the axis of symmetry, we take the average of the roots, as follows:\n\\begin{align*}\nx &amp;= \\dfrac{x_1+x_2}{2}\n= \\dfrac{(-5)+1}{2}\n= \\dfrac{-4}{2}\n=-2.\n\\end{align*}\nTherefore, the axis of symmetry is $x=-2.$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$x=-2$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$x=2$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$x=1$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$x=-5$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$x=-1$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Average of the Roots Formula"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Find the axis of symmetry for the parabola sketched above."</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"From the graph, we deduce that the roots are $x_1=-3$ and $x_2=1.$\nTo find the axis of symmetry, we take the average of the roots, as follows:\n\\begin{align*}\nx &amp;= \\dfrac{x_1+x_2}{2}\n= \\dfrac{(-3)+1}{2}\n= \\dfrac{-2}{2}.\n\\end{align*}\nTherefore, the axis of symmetry is $x= -1.$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$x=-1$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$x=1$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$x=-2$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$x=-1.5$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$x=0$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Average of the Roots Formula"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Find the axis of symmetry for the parabola $y=(x-2)(x+6).$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"From the equation $y=(x-2)(x+6),$ we find that the roots of the parabola are $x_1=2$ and $x_2=-6.$\nTo find the axis of symmetry, we take the average of the roots, as follows:\n\\begin{align*}\nx &amp;= \\dfrac{x_1+x_2}{2}\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= \\dfrac{2+(-6)}{2} \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= -2.\n\\end{align*}\nTherefore, the axis of symmetry is $x=-2.$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$x=-2$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$x=-4$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$x=-3$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$x=-\\dfrac32$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$x=-\\dfrac52$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Average of the Roots Formula"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Find the axis of symmetry of the parabola $y= (2x-1)\\left(4x+7\\right).$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"From the equation $y= (2x-1)\\left(4x+7\\right),$ we find that the roots of the parabola are $x_1=\\dfrac{1}{2}$ and $x_2=-\\dfrac{7}{4}.$\nTo find the axis of symmetry, we take the average of the roots, as follows:\n\\begin{align*}\nx\n&amp;= \\dfrac{x_1+x_2}{2}\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= \\dfrac{\\left(\\dfrac12 - \\dfrac74\\right)}{2}\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= \\dfrac{\\left(\\dfrac{2}{4} - \\dfrac74\\right)}{2}\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= \\dfrac{\\left( -\\dfrac54\\right)}{2}\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=-\\dfrac{5}{8}\n\\end{align*}\nTherefore, the axis of symmetry is $x=-\\dfrac{5}{8}.$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$x=-\\dfrac{5}{8}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$x=-\\dfrac{3}{8}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$x=-\\dfrac{7}{8}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$x=\\dfrac{3}{8}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$x=\\dfrac{5}{4}$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"M"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Average of the Roots Formula"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The axis of symmetry of $y=\\dfrac 34(x+4k)(x+3k)$ is $x=1.$ The value of $k=$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"First, we calculate the axis of symmetry in terms of $k.$ Then, since we know that the axis of symmetry is $x=1,$ we will set our result equal to $1.$\nFrom the equation $y=\\dfrac 34(x+4k)(x+3k),$ we find that the roots of the parabola are $x_1=-4k$ and $x_2=-3k.$\nTo find the axis of symmetry, we take the average of the roots, as follows:\n\\begin{align*}\nx\n&amp;= \\dfrac{x_1+x_2}{2}\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= \\dfrac{(-4k) + (-3k)}{2}\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= \\dfrac{-7k}{2}\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=-\\dfrac{7k}{2}.\n\\end{align*}\nWe know that the axis of symmetry is $x=1.$ Therefore, we have\n\\begin{align*}\n-\\dfrac{7k}{2} &amp;= 1 \\\\[5pt]\nk &amp;=-\\dfrac{2}{7}.\n\\end{align*}"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$-\\dfrac{2}{7}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$-\\dfrac{1}{7}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$-\\dfrac{3}{7}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{3}{7}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{5}{7}$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"M"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Equations of Circles Centered at the Origin"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"What is the center and radius of the circle with the equation $x^2 + y^2 = 256?$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The general equation of a circle with radius $r$ centered at the origin is\n\\[\nx^2 + y^2 = r^2.\n\\]\nSo, the equation $x^2 + y^2 = 256$ represents a circle that is centered at the origin, with radius\n\\begin{align*}\nr^2 &amp; = 256 \\\\[5pt]\nr &amp; = \\sqrt{256} \\\\[5pt]\nr &amp; = 16.\n\\end{align*}\nNote that we disregarded the negative solution for the radius since the radius of a circle must be positive.\nTherefore, the circle is centered at $(0,0)$ and its radius is $16.$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"The center is $(0,0)$ and the radius is $16$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"The center is $(0,0)$ and the radius is $4$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"The center is $(0,0)$ and the radius is $256$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"The center is $(0,0)$ and the radius is $512$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"The center is $(0,0)$ and the radius is $32$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"M"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Equations of Circles Centered at the Origin"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"What is the center and the radius of the circle with equation $x^2 + y^2 =64 ?$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The general equation of a circle with radius $r$ centered at the origin is\n\\[\nx^2 + y^2 = r^2.\n\\]\nSo, the equation $x^2 + y^2 = 64$ represents a circle that is centered at the origin, with radius\n\\begin{align*}\nr^2 &amp; = 64 \\\\[5pt]\nr &amp;= \\sqrt{64}\\\\[5pt]\nr &amp;= 8.\n\\end{align*}\nNote that we disregarded the negative solution for the radius since the radius of a circle must be positive.\nTherefore, the circle is centered at $(0,0)$ and its radius is $8.$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"The center is $(0,0)$ and the radius is $8$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"The center is $(0,0)$ and the radius is $\\sqrt{8}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"The center is $(0,0)$ and the radius is $64$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"The center is $(1,1)$ and the radius is $\\sqrt{8}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"The center is $(1,1)$ and the radius is $8$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Equations of Circles Centered at the Origin"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"What is the equation of the circle given above?"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The general equation of a circle with radius $r$ centered at the origin is\n\\[\nx^2 + y^2 = r^2.\n\\]\nFrom the picture, we see that our circle is centered at $(0,0)$ and has radius $r = 13.$ So, the equation is\n\\begin{align*}\nx^2 + y^2 &amp; = r^2 \\\\[5pt]\nx^2 + y^2 &amp; = 13^2 \\\\[5pt]\nx^2 + y^2 &amp;= 169.\n\\end{align*}"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$ x^2 + y^2 =169$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$ x^2 + y^2 =\\sqrt{13}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$ x^2 + y^2 = 13$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$ \\left(x-13\\right)^2 + \\left(y-13\\right)^2 = \\sqrt{13}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$ \\left(x-\\sqrt{13}\\right)^2 + \\left(y-\\sqrt{13}\\right)^2 = 13$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Equations of Circles Centered at the Origin"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"What is the equation of the circle given above?"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The general equation of a circle with radius $r$ centered at the origin is\n\\[\nx^2 + y^2 = r^2.\n\\]\nFrom the picture, we see that our circle is centered at $(0,0)$ and has radius $r = 12.$ So, the equation is\n\\begin{align*}\nx^2 + y^2 &amp; = r^2 \\\\[5pt]\nx^2 + y^2 &amp; = 12^2 \\\\[5pt]\nx^2 + y^2 &amp; = 144.\n\\end{align*}"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$x^2 + y^2 = 144$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$x^2 + y^2 = 12$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$x^2 + y^2 = \\sqrt{12}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$x^2 + y^2 = 24$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$(x-12)^2 + (x-12)^2 = 1$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"M"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Equations of Circles Centered at the Origin"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Which equation describes the set of all points $(x,y)$ in the coordinate plane that are a distance of $11$ units from the origin?"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The set of all points $(x,y)$ that are a distance of $11$ units from the origin defines a circle with radius $r = 11$ centered at the origin, as shown below.\nSo, the required equation is\n\\begin{align*}\nx^2 + y^2 &amp;= r^2 \\\\[5pt]\nx^2 + y^2 &amp;= 11^2 \\\\[5pt]\nx^2 + y^2 &amp;= 121.\n\\end{align*}"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$x^2 + y^2 = 121$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$x^2 + y^2 = 49$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$x^2 + y^2 = 11$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$x^2 + y^2 = \\sqrt{11}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$x^2 + y^2 +11 = 0$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Equations of Circles Centered at the Origin"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Which equation describes the set of all points $(x,y)$ in the coordinate plane that are a distance of $8$ units from the origin?"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The set of all points $(x,y)$ that are a distance of $8$ units from the origin defines a circle with radius $r = 8$ centered at the origin, as shown below.\nSo, the required equation is\n\\begin{align*}\nx^2 + y^2 &amp;=r^2 \\\\[5pt]\nx^2 + y^2 &amp;=8^2 \\\\[5pt]\nx^2 + y^2 &amp;=64.\n\\end{align*}"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$x^2 + y^2 =64$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$x^2 + y^2 =32$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$x^2 + y^2 =36$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$x^2 + y^2 =16$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$x^2 + y^2 =8$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Equations of Circles Centered at the Origin"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Given that the points $X$ and $Y$ with coordinates $(5,1)$ and $(-5,-1),$ respectively, are the endpoints of a diameter of a circle centered at the origin, what is the equation of the circle?"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The equation of a circle with radius $r,$ centered at the origin, is\n\\[\nx^2 + y^2 = r^2.\n\\]\nFirst, we calculate the length of the diameter $\\overline{XY}\\mathbin{:}$\n\\begin{align*}\nXY &amp;= \\sqrt{{(5-(-5))}^2+{(1-(-1))}^2}\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=\\sqrt{{10}^2+2^2}\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=\\sqrt{100+4}\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=2\\sqrt{26}\n\\end{align*}\nSince $\\overline{XY}$ is a diameter, the radius is\n\\[\nr = \\dfrac{XY}{2} =\\dfrac {2\\sqrt{26}}{2}=\\sqrt{26}.\n\\]\nSo, the equation of the circle is\n\\begin{align*}\nx^2 + y^2 &amp; = r^2 \\\\[5pt]\nx^2 + y^2 &amp; = {\\left(\\sqrt{26}\\right)}^2 \\\\[5pt]\nx^2 + y^2 &amp; = 26.\n\\end{align*}"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$x^2 + y^2 = 26$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$x^2 + y^2 = 104$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$x^2 + y^2 = \\sqrt{26}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$(x-2)^2 + (y-2)^2 =2\\sqrt {26}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$(x-5)^2 + (y-1)^2 = 26$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"H"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Equations of Circles Centered at the Origin"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Given that the points $X$ and $Y$ with coordinates $(-4,1)$ and $(4,-1),$ respectively, are the endpoints of a diameter of a circle centered at the origin, what is the equation of the circle?"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The equation of a circle with radius $r,$ centered at the origin, is\n\\[\nx^2 + y^2 = r^2.\n\\]\nFirst, we calculate the length of the diameter $XY\\mathbin{:}$\n\\begin{align*}\nXY &amp;= \\sqrt{ (-4-4)^2+ (1-(-1))^2}\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= \\sqrt{ (-8)^2+2^2}\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= \\sqrt{64+4}\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= 2\\sqrt{17}\n\\end{align*}\nSince $\\overline{XY}$ is a diameter, the radius is\n\\[\nr=\\dfrac{XY}{2} = \\dfrac {2\\sqrt{17}}{2}=\\sqrt{17}.\n\\]\nSo, the equation of the circle is\n\\begin{align*}\nx^2 + y^2 &amp; = r^2 \\\\[5pt]\nx^2 + y^2 &amp; =\\left(\\sqrt{17}\\right)^2 \\\\[5pt]\nx^2 + y^2 &amp; = 17.\n\\end{align*}"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$x^2 + y^2 = 17$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$x^2 + y^2 = \\sqrt{17}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$x^2 + y^2 = \\sqrt{34}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$x^2 + y^2 = 34$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$x^2 + y^2 = 21$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"M"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Scalar Multiplication of Matrices"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Calculate $\\dfrac{1}{3}Q$, if\n$\n{Q} =\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n3 &amp; 6 \\\\\n0 &amp; -9 \\\\\n-3 &amp; 0\n}\n\\right\\rbrack.\n$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"We multiply each entry of $Q$ by $\\dfrac{1}{3},$ as follows:\n\\begin{align*}\n\\dfrac{1}{3} {Q}\n&amp;=\n\\dfrac{1}{3}\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n3 &amp; 6 \\\\\n0 &amp; -9 \\\\\n-3 &amp; 0\n}\n\\right\\rbrack \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n\\dfrac{1}{3} \\cdot 3 &amp; \\dfrac{1}{3} \\cdot 6 \\\\\n\\dfrac{1}{3} \\cdot 0 &amp; \\dfrac{1}{3} \\cdot (-9) \\\\\n\\dfrac{1}{3} \\cdot (-3) &amp; \\dfrac{1}{3} \\cdot 0\n}\n\\right\\rbrack \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n1 &amp; 2 \\\\\n0 &amp; -3 \\\\\n-1 &amp; 0\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n\\end{align*}"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n1 &amp; 2 \\\\\n0 &amp; -3 \\\\\n-1 &amp; 0\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n3 &amp; 6 \\\\\n0 &amp; -9 \\\\\n-3 &amp; 0\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n9 &amp; 18 \\\\\n0 &amp; -27 \\\\\n-9 &amp; 0\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n1 &amp; 2 \\\\\n0 &amp; 3 \\\\\n-1 &amp; 1\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n1 &amp; 2 \\\\\n0 &amp; -3 \\\\\n-1 &amp; \\dfrac{1}{3}\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Scalar Multiplication of Matrices"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"$5\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n4 &amp; 1 &amp; -2\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n=$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"We multiply each entry of the matrix by $5$, as follows:\n\\begin{eqnarray}\n\\eqalign{\n5\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n4 &amp; 1 &amp; -2\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n&amp;=\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n5 \\cdot 4 &amp; 5 \\cdot 1 &amp; 5 \\cdot (-2)\n}\n\\right\\rbrack \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n20 &amp; 5 &amp; -10\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n}\n\\end{eqnarray}"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n20 &amp; 5 &amp; -10\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n4 &amp; 1 &amp; -2\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n-20 &amp; -5 &amp; 10\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n20 \\\\ 5 \\\\ -10\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n4 \\\\ 1 \\\\ -2\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Scalar Multiplication of Matrices"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"If $k {M} =\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n0 &amp; 6 &amp; -3 \\\\\n6 &amp; -9 &amp; 6 \\\\\n15 &amp; 9 &amp; 0\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n$,\nwhere ${M} =\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n0 &amp; 2 &amp; -1 \\\\\n2 &amp; -3 &amp; 2 \\\\\n5 &amp; 3 &amp; 0\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n$\nand $k$ is a scalar, calculate the value of $k.$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"To express $kM,$ we multiply each entry of $M$ by $k,$ as follows:\n\\begin{align*}\nk {M} &amp;=\nk\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n0 &amp; 2 &amp; -1 \\\\\n2 &amp; -3 &amp; 2 \\\\\n5 &amp; 3 &amp; 0\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n0 &amp; 2k &amp; -k \\\\\n2k &amp; -3k &amp; 2k \\\\\n5k &amp; 3k &amp; 0\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n\\end{align*}\nNow, we set the above result equal to the matrix given for $kM$ in the problem statement:\n\\[\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n0 &amp; 2k &amp; -k \\\\\n2k &amp; -3k &amp; 2k \\\\\n5k &amp; 3k &amp; 0\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n=\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n0 &amp; 6 &amp; -3 \\\\\n6 &amp; -9 &amp; 6 \\\\\n15 &amp; 9 &amp; 0\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n\\]\nWe can solve for $k$ by equating any two corresponding (nonzero) elements. Let's choose the top-right elements, $-k$ and $-3.$ Equating these elements, we get\n\\begin{align}\n-k &amp;= -3 \\\\[3pt]\nk &amp;= 3.\n\\end{align}\nNote: We would still get the same result if we chose any other two corresponding (nonzero) elements."</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$k=3$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$k=-3$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$k=2$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$k=-2$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$k=0$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Scalar Multiplication of Matrices"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"If $k {L} =\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n-4 &amp; 0 &amp; -10 \\\\\n-6 &amp; -6 &amp; -14 \\\\\n0 &amp; -10 &amp; -2\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n$,\nwhere ${L} =\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n2 &amp; 0 &amp; 5 \\\\\n3 &amp; 3 &amp; 7 \\\\\n0 &amp; 5 &amp; 1\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n$\nand $k$ is a scalar, calculate the value of $k.$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"To express $kL,$ we multiply each entry of $L$ by $k,$ as follows:\n\\begin{align*}\nk {L} &amp;=\nk\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n2 &amp; 0 &amp; 5 \\\\\n3 &amp; 3 &amp; 7 \\\\\n0 &amp; 5 &amp; 1\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n2k &amp; 0 &amp; 5k \\\\\n3k &amp; 3k &amp; 7k \\\\\n0 &amp; 5k &amp; k\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n\\end{align*}\nNow, we set the above result equal to the matrix given for $kL$ in the problem statement:\n\\[\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n2k &amp; 0 &amp; 5k \\\\\n3k &amp; 3k &amp; 7k \\\\\n0 &amp; 5k &amp; k\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n=\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n-4 &amp; 0 &amp; -10 \\\\\n-6 &amp; -6 &amp; -14 \\\\\n0 &amp; -10 &amp; -2\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n\\]\nWe can solve for $k$ by equating any two corresponding (nonzero) elements. Let's choose the bottom-right elements, $k$ and $-2.$ Equating these elements, we get\n\\begin{align}\nk &amp;= -2.\n\\end{align}\nNote: We would still get the same result if we chose any other two corresponding (nonzero) elements."</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$k=-2$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$k=-1$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$k=-3$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$k=0$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$k=-4$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Scalar Multiplication of Matrices"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"If $k {R} =\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n2 &amp; -3 \\\\\n0 &amp; 1\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n$,\nwhere $ {R} =\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n-2 &amp; 3 \\\\\n0 &amp; -1\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n$\nand $k$ is a scalar, calculate the value of $k.$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"To express $kR,$ we multiply each entry of $R$ by $k,$ as follows:\n\\begin{align*}\nk {R} &amp;=\nk\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n-2 &amp; 3 \\\\\n0 &amp; -1\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n-2k &amp; 3k \\\\\n0 &amp; -k\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n\\end{align*}\nNow, we set the above result equal to the matrix given for $kR$ in the problem statement:\n\\[\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n-2k &amp; 3k \\\\\n0 &amp; -k\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n=\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n2 &amp; -3 \\\\\n0 &amp; 1\n}\n\\right\\rbrack.\n\\]\nWe can solve for $k$ by equating any two corresponding (nonzero) elements. Let's choose the top-right elements, $3k$ and $-3.$ Equating these elements, we get\n\\begin{align}\n3k &amp;= -3 \\\\[3pt]\nk &amp;= -1.\n\\end{align}\nNote: We would still get the same result if we chose any other two corresponding (nonzero) elements."</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$k=-1$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$k=1$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$k=-2$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$k=2$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$k=0$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Scalar Multiplication of Matrices"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Calculate $-{W} - 2{A}$, where\n${W}=\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n-3 \\\\ 1 \\\\ 1\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n$\nand\n$\n{A}=\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n5 \\\\ -2 \\\\ 2\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n.$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"We multiply each entry of $W$ by $-1$ and each entry of $A$ by $2,$ and then subtract the resulting matrices, as follows:\n\\[\n\\begin{align}\n-{W} - 2{A}\n&amp;=\n(-1)\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n-3 \\\\ 1 \\\\ 1\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n-\n2\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n5 \\\\ -2 \\\\ 2\n}\n\\right\\rbrack \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n(-1) \\cdot (-3) \\\\ (-1) \\cdot 1 \\\\ (-1) \\cdot 1\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n-\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n2 \\cdot 5 \\\\ 2 \\cdot (-2) \\\\ 2 \\cdot 2\n}\n\\right\\rbrack \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n3 \\\\ -1 \\\\ -1\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n-\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n10 \\\\ -4 \\\\ 4\n}\n\\right\\rbrack \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n3-10 \\\\ -1-(-4) \\\\ -1-4\n}\n\\right\\rbrack \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n-7 \\\\ 3 \\\\ -5\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n\\end{align}\n\\]"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n-7 \\\\ 3 \\\\ -5\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n2 \\\\ 3 \\\\ -1\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n-7 \\\\ 0 \\\\ -1\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n-2 \\\\ 1 \\\\ -5\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n-7 \\\\ -1 \\\\ 2\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"H"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Scalar Multiplication of Matrices"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Calculate $\\dfrac{1}{3}{S} - 2{T}$, where\n${S}=\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n-3 &amp; -3 \\\\\n-3 &amp; 6 \\\\\n0 &amp; 0 \\\\\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n$\nand\n$\n{T}=\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n1 &amp; -2 \\\\\n1 &amp; 0 \\\\\n1 &amp; 1 \\\\\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n.$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"We multiply each entry of $S$ by $\\dfrac{1}{3}$ and each entry of $T$ by $2,$ and then subtract the resulting matrices, as follows:\n\\[\n\\begin{align}\n\\dfrac{1}{3}{S} - 2{T}\n&amp;=\n\\dfrac{1}{3}\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n-3 &amp; -3 \\\\\n-3 &amp; 6 \\\\\n0 &amp; 0 \\\\\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n-\n2\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n1 &amp; -2 \\\\\n1 &amp; 0 \\\\\n1 &amp; 1 \\\\\n}\n\\right\\rbrack \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n\\dfrac{1}{3} \\cdot (-3) &amp; \\dfrac{1}{3} \\cdot (-3) \\\\\n\\dfrac{1}{3} \\cdot (-3) &amp; \\dfrac{1}{3} \\cdot 6 \\\\\n\\dfrac{1}{3} \\cdot 0 &amp; \\dfrac{1}{3} \\cdot 0 \\\\\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n-\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n2 \\cdot 1 &amp; 2 \\cdot (-2) \\\\\n2 \\cdot 1 &amp; 2 \\cdot 0 \\\\\n2 \\cdot 1 &amp; 2 \\cdot 1 \\\\\n}\n\\right\\rbrack \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n-1 &amp; -1 \\\\\n-1 &amp; 2 \\\\\n0 &amp; 0\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n-\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n2 &amp; -4 \\\\\n2 &amp; 0 \\\\\n2 &amp; 2 \\\\\n}\n\\right\\rbrack \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n-1-2 &amp; -1-(-4) \\\\\n-1-2 &amp; 2-0 \\\\\n0-2 &amp; 0-2\n}\n\\right\\rbrack \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n-3 &amp; 3 \\\\\n-3 &amp; 2 \\\\\n-2 &amp; -2\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n\\end{align}\n\\]"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n-3 &amp; 3 \\\\\n-3 &amp; 2 \\\\\n-2 &amp; -2\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n-2 &amp; 3 \\\\\n-3 &amp; 2 \\\\\n-1 &amp; -2\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n1 &amp; -5 \\\\\n1 &amp; 2 \\\\\n2 &amp; 2\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n1 &amp; -3 \\\\\n-1 &amp; 2 \\\\\n2 &amp; 2\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n-3 &amp; 3 \\\\\n2 &amp; 2 \\\\\n-2 &amp; -1\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"H"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Elementary Row Operations"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Consider the matrix $C,$ given by\n\\[\nC =\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n1&amp; 2&amp; 1 \\\\\n-9&amp; 0&amp; -4\\\\\n2&amp; 0 &amp; -1\n\\end{bmatrix}.\n\\]\nWhat matrix results from carrying out the following row operations on $C?$\n\\[\n\\begin{align*}\nR_1 &amp;:= R_1 + 3R_3\\\\\nR_2 &amp;:= R_2 + 5R_3\n\\end{align*}\n\\]"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Let's list the required operations:\nThe operation $R_1 :=R_1 + 3R_3$ means that we need to add $3$ times the third row to the first row.\nThe operation $R_2 :=R_2 + 5R_3$ means that we need to add $5$ times the third row to the second row.\nApplying the operations, we obtain the following:\n\\[\n\\begin{align*}\nC&amp;=\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n1 &amp; 2 &amp; 1 \\\\\n-9 &amp; 0 &amp; -4 \\\\\n{\\color{blue}2}&amp; {\\color{blue}0}&amp; {\\color{blue}-1}\n\\end{bmatrix} \\qquad R_1 := R_1 + {\\color{red}3} \\cdot {\\color{blue}R_3}\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;\\sim\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n1 + ({\\color{red}3})({\\color{blue}2}) &amp; 2 + ({\\color{red}3})({\\color{blue}0}) &amp; 1 + ({\\color{red}3})({\\color{blue}-1}) \\\\\n-9 &amp; 0 &amp; -4 \\\\\n{\\color{blue}2} &amp; {\\color{blue}0}&amp; {\\color{blue}-1}\n\\end{bmatrix}\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;\\sim\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n7 &amp; 2 &amp; -2 \\\\\n-9 &amp; 0 &amp; -4 \\\\\n{\\color{blue}2}&amp; {\\color{blue}0}&amp; {\\color{blue}-1}\n\\end{bmatrix} \\qquad R_2 := R_2 + {\\color{red}5} \\cdot {\\color{blue}R_3}\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;\\sim\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n7&amp; 2 &amp; -2 \\\\\n-9 + ({\\color{red}5})({\\color{blue}2}) &amp; 0 + ({\\color{red}5})({\\color{blue}0}) &amp; -4 + ({\\color{red}5})({\\color{blue}-1}) \\\\\n{\\color{blue}2} &amp; {\\color{blue}0} &amp; {\\color{blue}-1} \\\\\n\\end{bmatrix}\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;\\sim\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n7 &amp; 2 &amp; -2 \\\\\n{\\color{red}1} &amp; {\\color{red}0} &amp; {\\color{red}-9} \\\\\n{\\color{blue}2}&amp; {\\color{blue}0}&amp; {\\color{blue}-1}\\\\\n\\end{bmatrix}\n\\end{align*}\n\\]"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\begin{bmatrix}\n7 &amp; 2 &amp; -2 \\\\\n1 &amp; 0 &amp; -9 \\\\\n2&amp; 0&amp; -1\\\\\n\\end{bmatrix}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\begin{bmatrix}\n1 &amp; 2 &amp; 1 \\\\\n7 &amp; 2 &amp; -2 \\\\\n2&amp; 0&amp; -1\\\\\n\\end{bmatrix}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\begin{bmatrix}\n7 &amp; 2 &amp; -2 \\\\\n-9 &amp; 0 &amp; -4 \\\\\n1&amp; 0&amp; -9\\\\\n\\end{bmatrix}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\begin{bmatrix}\n1 &amp; 2 &amp; 1 \\\\\n7 &amp; 2 &amp; -2 \\\\\n1&amp; 0&amp; -9\\\\\n\\end{bmatrix}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\begin{bmatrix}\n5 &amp; 2 &amp; -4 \\\\\n-19 &amp; 0 &amp; -1 \\\\\n2&amp; 0&amp; -1\\\\\n\\end{bmatrix}$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"H"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Elementary Row Operations"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Consider the matrix $M,$ given by\n\\[\nM =\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n0 &amp; -1 &amp; 2 \\\\\n-3 &amp; 2 &amp; -1 \\\\\n6 &amp; 0 &amp; 4\n\\end{bmatrix}.\n\\]\nWhat matrix results from carrying out the row operation $R_1:=R_1 + 4 R_2$ on $M?$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The operation $R_1:=R_1 + 4 R_2$ means that we need to add $4$ times the second row to the first row.\nApplying this row operation, we obtain the following:\n\\begin{align*}\nM &amp;=\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n0 &amp; -1 &amp; 2 \\\\\n{\\color{blue}-3} &amp; {\\color{blue}2} &amp; {\\color{blue}-1} \\\\\n6 &amp; 0 &amp; 4\n\\end{bmatrix} \\qquad R_1:=R_1 + {\\color{red}4} \\cdot {\\color{blue}R_2} \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;\\sim\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n0 + ({\\color{red}4}) ({\\color{blue}-3})&amp; -1 + ({\\color{red}4}) ({\\color{blue}2}) &amp; 2 +({\\color{red}4}) ({\\color{blue}-1}) \\\\\n{\\color{blue}-3}&amp; {\\color{blue}2}&amp; {\\color{blue}-1} \\\\\n6 &amp; 0 &amp; 4\n\\end{bmatrix} \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;\\sim\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n\\color{red}-12 &amp; \\color{red}7 &amp; \\color{red}-2 \\\\\n{\\color{blue}-3}&amp; {\\color{blue}2}&amp; {\\color{blue}-1} \\\\\n6 &amp; 0 &amp; 4\n\\end{bmatrix}\n\\end{align*}"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\begin{bmatrix}\n-12 &amp; 7 &amp; -2 \\\\\n-3&amp; 2 &amp; -1 \\\\\n6 &amp; 0 &amp; 4\n\\end{bmatrix}\n$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\begin{bmatrix}\n0 &amp; -1 &amp; 2 \\\\\n-3&amp; -2 &amp; 7 \\\\\n6 &amp; 0 &amp; 4\n\\end{bmatrix}\n$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\begin{bmatrix}\n24 &amp; -1 &amp; 18 \\\\\n-3&amp; 2 &amp; -1 \\\\\n6 &amp; 0 &amp; 4\n\\end{bmatrix}\n$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\begin{bmatrix}\n-3 &amp; 1 &amp; 1 \\\\\n-3&amp; 2 &amp; -1 \\\\\n6 &amp; 0 &amp; 4\n\\end{bmatrix}\n$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\begin{bmatrix}\n-12 &amp; 9 &amp; -6 \\\\\n-3&amp; 2 &amp; -1 \\\\\n6 &amp; 0 &amp; 4\n\\end{bmatrix}\n$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"M"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Elementary Row Operations"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Consider the augmented matrix $M,$ given by\n\\[\nM =\n\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n1&amp; 0&amp; 2 &amp; 3 \\\\\n0&amp; -2&amp; 1 &amp; 4 \\\\\n1&amp; -1&amp; 1 &amp; 2\n\\end{array}\n\\right].\n\\]\nWhat matrix results from carrying out the row operation $R_3 := R_3 + (-1) \\cdot R_1$ on $M?$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The operation $R_3 := R_3 + (-1) \\cdot R_1$ means that we need to add $-1$ times the first row to the third row.\nApplying this row operation, we obtain the following:\n\\begin{align*}\nM &amp;=\n\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n{\\color{blue}1}&amp; {\\color{blue}0}&amp; {\\color{blue}2} &amp; {\\color{blue}3} \\\\\n0&amp; -2&amp; 1 &amp; 4 \\\\\n1&amp; -1&amp; 1 &amp; 2\n\\end{array}\n\\right] \\qquad R_3 := R_3 + ({\\color{red}-1}) \\cdot {\\color{blue}R_1} \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;\\sim\n\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n{\\color{blue}1}&amp; {\\color{blue}0}&amp; {\\color{blue}2} &amp; {\\color{blue}3} \\\\\n0&amp; -2&amp; 1 &amp; 4 \\\\\n1 + ({\\color{red}-1})({\\color{blue}1})&amp; -1 + ({\\color{red}-1})({\\color{blue}0})&amp; 1 + ({\\color{red}-1})({\\color{blue}2}) &amp; 2 + ({\\color{red}-1})({\\color{blue}3})\n\\end{array}\n\\right] \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;\\sim\n\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n{\\color{blue}1}&amp; {\\color{blue}0}&amp; {\\color{blue}2} &amp; {\\color{blue}3} \\\\\n0&amp; -2&amp; 1 &amp; 4 \\\\\n\\color{red}0&amp; \\color{red}-1&amp; \\color{red}-1 &amp; \\color{red}-1\n\\end{array}\n\\right]\n\\end{align*}"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n1&amp; 0&amp; 2 &amp; 3 \\\\\n0&amp; -2&amp; 1 &amp; 4 \\\\\n0&amp; -1&amp; -1 &amp; -1\n\\end{array}\n\\right] $"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n1&amp; 0&amp; 2 &amp; 3 \\\\\n0&amp; -2&amp; 1 &amp; 4 \\\\\n1&amp; -1&amp; 1 &amp; 2\n\\end{array}\n\\right] $"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n0&amp; -2&amp; 1 &amp; 4 \\\\\n1&amp; 0&amp; 2 &amp; 3 \\\\\n1&amp; 1&amp; 0 &amp; 2\n\\end{array}\n\\right]$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n0&amp; -2&amp; 1 &amp; 4 \\\\\n1&amp; 0&amp; 2 &amp; 3 \\\\\n1&amp; 1&amp; 0 &amp; -2\n\\end{array}\n\\right]$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n1&amp; 3&amp; 9 &amp; 1 \\\\\n0&amp; -3&amp; 3 &amp; 0 \\\\\n0&amp; 1&amp; 1 &amp; 1\n\\end{array}\n\\right] $"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"M"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Elementary Row Operations"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Consider the augmented matrix $T,$ given by\n\\[\nT =\n\\left[\n\\begin{array}{cc|c}\n1 &amp; 3&amp; 2\\\\\n-2 &amp; -2&amp; 1\n\\end{array}\n\\right].\n\\]\nWhat matrix results from carrying out the row operation $R_2 := R_2+2R_1$ on $T?$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The operation $R_2 :=R_2 + 2 R_1$ means that we need to add $2$ times the first row to the second row.\nApplying this row operation, we obtain the following:\n\\begin{align*}\nT &amp; =\n\\left[\n\\begin{array}{cc|c}\n\\color{blue}1 &amp; \\color{blue}3&amp; \\color{blue}2\\\\\n-2 &amp; -2&amp; 1\\\\\n\\end{array}\n\\right] \\qquad R_2 := R_2 + {\\color{red}2} \\cdot {\\color{blue}R_1} \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;\\sim\n\\left[\n\\begin{array}{cc|c}\n\\color{blue}1 &amp; \\color{blue}3 &amp; \\color{blue}2\\\\\n-2 + ({\\color{red}2})({\\color{blue}1}) &amp; -2 + ({\\color{red}2})({\\color{blue}3})&amp; 1 + ({\\color{red}2})({\\color{blue}2})\\\\\n\\end{array}\n\\right] \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;\\sim\n\\left[\n\\begin{array}{cc|c}\n\\color{blue}1 &amp; \\color{blue} 3&amp; \\color{blue}2\\\\\n\\color{red}0 &amp; \\color{red} 4&amp; \\color{red} 5\\\\\n\\end{array}\n\\right]\n\\end{align*}"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\left[\n\\begin{array}{cc|c}\n1 &amp; 3&amp; 2\\\\\n0 &amp; 4 &amp;5\\\\\n\\end{array}\n\\right]$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\left[\n\\begin{array}{cc|c}\n1 &amp; 3&amp; 2\\\\\n0 &amp; 4 &amp;-5\\\\\n\\end{array}\n\\right]$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\left[\n\\begin{array}{cc|c}\n1 &amp; 3&amp; 2\\\\\n0 &amp; -4 &amp;5\\\\\n\\end{array}\n\\right]$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\left[\n\\begin{array}{cc|c}\n1 &amp; 3&amp; 2\\\\\n0 &amp; 5 &amp;4\\\\\n\\end{array}\n\\right]$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\left[\n\\begin{array}{cc|c}\n1 &amp; 3&amp; 2\\\\\n0 &amp; -5&amp;-4\\\\\n\\end{array}\n\\right]$"</span>
