        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"First, we solve for $x$ as follows:\n\\begin{align*}\nx^2+q+3 &amp;= 7 \\\\[3pt]\nx^2 &amp;= 7-q-3 \\\\[3pt]\nx^2 &amp;= 4-q \\\\[3pt]\n\\sqrt{x^2} &amp;= \\sqrt{4-q} \\\\[3pt]\n\\vert x\\vert &amp;= \\sqrt{4-q}\n\\end{align*}\nThe equation has only one solution if the right-hand side is equal to zero, and the right-hand side is zero only if the expression underneath the radical is zero. So, we solve for the value of $q$ that makes the expression under the radical equal to zero:\n\\begin{align*}\n4-q &amp;= 0 \\\\[3pt]\nq &amp;= 4\n\\end{align*}\nTherefore, $q=4$ will give only one solution to the equation."</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$4$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$-3$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$7$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$-4$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$10$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
      }
    ],
    <span class="hljs-attr">"linear-systems"</span>: [
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Creating Rows or Columns Containing Zeros Using Gaussian Elimination"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"\\[A =\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1} &amp; \\color{blue}5 &amp; \\color{blue}7 \\\\\n-3 &amp; 1 &amp; -5 \\\\\n2 &amp; 9 &amp; 7\n\\end{bmatrix},\n\\qquad\nB=\\begin{bmatrix}\n\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1} &amp; \\color{blue}5 &amp; \\color{blue}7 \\\\\n\\color{red}0 &amp; 16 &amp; 16 \\\\\n\\color{red}0 &amp; -1 &amp; -7\n\\end{bmatrix}\n\\]\nBy applying elementary row operations of the form $R_i:=R_i+kR_1,$ the matrix $A$ can be transformed into the matrix $B.$ What are the elementary row operations?"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"To transform $A$ into $B,$ we need to make the entries below $a_{11}={\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1}}$ equal to ${\\color{red}0}.$\nTo do this, we can apply the following elementary row operations:\n\\begin{align*}\nA\n&amp;=\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1} &amp; 5 &amp; 7 \\\\\n-3 &amp; 1 &amp; -5 \\\\\n2 &amp; 9 &amp; 7\n\\end{bmatrix}\n&amp;&amp; \\qquad\n\\begin{array}{l}\nR_2:=R_2+3R_1\n\\end{array} \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;\\sim\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1} &amp; 5 &amp; 7 \\\\\n\\color{red}0 &amp; 16 &amp; 16 \\\\\n2 &amp; 9 &amp; 7\n\\end{bmatrix}\n&amp;&amp; \\qquad\n\\begin{array}{l}\nR_3:=R_3-2R_1\n\\end{array} \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;\\sim\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1} &amp; 5 &amp; 7 \\\\\n\\color{red}0 &amp; 16 &amp; 16 \\\\\n\\color{red}0 &amp; -1 &amp; -7\n\\end{bmatrix} \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= B\n\\end{align*}\nTherefore, the elementary row operations are\n$\\qquad$ $R_2:=R_2+3R_1\\quad$ and $\\quad R_3:=R_3-2R_1.$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$R_2:=R_2+3R_1\\quad$ and $\\quad R_3:=R_3-2R_1$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$R_2:=R_2-3R_1\\quad$ and $\\quad R_3:=R_3-2R_1$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$R_2:=R_2+3R_1\\quad$ and $\\quad R_3:=R_3+2R_1$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$R_2:=R_2-3R_1\\quad$ and $\\quad R_3:=R_3+2R_1$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$R_2:=R_2+2R_1\\quad$ and $\\quad R_3:=R_3-3R_1$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Creating Rows or Columns Containing Zeros Using Gaussian Elimination"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"\\[A =\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n7 &amp; 4 &amp; 2\\\\\n-9 &amp; -6 &amp; -12 \\\\\n\\color{blue}6 &amp; \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{2} &amp; \\color{blue}5 \\\\\n\\end{bmatrix},\n\\qquad\nB=\\begin{bmatrix}\n-5 &amp; \\color{red}0 &amp; -8\\\\\n9 &amp; \\color{red}0 &amp; 3\\\\\n\\color{blue}6 &amp; \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{2} &amp; \\color{blue}5 \\\\\n\\end{bmatrix}\n\\]\nBy applying elementary row operations of the form $R_i:=R_i+kR_3,$ the matrix $A$ can be transformed into the matrix $B.$ What are the elementary row operations?"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"To transform $A$ into $B,$ we need to make the entries above $a_{32}={\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{2}}$ equal to ${\\color{red}0}.$\nTo do this, we can apply the following elementary row operations:\n\\begin{align*}\nA\n&amp;=\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n7 &amp; 4 &amp; 2\\\\\n-9 &amp; -6 &amp; -12 \\\\\n6 &amp; \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{2} &amp; 5 \\\\\n\\end{bmatrix}\n&amp;&amp; \\qquad\n\\begin{array}{l}\nR_1:=R_1-2R_3\n\\end{array} \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;\\sim\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n-5 &amp; {\\color{red}0} &amp; -8\\\\\n-9 &amp; -6 &amp; -12 \\\\\n6 &amp; \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{2} &amp; 5 \\\\\n\\end{bmatrix}\n&amp;&amp; \\qquad\n\\begin{array}{l}\nR_2:=R_2+3R_3\n\\end{array} \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;\\sim\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n-5 &amp; {\\color{red}0} &amp; -8\\\\\n9 &amp; {\\color{red}0} &amp; 3\\\\\n6 &amp; \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{2} &amp; 5 \\\\\n\\end{bmatrix} \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= B\n\\end{align*}\nTherefore, the elementary row operations are\n$\\qquad$ $R_1:=R_1-2R_3\\quad$ and $\\quad R_2:=R_2+3R_3.$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$R_1:=R_1-2R_3\\quad$ and $\\quad R_2:=R_2+3R_3$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$R_1:=R_1+2R_3\\quad$ and $\\quad R_2:=R_2-3R_3$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$R_1:=R_1+2R_3\\quad$ and $\\quad R_2:=R_2+3R_3$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$R_1:=R_1-2R_3\\quad$ and $\\quad R_2:=R_2-3R_3$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$R_1:=R_1-3R_3\\quad$ and $\\quad R_2:=R_2+2R_3$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Creating Rows or Columns Containing Zeros Using Gaussian Elimination"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"\\[A =\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n-1 &amp; 4 &amp; 3&amp; 7\\\\\n-9 &amp; 2 &amp; 2 &amp; -1\\\\\n\\color{blue}-5 &amp; \\color{blue}6 &amp; \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1} &amp; \\color{blue}7\\\\\n4 &amp; -2 &amp; 2 &amp; 6\n\\end{bmatrix},\n\\qquad\nB=\\begin{bmatrix}\n14 &amp; -14 &amp; \\color{red}0 &amp; -14\\\\\n1 &amp; -10 &amp; \\color{red}0 &amp; -15\\\\\n\\color{blue}-5 &amp; \\color{blue}6 &amp; \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1} &amp; \\color{blue}7\\\\\n14 &amp; -14 &amp; \\color{red}0 &amp; -8\n\\end{bmatrix}\n\\]\nConsider the matrices $A$ and $B$ above. By applying three matrix elementary row operations\n$R_1 := R_1 - 3R_3$\n$R_2:=\\ldots$\n$R_4:= \\ldots$\neach of the form $R_i:=R_i+kR_3,$ the matrix $A$ can be transformed into the matrix $B.$ What are the missing elementary row operations?"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"To transform $A$ into $B,$ we need to make entries above and below $a_{33}={\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1}}$ equal to ${\\color{red}0}.$\nTo do this, we can apply the following elementary row operations:\n\\begin{align*}\nA\n&amp;=\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n-1 &amp; 4 &amp; 3&amp; 7\\\\\n-9 &amp; 2 &amp; 2 &amp; -1\\\\\n\\color{blue}-5 &amp; \\color{blue}6 &amp; \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1} &amp; \\color{blue}7\\\\\n4 &amp; -2 &amp; 2 &amp; 6\n\\end{bmatrix}\n&amp;&amp; \\qquad\n\\begin{array}{l}\nR_1:=R_1-3R_3\n\\end{array} \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;\\sim\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n14 &amp; -14 &amp; {\\color{red}0} &amp; -14\\\\\n-9 &amp; 2 &amp; 2 &amp; -1\\\\\n\\color{blue}-5 &amp; \\color{blue}6 &amp; \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1} &amp; \\color{blue}7\\\\\n4 &amp; -2 &amp; 2 &amp; 6\n\\end{bmatrix}\n&amp;&amp; \\qquad\n\\begin{array}{l}\nR_2:=R_2-2R_3\n\\end{array} \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;\\sim\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n14 &amp; -14 &amp; {\\color{red}0} &amp; -14\\\\\n1 &amp; -10 &amp; {\\color{red}0} &amp; -15\\\\\n\\color{blue}-5 &amp; \\color{blue}6 &amp; \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1} &amp; \\color{blue}7\\\\\n4 &amp; -2 &amp; 2 &amp; 6\n\\end{bmatrix}\n&amp;&amp; \\qquad\n\\begin{array}{l}\nR_4:=R_4-2R_3\n\\end{array} \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;\\sim\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n14 &amp; -14 &amp; {\\color{red}0} &amp; -14\\\\\n1 &amp; -10 &amp; {\\color{red}0} &amp; -15\\\\\n\\color{blue}-5 &amp; \\color{blue}6 &amp; \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1} &amp; \\color{blue}7\\\\\n14 &amp; -14 &amp; {\\color{red}0}&amp; -8\n\\end{bmatrix} \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= B.\n\\end{align*}\nTherefore, the missing elementary row operations are\n$\\qquad$ $R_2 := R_2 - 2R_3\\quad$ and $\\quad R_4:=R_4-2R_3.$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$R_2 := R_2 - 2R_3\\quad$ and $\\quad R_4:=R_4-2R_3$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$R_2 := R_2 - 2R_3\\quad$ and $\\quad R_4:=R_4+2R_3$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$R_2 := R_2 + 2R_3\\quad$ and $\\quad R_4:=R_4-2R_3$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$R_2 := R_2 + 2R_3\\quad$ and $\\quad R_4:=R_4+2R_3$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$R_2 := R_2 - R_3\\quad$ and $\\quad R_4:=R_4-R_3$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"M"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Creating Rows or Columns Containing Zeros Using Gaussian Elimination"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"\\[A =\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n-10 &amp; 6 &amp; 6&amp; 2\\\\\n\\color{blue}8 &amp; \\color{blue}-3 &amp; \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{-3} &amp; \\color{blue}0\\\\\n3 &amp; -8 &amp; -9 &amp; 0\\\\\n0 &amp; 8 &amp; 3 &amp; 8\n\\end{bmatrix},\n\\qquad\nB=\\begin{bmatrix}\n6 &amp; 0 &amp; \\color{red}0 &amp; 2\\\\\n\\color{blue}8 &amp; \\color{blue}-3 &amp; \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{-3} &amp; \\color{blue}0 \\\\\n-21 &amp; 1 &amp; \\color{red}0 &amp; 0\\\\\n8 &amp; 5 &amp; \\color{red}0 &amp; 8\n\\end{bmatrix}\n\\]\nConsider the matrices $A$ and $B$ above. By applying three matrix elementary row operations\n$R_1:= \\ldots$\n$R_3 := R_3 - 3R_2$\n$R_4:= \\ldots$\neach of the form $R_i:=R_i+kR_2,$ the matrix $A$ can be transformed into the matrix $B.$ What are the missing elementary row operations?"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"To transform $A$ into $B,$ we need to make entries above and below $a_{23}={\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{-3}}$ equal to ${\\color{red}0}.$\nTo do this, we can apply the following elementary row operations:\n\\begin{align*}\nA\n&amp;=\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n-10 &amp; 6 &amp; 6&amp; 2\\\\\n\\color{blue}8 &amp; \\color{blue}-3 &amp; \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{-3} &amp; \\color{blue}0 \\\\\n3 &amp; -8 &amp; -9 &amp; 0\\\\\n0 &amp; 8 &amp; 3 &amp; 8\n\\end{bmatrix}\n&amp;&amp; \\qquad\n\\begin{array}{l}\nR_1:=R_1+2R_2\n\\end{array} \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;\\sim\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n6 &amp; 0 &amp; {\\color{red}0}&amp; 2\\\\\n\\color{blue}8 &amp; \\color{blue}-3 &amp; \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{-3} &amp; \\color{blue}0 \\\\\n3 &amp; -8 &amp; -9 &amp; 0\\\\\n0 &amp; 8 &amp; 3 &amp; 8\n\\end{bmatrix}\n&amp;&amp; \\qquad\n\\begin{array}{l}\nR_3:=R_3-3R_2\n\\end{array} \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;\\sim\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n6 &amp; 0 &amp; {\\color{red}0}&amp; 2\\\\\n\\color{blue}8 &amp; \\color{blue}-3 &amp; \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{-3} &amp; \\color{blue}0 \\\\\n-21 &amp; 1 &amp; {\\color{red}0} &amp; 0\\\\\n0 &amp; 8 &amp; 3 &amp; 8\n\\end{bmatrix}\n&amp;&amp; \\qquad\n\\begin{array}{l}\nR_4:=R_4+R_2\n\\end{array} \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;\\sim\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n6 &amp; 0 &amp; {\\color{red}0}&amp; 2\\\\\n\\color{blue}8 &amp; \\color{blue}-3 &amp; \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{-3} &amp; \\color{blue}0 \\\\\n-21 &amp; 1 &amp; {\\color{red}0} &amp; 0\\\\\n8 &amp; 5 &amp; {\\color{red}0} &amp; 8\n\\end{bmatrix} \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= B.\n\\end{align*}\nTherefore, the missing elementary row operations are\n$\\qquad$ $R_1 := R_1 + 2 R_2\\quad$ and $\\quad R_4:=R_4+ R_2.$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$R_1 := R_1 + 2 R_2\\quad$ and $\\quad R_4:=R_4+ R_2$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$R_1 := R_1 + 2 R_2\\quad$ and $\\quad R_4:=R_4- R_2$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$R_1 := R_1 - 2 R_2\\quad$ and $\\quad R_4:=R_4+ R_2$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$R_1 := R_1 - 2 R_2\\quad$ and $\\quad R_4:=R_4- R_2$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$R_1 := R_1 + R_2\\quad$ and $\\quad R_4:=R_4+ 2R_2$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"M"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Creating Rows or Columns Containing Zeros Using Gaussian Elimination"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"\\[A =\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n\\color{blue}9 &amp; -2 &amp; -16 \\\\\n\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{-2} &amp; 2 &amp; 8 \\\\\n\\color{blue}0 &amp; 4 &amp; 2 \\\\\n\\end{bmatrix},\n\\qquad\nB=\\begin{bmatrix}\n\\color{blue}9 &amp; 7 &amp; 20 \\\\\n\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{-2} &amp; \\color{red}0 &amp; \\color{red}0 \\\\\n\\color{blue}0 &amp; 4 &amp; 2 \\\\\n\\end{bmatrix}\n\\]\nBy applying elementary column operations of the form $C_i:=C_i+kC_1,$ the matrix $A$ can be transformed into the matrix $B.$ What are the elementary column operations?"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"To transform $A$ into $B,$ we need to make entries to the right of $a_{21}={\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{-2}}$ equal to ${\\color{red}0}.$\nTo do this, we can apply the following elementary column operations:\n\\begin{align*}\nA\n&amp;=\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n\\color{blue}9 &amp; -2 &amp; -16 \\\\\n\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{-2} &amp; 2 &amp; 8 \\\\\n\\color{blue}0 &amp; 4 &amp; 2 \\\\\n\\end{bmatrix}\n&amp;&amp; \\qquad\n\\begin{array}{l}\nC_2:=C_2+C_1\n\\end{array} \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;\\sim\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n\\color{blue}9 &amp; 7 &amp; -16 \\\\\n\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{-2} &amp; \\color{red}0 &amp; 8 \\\\\n\\color{blue}0 &amp; 4 &amp; 2 \\\\\n\\end{bmatrix}\n&amp;&amp; \\qquad\n\\begin{array}{l}\nC_3:=C_3+4C_1\n\\end{array} \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;\\sim\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n\\color{blue}9 &amp; 7 &amp; 20 \\\\\n\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{-2} &amp; \\color{red}0 &amp; \\color{red}0 \\\\\n\\color{blue}0 &amp; 4 &amp; 2 \\\\\n\\end{bmatrix}\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= B\n\\end{align*}\nTherefore, the elementary column operations are\n$\\qquad$ $C_2:=C_2+C_1\\quad$ and $\\quad C_3:=C_3+4C_1.$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$C_2:=C_2+C_1\\quad$ and $\\quad C_3:=C_3+4C_1$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$C_2:=C_2+C_1\\quad$ and $\\quad C_3:=C_3-4C_1$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$C_2:=C_2-C_1\\quad$ and $\\quad C_3:=C_3-4C_1$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$C_2:=C_2-C_1\\quad$ and $\\quad C_3:=C_3+4C_1$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$C_2:=C_2+4C_1\\quad$ and $\\quad C_3:=C_3+C_1$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Creating Rows or Columns Containing Zeros Using Gaussian Elimination"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"\\[A =\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n\\color{blue}0 &amp; 1 &amp; 2 \\\\\n\\color{blue}3 &amp; 7 &amp; 4 \\\\\n\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{3} &amp; 6 &amp; 3\\\\\n\\end{bmatrix},\n\\qquad\nB= \\begin{bmatrix}\n\\color{blue}0 &amp; 1 &amp; 2 \\\\\n\\color{blue}3 &amp; 1 &amp; 1 \\\\\n\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{3} &amp; \\color{red}0 &amp; \\color{red}0\\\\\n\\end{bmatrix}\n\\]\nBy applying elementary column operations of the form $C_i:=C_i+kC_1,$ the matrix $A$ can be transformed into the matrix $B.$ What are the elementary column operations?"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"To transform $A$ into $B,$ we need to make entries to the right of $a_{31}={\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{3}}$ equal to ${\\color{red}0}.$\nTo do this, we can apply the following elementary column operations:\n\\begin{align*}\nA\n&amp;=\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n\\color{blue}0 &amp; 1 &amp; 2 \\\\\n\\color{blue}3 &amp; 7 &amp; 4 \\\\\n\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{3} &amp; 6 &amp; 3\\\\\n\\end{bmatrix}\n&amp;&amp; \\qquad\n\\begin{array}{l}\nC_2:=C_2-2C_1\n\\end{array} \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;\\sim\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n\\color{blue}0 &amp; 1 &amp; 2 \\\\\n\\color{blue}3 &amp; 1 &amp; 4 \\\\\n\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{3} &amp; \\color{red}0 &amp; 3\\\\\n\\end{bmatrix}\n&amp;&amp; \\qquad\n\\begin{array}{l}\nC_3:=C_3-C_1\n\\end{array} \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;\\sim\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n\\color{blue}0 &amp; 1 &amp; 2 \\\\\n\\color{blue}3 &amp; 1 &amp; 1 \\\\\n\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{3} &amp; \\color{red}0 &amp; \\color{red}0\\\\\n\\end{bmatrix} \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= B\n\\end{align*}\nTherefore, the elementary column operations are\n$\\qquad$ $C_2:=C_2-2C_1\\quad$ and $\\quad C_3:=C_3-C_1.$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$C_2:=C_2-2C_1\\quad$ and $\\quad C_3:=C_3-C_1$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$C_2:=C_2-2C_1\\quad$ and $\\quad C_3:=C_3+C_1$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$C_2:=C_2+2C_1\\quad$ and $\\quad C_3:=C_3-C_1$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$C_2:=C_2-C_1\\quad$ and $\\quad C_3:=C_3-2C_1$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$C_2:=C_2+3C_1\\quad$ and $\\quad C_3:=C_3+2C_1$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Creating Rows or Columns Containing Zeros Using Gaussian Elimination"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"\\[A =\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n1 &amp; 1 &amp; 6 &amp; \\color{blue}1 \\\\\n8 &amp; 1 &amp; 2 &amp; \\color{blue}{5} \\\\\n0 &amp; 9 &amp; -5 &amp; \\color{blue}4 \\\\\n4 &amp; 5 &amp; 3 &amp; \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1}\n\\end{bmatrix},\n\\qquad\nB= \\begin{bmatrix}\n-3 &amp; -4 &amp; 3 &amp; \\color{blue}1 \\\\\n-12 &amp; -24 &amp; -13 &amp; \\color{blue}{5} \\\\\n-16 &amp; -11 &amp; -17 &amp; \\color{blue}4 \\\\\n\\color{red}0 &amp; \\color{red}0 &amp; \\color{red}0 &amp; \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1}\n\\end{bmatrix}\n\\]\nConsider the matrices $A$ and $B$ above. By applying three matrix elementary column operations\n$C_1:=C_1-4C_4$\n$C_2:= \\ldots$\n$C_3:= \\ldots$\neach of the form $C_i:=C_i+kC_4,$ the matrix $A$ can be transformed into the matrix $B.$ What are the missing elementary column operations?"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"To transform $A$ into $B,$ we need to make entries to the left of $a_{44}={\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1}}$ equal to ${\\color{red}0}.$\nTo do this, we can apply the following elementary column operations:\n\\begin{align*}\nA\n&amp;=\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n1 &amp; 1 &amp; 6 &amp; \\color{blue}1 \\\\\n8 &amp; 1 &amp; 2 &amp; \\color{blue}{5} \\\\\n0 &amp; 9 &amp; -5 &amp; \\color{blue}4 \\\\\n4 &amp; 5 &amp; 3 &amp; \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1}\n\\end{bmatrix}\n&amp;&amp; \\qquad\n\\begin{array}{l}\nC_1:=C_1-4C_4\n\\end{array} \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;\\sim\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n-3 &amp; 1 &amp; 6 &amp; \\color{blue}1 \\\\\n-12 &amp; 1 &amp; 2 &amp; \\color{blue}{5} \\\\\n-16 &amp; 9 &amp; -5 &amp; \\color{blue}4 \\\\\n\\color{red}0 &amp; 5 &amp; 3 &amp; \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1}\n\\end{bmatrix}\n&amp;&amp; \\qquad\n\\begin{array}{l}\nC_2:=C_2-5C_4\n\\end{array} \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;\\sim\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n-3 &amp; -4 &amp; 6 &amp; \\color{blue}1 \\\\\n-12 &amp; -24 &amp; 2 &amp; \\color{blue}{5} \\\\\n-16 &amp; -11 &amp; -5 &amp; \\color{blue}4 \\\\\n\\color{red}0 &amp; \\color{red}0 &amp; 3 &amp; \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1}\n\\end{bmatrix}\n&amp;&amp; \\qquad\n\\begin{array}{l}\nC_3:=C_3-3C_4\n\\end{array} \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;\\sim\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n-3 &amp; -4 &amp; 3 &amp; \\color{blue}1 \\\\\n-12 &amp; -24 &amp; -13 &amp; \\color{blue}{5} \\\\\n-16 &amp; -11 &amp; -17 &amp; \\color{blue}4 \\\\\n\\color{red}0 &amp; \\color{red}0 &amp; \\color{red}0 &amp; \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1}\n\\end{bmatrix} \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= B.\n\\end{align*}\nTherefore, the corresponding elementary column operations are\n$\\qquad$ $C_2:=C_2-5C_4\\quad$ and $\\quad C_3:=C_3-3C_4.$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$C_2:=C_2-5C_4\\quad$ and $\\quad C_3:=C_3-3C_4$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$C_2:=C_2+5C_4\\quad$ and $\\quad C_3:=C_3+3C_4$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$C_2:=C_2-5C_4\\quad$ and $\\quad C_3:=C_3+3C_4$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$C_2:=C_2-3C_4\\quad$ and $\\quad C_3:=C_3-5C_4$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$C_2:=C_2+3C_4\\quad$ and $\\quad C_3:=C_3+5C_4$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"M"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Creating Rows or Columns Containing Zeros Using Gaussian Elimination"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"\\[A =\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n1 &amp; 5 &amp; \\color{blue}1&amp; 3\\\\\n3 &amp; 9 &amp; \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1} &amp; 4\\\\\n4 &amp; 0 &amp; \\color{blue}1 &amp; 7\\\\\n2 &amp; 3 &amp; \\color{blue}0 &amp; 1\n\\end{bmatrix},\n\\qquad\nB= \\begin{bmatrix}\n-2 &amp; -4 &amp; \\color{blue}1&amp; -1\\\\\n\\color{red}0 &amp; \\color{red}0 &amp; \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1} &amp; \\color{red}0\\\\\n1 &amp; -9 &amp; \\color{blue}1 &amp; 3\\\\\n2 &amp; 3 &amp; \\color{blue}0 &amp; 1\n\\end{bmatrix}\n\\]\nConsider the matrices $A$ and $B$ above. By applying three matrix elementary column operations\n$C_1:=C_1 - 3C_3$\n$C_2:= \\ldots$\n$C_4:= \\ldots$\neach of the form $C_i:=C_i+kC_3,$ the matrix $A$ can be transformed into the matrix $B.$ What are the missing elementary column operations?"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"To transform $A$ into $B,$ we need to make entries to the left and to the right of $a_{23}={\\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1}}$ equal to ${\\color{red}0}.$\nTo do this, we can apply the following elementary column operations:\n\\begin{align*}\nA\n&amp;=\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n1 &amp; 5 &amp; \\color{blue}1&amp; 3\\\\\n3 &amp; 9 &amp; \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1} &amp; 4\\\\\n4 &amp; 0 &amp; \\color{blue}1 &amp; 7\\\\\n2 &amp; 3 &amp; \\color{blue}0 &amp; 1\n\\end{bmatrix}\n&amp;&amp; \\qquad\n\\begin{array}{l}\nC_1:=C_1-3C_3\n\\end{array} \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;\\sim\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n-2 &amp; 5 &amp; \\color{blue}1&amp; 3\\\\\n\\color{red}0 &amp; 9 &amp; \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1} &amp; 4\\\\\n1 &amp; 0 &amp; \\color{blue}1 &amp; 7\\\\\n2 &amp; 3 &amp; \\color{blue}0 &amp; 1\n\\end{bmatrix}\n&amp;&amp; \\qquad\n\\begin{array}{l}\nC_2:=C_2-9C_3\n\\end{array} \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;\\sim\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n-2 &amp; -4 &amp; \\color{blue}1&amp; 3\\\\\n\\color{red}0 &amp; \\color{red}0 &amp; \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1} &amp; 4\\\\\n1 &amp; -9 &amp; \\color{blue}1 &amp; 7\\\\\n2 &amp; 3 &amp; \\color{blue}0 &amp; 1\n\\end{bmatrix}\n&amp;&amp; \\qquad\n\\begin{array}{l}\nC_4:=C_4-4C_3\n\\end{array} \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;\\sim\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n-2 &amp; -4 &amp; \\color{blue}1&amp; -1\\\\\n\\color{red}0 &amp; \\color{red}0 &amp; \\color{blue}\\bbox[lightgray,2pt]{1} &amp; \\color{red}0\\\\\n1 &amp; -9 &amp; \\color{blue}1 &amp; 3\\\\\n2 &amp; 3 &amp; \\color{blue}0 &amp; 1\n\\end{bmatrix} \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= B.\n\\end{align*}\nTherefore, the corresponding elementary column operations are\n$\\qquad$ $C_2:=C_2-9C_3\\quad$ and $\\quad C_4:=C_4-4C_3.$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$C_2:=C_2-9C_3\\quad$ and $\\quad C_4:=C_4-4C_3$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$C_2:=C_2+9C_3\\quad$ and $\\quad C_4:=C_4+4C_3$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$C_2:=C_2-9C_3\\quad$ and $\\quad C_4:=C_4+4C_3$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$C_2:=C_2-4C_3\\quad$ and $\\quad C_4:=C_4-9C_3$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$C_2:=C_2+4C_3\\quad$ and $\\quad C_4:=C_4+9C_3$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"M"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Row Echelon Form"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"What is the sum of the leading entries of the $2$nd and $4$th rows in the matrix $A,$ given below?\n\\[\nA=\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n3 &amp; 2 &amp; 5 &amp; -9\\\\\n0 &amp; 0 &amp; -4 &amp; 1 \\\\\n0 &amp; 0 &amp; 0 &amp; 5 \\\\\n0 &amp; 7 &amp; 7 &amp; -3\n\\end{bmatrix}\n\\]"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The first non-zero entry of each row (from left to right) is the leading entry.\nTherefore, the leading entries of the $2$nd and $4$th rows of $A$ are $-4$ and $7$, respectively.\n\\[\nA\n=\\begin{bmatrix}\n3 &amp; 2 &amp; 5 &amp; -9\\\\\n0 &amp; 0 &amp; \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{-4} &amp; 1 \\\\\n0 &amp; 0 &amp; 0 &amp; 5 \\\\\n0 &amp; \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{7} &amp; 7 &amp; -3\n\\end{bmatrix}.\n\\]\nSo, the answer is $-4+7=3.$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$3$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$-4$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$1$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$8$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$5$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Row Echelon Form"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"What is the sum of the leading entries of the $1$st, $2$nd, and $3$rd rows in the matrix $A,$ given below?\n\\[\nA=\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n0 &amp; -7 &amp; 2 &amp; 0\\\\\n0 &amp; 0 &amp; 5 &amp; 6 \\\\\n3 &amp; 0 &amp; 2 &amp; -1 \\\\\n-1 &amp; 0 &amp; 0 &amp; 1\n\\end{bmatrix}\n\\]"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The first non-zero entry of each row (from left to right) is the leading entry.\nTherefore, the leading entries of the $1$st, $2$nd, and $3$rd rows of $A$ are $-7$, $5$, and $3$, respectively.\n\\[\nA\n=\\begin{bmatrix}\n0 &amp; \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{-7} &amp; 2 &amp; 0 \\\\\n0 &amp; 0 &amp; \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{5} &amp; 6 \\\\\n\\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{3} &amp; 0 &amp; 2 &amp; -1 \\\\\n-1 &amp; 0 &amp; 0 &amp; 1\n\\end{bmatrix}\n\\]\nSo, the answer is $-7+5+3=1.$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$1$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$10$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$0$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$2$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$8$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Row Echelon Form"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Which of the following matrices are not in row echelon form?\n\\[\nA= \\left[\n\\begin{array}{cccc}\n6 &amp; 0 &amp; 3 &amp; 0 \\\\\n0 &amp; 0 &amp; 3 &amp; 1 \\\\\n0 &amp; 0 &amp; 0 &amp; 2 \\\\\n0 &amp; 0 &amp; 6 &amp; 0 \\\\\n0 &amp; 0 &amp; 0 &amp; 0 \\\\\n\\end{array}\n\\right],\n\\quad\nB= \\left[\n\\begin{array}{cccccc}\n1 &amp; 0 &amp; 5 &amp; 1 &amp; -5 \\\\\n0 &amp; 0 &amp; 2 &amp; 1 &amp; 0 \\\\\n0 &amp; 0 &amp; 0 &amp; 0 &amp; 4\n\\end{array}\n\\right],\n\\quad\nC= \\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc}\n5 &amp; 5 &amp; 3 &amp; 4 \\\\\n0 &amp; 1 &amp; 5 &amp; 3 \\\\\n0 &amp; 2 &amp; 2 &amp; 2 \\\\\n0 &amp; 0 &amp; -7 &amp; 4\n\\end{array}\n\\right].\n\\]"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Remember that a matrix is in row echelon form if it satisfies the following conditions:\nAll the zero rows (rows with only zeros), if any, are placed at the bottom of the matrix.\nEach leading entry of a row is in a column to the right of the leading entry of the row above it.\nWith that in mind, let's look at each matrix in turn.\nThe matrix $A$ is not in row echelon form since the leading entry $a_{43} \\neq 0$ (condition II is violated).\n\\[\nA= \\left[\\begin{array}{cccc}\n\\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{6} &amp; 0 &amp; 3 &amp; 0 \\\\\n{\\color{blue}0} &amp; {\\color{blue}0} &amp; \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{3} &amp; 1 \\\\\n{\\color{blue}0} &amp; {\\color{blue}0} &amp; {\\color{blue}0} &amp; \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{2} \\\\\n{\\color{blue}0} &amp; {\\color{blue}0} &amp; \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{{\\color{red}6}} &amp; {\\color{blue}0} \\\\\n{\\color{blue}0} &amp; {\\color{blue}0} &amp; {\\color{blue}0} &amp; {\\color{blue}0}\n\\end{array}\\right]\n\\]\nThe matrix $B$ is in row echelon form (both conditions are satisfied).\n\\[\nB= \\left[\\begin{array}{cccccc}\n\\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{1} &amp; 0 &amp; 5 &amp; 1 &amp; -5 \\\\\n{\\color{blue}0} &amp; {\\color{blue}0} &amp; \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{2} &amp; 1 &amp; 0 \\\\\n{\\color{blue}0} &amp; {\\color{blue}0} &amp; {\\color{blue}0} &amp; {\\color{blue}0} &amp; \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{4}\n\\end{array}\\right]\n\\]\nThe matrix $C$ is not in row echelon form since the leading entry $c_{32} \\neq 0$ (condition II is violated).\n\\[\nC=\\begin{bmatrix}\n\\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{5} &amp; 5 &amp; 3 &amp; 4 \\\\\n{\\color{blue}0} &amp; \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{1} &amp; 5 &amp; 3 \\\\\n{\\color{blue}0} &amp; \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{{\\color{red}2}} &amp; 2 &amp; 2 \\\\\n{\\color{blue}0} &amp; {\\color{blue}0} &amp; \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{-7} &amp; 4\n\\end{bmatrix}\n\\]\nTherefore, only $A$ and $C$ are not in row echelon form."</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$A$ and $C$ only"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$A$ and $B$ only"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$A$ only"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$B$ only"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$B$ and $C$ only"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Row Echelon Form"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Which of the following matrices are not in row echelon form?\n\\[\nA= \\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc}\n1 &amp; 3 &amp; 5 \\\\\n0 &amp; 1 &amp; 2 \\\\\n0 &amp; 0 &amp; 0\n\\end{array}\n\\right],\n\\quad\nB= \\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccccc}\n1 &amp; 0 &amp; 0 &amp; 0 \\\\\n0 &amp; 5 &amp; 0 &amp; 4 \\\\\n0 &amp; 0 &amp; 0 &amp; 2 \\\\\n0 &amp; 0 &amp; 0 &amp; 0\n\\end{array}\n\\right],\n\\quad\nC= \\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc}\n0 &amp; 2 &amp; 3 \\\\\n0 &amp; -1 &amp; -2 \\\\\n0 &amp; 0 &amp; -1 \\\\\n\\end{array}\n\\right].\n\\]"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Remember that a matrix is in row echelon form if it satisfies the following conditions:\nAll the zero rows (rows with only zeros), if any, are placed at the bottom of the matrix.\nEach leading entry of a row is in a column to the right of the leading entry of the row above it.\nWith that in mind, let's look at each matrix in turn.\nThe matrix $A$ is in row echelon form (both conditions are satisfied).\n\\[\nA= \\left[\\begin{array}{cccc}\n\\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{1} &amp; 3 &amp; 5 \\\\\n{\\color{blue}0} &amp; \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{1} &amp; 2 \\\\\n{\\color{blue}0} &amp; {\\color{blue}0} &amp; {\\color{blue}0}\n\\end{array}\\right]\n\\]\nThe matrix $B$ is in row echelon form (both conditions are satisfied).\n\\[\nB= \\left[\\begin{array}{cccccc}\n\\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{1} &amp; 0 &amp; 0 &amp; 0 \\\\\n{\\color{blue}0} &amp; \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{5} &amp; 0 &amp; 4 \\\\\n{\\color{blue}0} &amp; {\\color{blue}0} &amp; {\\color{blue}0} &amp; \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{2} \\\\\n{\\color{blue}0} &amp; {\\color{blue}0} &amp; {\\color{blue}0} &amp; {\\color{blue}0}\n\\end{array}\\right]\n\\]\nThe matrix $C$ is not in row echelon form since the leading entry $c_{22} \\neq 0$ (condition II is violated).\n\\[\nC=\\begin{bmatrix}\n0 &amp; \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{2} &amp; 3 \\\\\n0 &amp; \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{\\color{red}-1} &amp; \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{ -2} \\\\\n0 &amp; 0 &amp; -1 \\\\\n\\end{bmatrix}\n\\]\nTherefore, only $C$ is not in row echelon form."</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$C$ only"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$A$ only"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$B$ only"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$A$ and $C$ only"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$B$ and $C$ only"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Row Echelon Form"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Which of the following augmented matrices are in row echelon form?\n\\[\nA=\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n1 &amp; 0 &amp; 9 &amp; 2 \\\\\n0 &amp; -2 &amp; 0 &amp; 7 \\\\\n0 &amp; 0 &amp; 5 &amp; 0 \\\\\n0 &amp; 0 &amp; 0 &amp; 0\n\\end{array}\n\\right],\n\\quad\nB=\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n8 &amp; 0 &amp; 7 &amp; 0 \\\\\n0 &amp; 0 &amp; 0 &amp; 0 \\\\\n0 &amp; -9 &amp; 0 &amp; 0 \\\\\n0 &amp; 0 &amp; 1 &amp; 0\n\\end{array}\n\\right],\n\\quad\nC=\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n0 &amp; -5 &amp; 0 &amp; 3 \\\\\n0 &amp; 0 &amp; 6 &amp; 7 \\\\\n0 &amp; 0 &amp; 0 &amp; 9\n\\end{array}\n\\right].\n\\]"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Remember that an augmented matrix is in row echelon form if its coefficient matrix satisfies the following conditions:\nAll the zero rows (rows with only zeros), if any, are placed at the bottom of the matrix.\nEach leading entry of a row is in a column to the right of the leading entry of the row above it.\nWith that in mind, let's look at each matrix in turn.\nThe matrix $A$ is in row echelon form (both conditions are satisfied).\n\\[\nA=\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n\\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{1} &amp; 0 &amp; 9 &amp; 2 \\\\\n{\\color{blue}0} &amp; \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{-2} &amp; 0 &amp; 7 \\\\\n{\\color{blue}0} &amp; {\\color{blue}0} &amp; \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{5} &amp; 0 \\\\\n{\\color{blue}0} &amp; {\\color{blue}0} &amp; {\\color{blue}0} &amp; 0\n\\end{array}\n\\right]\n\\]\nThe matrix $B$ is not in row echelon form since the second row contains only zeros and it is not at the bottom of the matrix (condition I is violated).\n\\[\nB=\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n\\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{9} &amp; 0 &amp; 7 &amp; 0 \\\\\n{\\color{red}0} &amp; {\\color{red}0} &amp; {\\color{red}0} &amp; 0 \\\\\n0 &amp; \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{-9} &amp; 0 &amp; 0 \\\\\n0 &amp; 0 &amp; \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{1} &amp; 0\n\\end{array}\n\\right]\n\\]\nThe matrix $C$ is in row echelon form (both conditions are satisfied).\n\\[\nC=\\left[\n\\begin{array}{ccc|c}\n{\\color{blue}0} &amp; \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{-5} &amp; 0 &amp; 3 \\\\\n{\\color{blue}0} &amp; {\\color{blue}0} &amp; \\bbox[lightgray, 3pt]{6} &amp; 7 \\\\\n{\\color{blue}0} &amp; {\\color{blue}0} &amp; {\\color{blue}0} &amp; 9\n\\end{array}\n\\right]\n\\]\nTherefore, only $A$ and $C$ are in row echelon form."</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$A$ and $C$ only"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$A$ and $B$ only"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$B$ and $C$ only"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$A,$ $B,$ and $C$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$A$ only"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
      }
    ],
    <span class="hljs-attr">"linear-algebra"</span>: [
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Minors of a 3x3 Matrix"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Calculate the minor of the entry $b_{22}$ in the matrix\n$\nB= \\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n3 &amp; 13 &amp; -6 \\\\\n2 &amp; 7 &amp; 0 \\\\\n5 &amp; 0 &amp; 4\n}\n\\right\\rbrack.\n$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The minor of $b_{22} = 7$ (second row, second column) is the determinant of the remaining entries when the column and row containing $7$ are removed:\n\\[\n{B} =\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n3 &amp; {\\color{gray}\\ast} &amp; -6 \\\\\n{\\color{gray}\\ast} &amp; \\bbox[lightgray, 2pt]{\\color{gray}7} &amp; {\\color{gray}\\ast} \\\\\n5 &amp; {\\color{gray}\\ast} &amp; 4\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n\\]\nSo, the minor of $b_{22}=7$ is\n\\begin{align*}\n\\text{det}\n\\left[\n\\begin{array}{cc}\n3 &amp; -6 \\\\\n5 &amp; 4\n\\end{array}\n\\right]\n&amp;=\n\\left|\n\\begin{array}{cc}\n3 &amp; -6 \\\\\n5 &amp; 4\n\\end{array}\n\\right| \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=\n3 \\cdot 4 - (-6) \\cdot 5 \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= 12 - (-30) \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= 42.\n\\end{align*}"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$42$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$-18$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$28$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$-42$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$3$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Minors of a 3x3 Matrix"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Calculate the minor of the entry $d_{33}$ in the matrix\n$\n{D} =\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n3 &amp; -2 &amp; 4 \\\\\n4 &amp; -1 &amp; 54 \\\\\n11 &amp; 6 &amp; 7\n}\n\\right\\rbrack.\n$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The minor of $d_{33} = 7$ (third row, third column) is the determinant of the remaining entries when the column and row containing $7$ are removed:\n\\[\n{D} =\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n3 &amp; -2 &amp; {\\color{gray}\\ast} \\\\\n4 &amp; -1 &amp; {\\color{gray}\\ast} \\\\\n{\\color{gray}\\ast} &amp; {\\color{gray}\\ast} &amp; \\bbox[lightgray, 2pt]{\\color{gray}7}\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n\\]\nSo, the minor of $d_{33}=7$ is\n\\begin{align*}\n\\text{det}\n\\left[\n\\begin{array}{cc}\n3 &amp; -2 \\\\\n4 &amp; -1\n\\end{array}\n\\right]\n&amp;=\n\\left|\n\\begin{array}{cc}\n3 &amp; -2 \\\\\n4 &amp; -1\n\\end{array}\n\\right| \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= 3 \\cdot (-1) - (-2) \\cdot 4 \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= -3 - (-8) \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= 5.\n\\end{align*}"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$5$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$40$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$35$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$-23$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$-38$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Minors of a 3x3 Matrix"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"What is the minor of $k_{31}$ in the matrix\n$\n{K} =\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\nw &amp; u &amp; u^2 \\\\\nu &amp; w &amp; w^2 \\\\\n2 &amp; 3 &amp; uw\n}\n\\right\\rbrack ?\n$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"First, we remove the third row and first column:\n\\[\n{X} =\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n{\\color{gray}\\ast} &amp; u &amp; u^2 \\\\\n{\\color{gray}\\ast} &amp; w &amp; w^2 \\\\\n\\bbox[lightgray, 2pt]{\\color{gray}2} &amp; {\\color{gray}\\ast}&amp; {\\color{gray}\\ast}\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n\\]\nNext, we calculate the determinant:\n\\begin{align*}\n\\text{det}\n\\left[\n\\begin{array}{cc}\nu &amp; u^2 \\\\\nw &amp; w^2\n\\end{array}\n\\right]\n&amp;=\n\\left|\\begin{array}{cc}\nu &amp; u^2 \\\\\nw &amp; w^2\n\\end{array}\\right| \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=\nu \\cdot w^2 - u^2 \\cdot w \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= uw^2 - wu^2\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= uw(w-u)\n\\end{align*}"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$uw(w-u)$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$uw(w+u)$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$uw(u-w)$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$u^2w(w-u)$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$uw^2(u-w)$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"M"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Minors of a 3x3 Matrix"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"What is the minor of $u_{21}$ in the matrix\n$\n{U} =\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n-4 &amp; j &amp;k-2j \\\\\nkj &amp; j &amp; k \\\\\n7 &amp; -k &amp; j\n}\n\\right\\rbrack ?\n$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"First, we remove the second row and the first column:\n\\[\n{U} =\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n{\\color{gray}\\ast} &amp; j &amp;k-2j \\\\\n{\\bbox[lightgray, 2pt]{\\color{gray}kj}} &amp; {\\color{gray}\\ast} &amp; {\\color{gray}\\ast} \\\\\n\\color{gray}\\ast &amp; -k &amp; j}\n\\right\\rbrack\n\\]\nNext, we calculate the determinant:\n\\begin{align*}\n\\require{cancel}\n\\text{det}\n\\left[\n\\begin{array}{cc}\nj &amp; k-2j \\\\\n- k &amp; j\n\\end{array}\n\\right]\n&amp;=\n\\left|\\begin{array}{cc}\nj&amp; k-2j \\\\\n-k &amp; j\n\\end{array}\\right| \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=\nj \\cdot j- ( -k)\\cdot (k-2j)\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= j^2+k(k-2j ) \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= j^2+k^2 -k2j \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= j^2-2jk + k^2\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= (j-k)^2\n\\end{align*}"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$(j-k)^2$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$j+k$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$j^2-k^2$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$j^2-2k^2$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$2(k-j)$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"M"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Minors of a 3x3 Matrix"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Given that the minor of $c_{23}$ is $-86$, find all the possible values of $x$ in the matrix\n\\[\nC =\\left[\\begin{array}{ccc}\n-3 &amp; 7&amp;-1\\\\\n4 &amp; -4&amp; 5\\\\\n11 &amp;x &amp;6\n\\end{array}\\right].\n\\]"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"First, we remove the second row and third column:\n\\[\nC=\n\\left[\\begin{array}{ccc}\n-3 &amp; 7 &amp; {\\color{gray}\\ast} \\\\\n{\\color{gray}\\ast} &amp; {\\color{gray}\\ast} &amp; \\bbox[lightgray, 2pt]{\\color{gray}5} \\\\\n11 &amp; x &amp; {\\color{gray}\\ast}\n\\end{array}\\right]\n\\]\nThe minor of $c_{23}$ is\n\\begin{align*}\n\\textrm{det}\\left[\\begin{array}{cc}\n-3 &amp; 7\\\\\n11 &amp; x\n\\end{array} \\right]\n&amp;= \\left|\\begin{array}{cc}\n-3 &amp; 7\\\\\n11 &amp; x\n\\end{array}\\right| \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=(-3) \\cdot x - 7 \\cdot 11 \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=-3x-77.\n\\end{align*}\nWe're told that the minor of $c_{23}$ is $-86$, so we must have:\n\\begin{align*}\n-3x-77 &amp;=-86 \\\\[3pt]\n-3x&amp;=-9 \\\\[3pt]\nx &amp;= 3\n\\end{align*}\nTherefore, the solution is $x=3.$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$x=3$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$x=9$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$x=-9$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$x=-3$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$x=-12$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"M"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Minors of a 3x3 Matrix"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Given that the minor of $m_{22}$ is $3k^2-45$, find all the possible values of $k$ in the matrix\n\\[\nM=\\left[\\begin{array}{ccc}\n2 &amp; 5&amp;1\\\\\n3 &amp; -4&amp; -1\\\\\n9k-15 &amp;9 &amp;-3\n\\end{array}\\right].\n\\]"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"First, we remove the second row and second column:\n\\[\nM=\n\\left[\\begin{array}{ccc}\n2 &amp; {\\color{gray}\\ast} &amp; 1 \\\\\n{\\color{gray}\\ast} &amp; \\bbox[lightgray, 2pt]{\\color{gray}-4} &amp; {\\color{gray}\\ast} \\\\\n9k-15 &amp; {\\color{gray}\\ast} &amp; -3\n\\end{array}\\right]\n\\]\nThe minor of $m_{22}$ is\n\\begin{align*}\n\\textrm{det}\\left[\\begin{array}{cc}\n2 &amp; 1\\\\\n9k-15 &amp;-3\n\\end{array} \\right] &amp;= \\left|\\begin{array}{cc}\n2 &amp; 1\\\\\n9k-15 &amp;-3\n\\end{array}\\right|\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= 2\\cdot (-3) - (9k-15)\\cdot 1 \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=-6 - (9k-15)\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= -9k+9.\n\\end{align*}\nWe're told that the minor of $m_{22}$ is $3k^2-45$, so we must have:\n\\begin{align*}\n-9k+9 &amp;=3k^2-45\\\\[3pt]\n-3k+3 &amp;=k^2-15\\\\[3pt]\nk^2+3k-18 &amp;=0\\\\[3pt]\n(k-3)(k+6) &amp;=0\n\\end{align*}\nTherefore, the solutions are $k=3$ and $k=-6.$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$k=3$ and $k=-6$ only"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$k=3$ and $k=6$ only"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$k=-6$ only"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$k=8$ and $k=-6$ only"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$k=-3$ and $k=6$ only"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"H"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Determinant of a 2x2 Matrix"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"$\n\\left|\n\\begin{array}{cc}\n2x &amp; y^2 \\\\\nz &amp; -3\n\\end{array}\n\\right| =\n$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"For a $2 \\times 2$ matrix, the determinant is given by the formula\n\\[\n\\begin{vmatrix} a &amp; b \\\\ c &amp; d \\end{vmatrix} = ad - bc.\n\\]\nUsing the formula, we have:\n\\begin{align}\n\\left|\n\\begin{array}{cc}\n2x &amp; y^2 \\\\\nz &amp; -3\n\\end{array}\n\\right|\n&amp; = 2x \\cdot (-3) - y^2 \\cdot z \\\\[5pt]\n&amp; = -6x - y^2z\n\\end{align}"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$-6x - y^2z$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$6x - y^2z$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$6x - y^2$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$5$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$1$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Determinant of a 2x2 Matrix"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"If\n$\nB =\n\\left\\lbrack\n\\matrix {\n2 &amp; x \\\\\n2 &amp; 6\n}\n\\right\\rbrack\n$\nand\n$\\text{det}({B}) = 2$, what is the value of $x?$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"For a $2 \\times 2$ matrix, the determinant is given by the formula\n\\[\n\\begin{vmatrix} a &amp; b \\\\ c &amp; d \\end{vmatrix} = ad - bc.\n\\]\nUsing the formula, we have:\n\\begin{align}\n\\text{det}({B})\n&amp; =\n\\left|\n\\matrix {\n2 &amp; x \\\\\n2 &amp; 6\n}\n\\right|\\\\[5pt]\n&amp; =\n2 \\cdot 6 - x \\cdot 2 \\\\[5pt]\n&amp; = 12 - 2x.\n\\end{align}\nSince $\\det(B) = 2$, we get:\n\\begin{align}\n\\det(B) &amp;= 2 \\\\[3pt]\n12 - 2 x &amp; = 2\\\\[3pt]\n-2 x &amp; = 2 - 12\\\\[3pt]\n-2x &amp; = -10\\\\[3pt]\nx &amp; = 5\n\\end{align}"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$x=5$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$x=-5$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$x=2$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$x=-2$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$x=6$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Determinant of a 2x2 Matrix"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"If\n$\nG =\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n2+q &amp; -q^2+2q \\\\\n1 &amp; 2-q\n\\end{bmatrix}\n$\nand $\\det(G) = -6$, what is the value of $q?$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"For a $2 \\times 2$ matrix, the determinant is given by the formula\n\\[\n\\begin{vmatrix} a &amp; b \\\\ c &amp; d \\end{vmatrix} = ad - bc.\n\\]\nUsing the formula, we have:\n\\begin{align}\n\\require{cancel}\n\\det(G) &amp;=\n\\begin{vmatrix}\n2+q &amp; -q^2+2q \\\\\n1 &amp; 2-q\n\\end{vmatrix} \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= (2+q)(2-q)-(-q^2+2q) \\cdot 1\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= 2^2-q^2+q^2-2q \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= 4-\\cancel{q^2}+\\cancel{q^2}-2q \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= 4-2q\n\\end{align}\nSince $\\det(G)=-6$, we obtain:\n\\begin{align}\n\\det(G) &amp;= -6 \\\\[3pt]\n4-2q &amp; = -6 \\\\[3pt]\n2q&amp; = 4+6 \\\\[3pt]\n2q &amp;= 10 \\\\[3pt]\nq &amp; = 5\n\\end{align}"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$q=5$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$q=6$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$q=4$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$q=3$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$q=2$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"H"</span>
      }
    ],
    <span class="hljs-attr">"chain-rule"</span>: [
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Chain Rule With Logarithmic Functions"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Calculate $\\dfrac {\\textrm d f}{\\textrm d x}$ for $f(x) =5\\ln(3-2x)+7.$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"First, we express $f(x)=5\\ln(3-2x)+7$ as the composite function\n\\[\nf = 5\\ln u + 7, \\qquad u = 3-2x.\n\\]\nDifferentiating gives\n\\[\n\\dfrac{\\textrm d f}{\\textrm d u} = \\dfrac{5}{u},\\qquad \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}u}{\\textrm d x} = -2.\n\\]\nWe now apply the chain rule, and we get\n\\[\n\\eqalign{\n\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}f}{\\textrm{d}x}&amp;=\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}f}{\\textrm{d}u}\\cdot \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}u}{\\textrm{d}x}\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=\\dfrac{5}{u} \\cdot (-2)\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=-\\dfrac{10}{u}\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= -\\dfrac{10}{3-2x}.\n}\n\\]"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$ -\\dfrac{10}{3-2x}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$ \\dfrac{5}{3-2x}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{12}{3-2x}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$ \\dfrac{5}{\\ln(3-2x)}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$- \\dfrac{10}{\\ln(3-2x)}$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"M"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Chain Rule With Logarithmic Functions"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Given that $g(x)=\\ln (7x-5)$, find $\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}g}{\\textrm{d}x}.$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"First, we express $g(x)$ as the composite function\n\\[\ng = \\ln u, \\qquad u = 7x-5.\n\\]\nDifferentiating gives\n\\[\n\\dfrac{\\textrm d g}{\\textrm d u} = \\dfrac{1}{u}, \\qquad \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}u}{\\textrm d x} = 7.\n\\]\nWe now apply the chain rule, and we get\n\\[\n\\eqalign{\n\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}g}{\\textrm{d}x}&amp;=\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}g}{\\textrm{d}u}\\cdot \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}u}{\\textrm{d}x}\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=\\dfrac{1}{u} \\cdot 7\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=\\dfrac{1}{7x-5}\\cdot 7\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=\\dfrac{7}{7x-5}.\n}\n\\]"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{7}{7x-5}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{1}{7x-5}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{7x}{7x-5}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{7}{x-5}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$7\\ln(7x-5)$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Chain Rule With Logarithmic Functions"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Find the equation of the tangent to the curve $y =\\log_{2}(3-2x)$ at the point $(1,0).$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"First, we need to find the slope of the tangent line. The slope is equal to the derivative of the curve at the given point:\n\\[\nm = \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}y}{\\textrm{d}x}\\Bigg|_{x=1}\n\\]\nTo compute $\\dfrac{\\text dy}{\\text dx},$ we first express $y$ as the composite function\n\\[\ny = \\log_2{u}, \\qquad u = 3-2x.\n\\]\nDifferentiating gives\n\\[\n\\dfrac{\\textrm d y}{\\textrm d u} = \\dfrac{1}{u\\ln 2}, \\qquad \\dfrac{\\textrm d u}{\\textrm d x} = -2.\n\\]\nWe now apply the chain rule, and get\n\\[\n\\eqalign{\n\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}y}{\\textrm{d}x}&amp;=\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}y}{\\textrm{d}u}\\cdot \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}u}{\\textrm{d}x}\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=\\dfrac{1}{u \\ln 2} \\cdot (-2)\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=\\dfrac{1}{(3-2x)\\ln 2}\\cdot (-2)\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=\\dfrac{-2}{(3-2x)\\ln 2}.\n}\n\\]\nTherefore, the slope $m$ of the tangent line at the point where $x=1$ is\n\\begin{eqnarray}\n\\eqalign{\nm &amp;= \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}y}{\\textrm{d}x}\\Bigg|_{x=1} \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=\\dfrac{-2}{(3-2x)\\ln 2}\\Bigg|_{x=1}\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=-\\dfrac{2}{(3-2(1))\\ln(2)}\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=-\\dfrac{2}{\\ln(2)}.\n}\n\\end{eqnarray}\nNow we know that the slope of the tangent line at the point $(1,0)$ is $m=-\\dfrac{2}{\\ln(2)}.$ Finally, we find the equation of the tangent line at that point:\n\\begin{align*}\ny-y_1&amp;=m(x-x_1)\\\\[5pt]\ny-0&amp;=-\\dfrac{2}{\\ln(2)}(x-1)\\\\[5pt]\ny&amp;=-\\dfrac{2x}{\\ln(2)}+\\dfrac{2}{\\ln(2)}.\n\\end{align*}"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$y=-\\dfrac{2x}{\\ln(2)}+\\dfrac{2}{\\ln(2)}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$y=\\dfrac{2x}{\\ln(2)}+\\dfrac{1}{\\ln(2)}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$y=-\\dfrac{2x}{\\ln(2)}-\\dfrac{1}{2\\ln(2)}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$y=-\\dfrac{x}{2\\ln(2)}+\\dfrac{1}{\\ln(2)}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$y=\\dfrac{x}{\\ln(2)}-\\dfrac{2}{\\ln(2)}$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"H"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Chain Rule With Logarithmic Functions"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Calculate $\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}y}{\\textrm{d}x}$ for $y =\\log_3 (\\sqrt {2x}).$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"First, we express $y$ as the composite function\n\\[\ny = \\log_3 u, \\qquad u = \\sqrt{2x}.\n\\]\nDifferentiating gives\n\\[\n\\dfrac{\\textrm d y}{\\textrm d u} = \\dfrac{1}{u\\ln 3}, \\qquad \\dfrac{\\textrm d u}{\\textrm d x} = \\dfrac{\\sqrt 2}{2\\sqrt{x}}= \\dfrac{1}{\\sqrt{2x}}.\n\\]\nWe now apply the chain rule, and get\n\\[\n\\eqalign{\n\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}y}{\\textrm{d}x}&amp;=\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}y}{\\textrm{d}u}\\cdot \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}u}{\\textrm{d}x}\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=\\dfrac{1}{u \\ln 3} \\cdot \\dfrac{1}{\\sqrt {2x}}\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=\\dfrac{1}{\\sqrt {2x} \\ln 3} \\cdot \\dfrac{1}{\\sqrt {2x}}\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=\\dfrac{1}{2x \\ln 3}.\n}\n\\]"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{1}{2x \\ln 3}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{2}{x}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{1}{x \\ln 3}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{1}{2x}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{2}{x \\ln 3}$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"H"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Chain Rule With Exponential Functions"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Calculate $f'(x)$ if $f(x)= -2e^{4x-1}+3.$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"First, we express $f(x)$ as the composite function\n\\[\nf = -2e^u+3, \\qquad u=4x-1.\n\\]\nDifferentiating gives\n\\[\n\\dfrac{\\textrm d f}{\\textrm d u} = -2e^u,\\qquad \\dfrac{\\textrm d u}{\\textrm d x} =4.\n\\]\nWe now apply the chain rule, and get\n\\[\n\\eqalign{\n\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}f} {\\textrm{d}x}&amp;=\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}f} {\\textrm{d}u} \\cdot \\dfrac {\\textrm{d}u} {\\textrm{d}x}\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=-2e^u\\cdot 4\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=-8e^u\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=-8e^{4x-1}.\n}\n\\]"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$-8e^{4x-1}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$-8e^{4x-1}+3$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$-8e^{4x-2}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$8e^{4x-1}+3$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac 1 2e^{4x-1}$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Chain Rule With Exponential Functions"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Given that $y =-3e^{5-x},$ find $\\dfrac {\\textrm d y}{\\textrm d x}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"First, we express $y$ as the composite function\n\\[\ny =-3e^u, \\qquad u=5-x.\n\\]\nDifferentiating gives\n\\[\n\\dfrac{\\textrm d y}{\\textrm d u} = -3e^u,\\qquad \\dfrac{\\textrm d u}{\\textrm d x} =-1.\n\\]\nWe now apply the chain rule, and get\n\\[\n\\eqalign{\n\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}y} {\\textrm{d}x}&amp;=\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}y} {\\textrm{d}u} \\cdot \\dfrac {\\textrm{d}u} {\\textrm{d}x}\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=-3e^u\\cdot (-1)\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=3e^u\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=3e^{5-x}.\n}\n\\]"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$3e^{5-x}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$-3e^{5-x}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$-e^{5-x}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$-15e^{x-5}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$15e^{5-x}$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Chain Rule With Exponential Functions"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Find $f'(x)$ for $f(x) =e^{x^5+1} .$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"First we express $f(x)$ as the composite function\n\\[\nf = e^u, \\qquad u=x^5+1.\n\\]\nDifferentiating gives\n\\[\n\\dfrac{\\textrm d f}{\\textrm d u} = e^u,\\qquad \\dfrac{\\textrm d u}{\\textrm d x} =5x^4.\n\\]\nWe now apply the chain rule, and get\n\\begin{align*}\n\\dfrac{\\textrm d f}{\\textrm d x} &amp;= \\dfrac{\\textrm d f}{\\textrm d u}\\cdot \\dfrac{\\textrm d u}{\\textrm d x}\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=e^u \\cdot (5x^4)\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=5x^4e^u\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=5x^4e^{x^5+1}.\n\\end{align*}"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$5x^4e^{x^5+1}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$x^5e^{x^5+1}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$4x^5e^{x^5+1}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$-5x^4e^{x^5+1}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{1}{5}x^2e^{x^5+1}$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Chain Rule With Exponential Functions"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Find $f'(x)$ for $f(x) =e^{2x^6-4} .$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"First we express $f(x)$ as the composite function\n\\[\nf = e^u, \\qquad u=2x^6-4.\n\\]\nDifferentiating gives\n\\[\n\\dfrac{\\textrm d f}{\\textrm d u} = e^u,\\qquad \\dfrac{\\textrm d u}{\\textrm d x} =12x^5.\n\\]\nWe now apply the chain rule, and get\n\\begin{align*}\n\\dfrac{\\textrm d f}{\\textrm d x} &amp;= \\dfrac{\\textrm d f}{\\textrm d u}\\cdot \\dfrac{\\textrm d u}{\\textrm d x}\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=e^u \\cdot (12x^5)\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=12x^5e^u\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=12x^5e^{2x^6-4}.\n\\end{align*}"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$12x^5e^{2x^6-4}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$6x^5e^{2x^6-4}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$2x^5e^{2x^6-4}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$12x^4e^{x^6+4}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$4x^5e^{2x^6-4}$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Chain Rule With Exponential Functions"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Find the slope of the tangent to the curve $y=2^{9-x^3}$ at the point where $x=2.$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"First, we express $y$ as the composite function\n\\[\ny = 2^u, \\qquad u=9-x^3.\n\\]\nDifferentiating gives\n\\[\n\\dfrac{\\textrm d y}{\\textrm d u} = 2^u\\ln 2,\\qquad \\dfrac{\\textrm d u}{\\textrm d x} =-3x^2.\n\\]\nWe now apply the chain rule and get\n\\begin{align*}\n\\dfrac{\\textrm{d} y}{\\textrm{d}x}\n&amp;= \\dfrac{\\textrm d y}{\\textrm d u} \\cdot \\dfrac{\\textrm d u}{\\textrm d x}\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= 2^u \\ln 2\\cdot \\left(-3x^2\\right)\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= -\\left(3x^2\\right)2^{9-x^3} \\ln 2.\n\\end{align*}\nFinally, to find the slope of the tangent to the curve at $x=2$, we have to evaluate $\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}y} {\\textrm{d}x}$ at that point as follows:\n\\begin{align*}\n\\left.\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}y} {\\textrm{d}x}\\right|_{x=2}\n&amp;= \\left.-\\left(3x^2\\right)2^{9-x^3} \\ln 2\\right|_{x=2}\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= -\\left(3(2)^2\\right)2^{9-(2)^3} \\ln 2\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= -24 \\ln 2\n\\end{align*}"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$-24 \\ln 2$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$12 \\ln 2$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$24 \\ln 2$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$-48 \\ln 2$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$-12 \\ln 2$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"M"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Chain Rule With Exponential Functions"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"If $f(x)=4^{x^3},$ find $f'(x).$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"First, we express $f(x)$ as the composite function\n\\[\nf = 4^u, \\qquad u=x^3.\n\\]\nDifferentiating gives\n\\[\n\\dfrac{\\textrm d f}{\\textrm d u} = 4^u\\ln 4,\\qquad \\dfrac{\\textrm d u}{\\textrm d x} =3x^2.\n\\]\nWe now apply the chain rule, and get\n\\begin{align*}\n\\dfrac{\\textrm d f}{\\textrm d x} &amp;= \\dfrac{\\textrm d f}{\\textrm d u}\\cdot \\dfrac{\\textrm d u}{\\textrm d x}\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=4^u\\ln 4 \\cdot (3x^2)\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=3x^2 \\cdot 4^u\\ln 4\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=3x^2 \\cdot 4^{x^3}\\ln 4.\n\\end{align*}"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$3x^2 \\cdot 4^{x^3}\\ln 4$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$3\\cdot 4^{2x^3}\\ln 4$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$x^2 \\cdot 4^{x^3}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$3x^7 \\cdot 4^{x^5}\\ln 4$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$3x^2 \\cdot 4^{x^2}$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Chain Rule for Differentiation"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Find $\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}f} {\\textrm{d}x} $ when $f(x) = (3+2x)^4.$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"First, we express $f(x)$ as a composite function:\n\\[\nf = g^4, \\qquad g = 3+2x\n\\]\nThe chain rule states that\n\\[\n\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}f} {\\textrm{d}x} = \\dfrac {\\textrm{d}f} {\\textrm{d}g} \\cdot \\dfrac {\\textrm{d}g} {\\textrm{d}x}.\n\\]\nDifferentiating, we have\n\\[\n\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}f} {\\textrm{d}g} = 4g^3,\n\\]\nand\n\\[\n\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}g} {\\textrm{d}x} = 2.\n\\]\nFinally, multiplying the two together and substituting for $g$ gives\n\\begin{align*}\n\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}f} {\\textrm{d}x}\n&amp;= 4g^3 \\cdot 2\\\\\n&amp;= 8(3+2x)^3\n\\end{align*}"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$8(3+2x)^3$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$4(3+2x)^3$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$8x(3+2x)^3$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$10(3+2x)^5$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{2(3+2x)^3}{x}$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Chain Rule for Differentiation"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Find $\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}f} {\\textrm{d}x} $ when $f(x) = \\left(x^2 - x\\right)^6.$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"First, we express $f(x)$ as a composite function:\n\\[\nf = g^6, \\qquad g = x^2- x\n\\]\nThe chain rule states that\n\\[\n\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}f} {\\textrm{d}x} = \\dfrac {\\textrm{d}f} {\\textrm{d}g} \\cdot \\dfrac {\\textrm{d}g} {\\textrm{d}x}.\n\\]\nDifferentiating, we have\n\\[\n\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}f} {\\textrm{d}g} = 6g^5,\n\\]\nand\n\\[\n\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}g} {\\textrm{d}x} = 2x-1.\n\\]\nFinally, multiplying the two together and substituting for $g$ gives\n\\begin{align*}\n\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}f} {\\textrm{d}x}\n&amp;= 6g^5 \\cdot (2x - 1) \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= 6\\left(x^2-x\\right)^5\\left(2x-1\\right).\n\\end{align*}"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$6\\left(x^2-x\\right)^5\\left(2x-1\\right)$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$6(2x-1)^5$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$6(x^2 - x)^5$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{2x(x^2- x)}{6}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$12x(x^2 - x)^5$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Chain Rule for Differentiation"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Find $\\dfrac{\\textrm d y}{\\textrm d x}$ for $y = \\dfrac{1}{\\sqrt{3x-2}}.$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"We use the chain rule in the form\n\\[\n\\dfrac{\\textrm d y}{\\textrm d x} = \\dfrac{\\textrm d y}{\\textrm d u}\\cdot \\dfrac{\\textrm d u}{\\textrm d x}.\n\\]\nLet $u=3x-2.$ Then $y = \\dfrac 1{\\sqrt u} = u^{-1/2}.$ Differentiating, we have\n\\[\n\\dfrac{\\textrm d y}{\\textrm d u} = -\\dfrac{1}{2}u^{-3/2} = -\\dfrac{1}{2\\sqrt{u^3}}\n\\]\nand\n\\[\n\\dfrac{\\textrm d u}{\\textrm d x} = 3.\n\\]\nMultiplying the two together gives\n\\begin{align*}\n\\dfrac{\\textrm d y}{\\textrm d x}\n&amp;= -\\dfrac{1}{2\\sqrt{u^3}}\\cdot 3\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=- \\dfrac{3}{2\\sqrt{(3x-2)^3}}.\n\\end{align*}"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$- \\dfrac{3}{2\\sqrt{(3x-2)^3}}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$-\\dfrac{3}{2\\sqrt{3x-2}}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{3}{3x-2}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$-\\dfrac{3}{\\sqrt{3x-2}}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$-\\dfrac{1}{2\\sqrt{3x-2}}$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"M"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Chain Rule for Differentiation"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Find $\\dfrac{\\textrm d y}{\\textrm d x}$ for $y = -\\dfrac{2}{\\sqrt{3x-1}}.$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"We use the chain rule in the form\n\\[\n\\dfrac{\\textrm d y}{\\textrm d x} = \\dfrac{\\textrm d y}{\\textrm d u} \\cdot \\dfrac{\\textrm d u}{\\textrm d x}.\n\\]\nLet $u =3x-1.$ Then $y = -2\\cdot\\dfrac{1}{\\sqrt{u}}=-2u^{-1/2}.$ Differentiating, we have\n\\[\n\\dfrac{\\textrm d y}{\\textrm d u} = -2\\left(- \\dfrac{1}{2u^{3/2}}\\right) = \\dfrac{1}{\\sqrt{u^3}}\n\\]\nand\n\\[\n\\dfrac{\\textrm d u}{\\textrm d x} = 3.\n\\]\nMultiplying the two together gives\n\\begin{align*}\n\\dfrac{\\textrm d y}{\\textrm d x} &amp;= \\dfrac{\\textrm d y}{\\textrm d u} \\cdot \\dfrac{\\textrm d u}{\\textrm d x}\\\\[3pt]\n&amp;= \\dfrac{1}{\\sqrt{u^3}}\\cdot 3\\\\[3pt]\n&amp;= \\dfrac{3}{\\sqrt{(3x-1)^3}}.\n\\end{align*}"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{3}{\\sqrt{(3x-1)^3}}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{3}{\\sqrt[3]{(3x-1)^2}}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$-\\dfrac{3}{\\sqrt{(3x-1)^3}}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{3}{2\\sqrt{(3x-1)^3}}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$-\\dfrac{3}{2\\sqrt{(3x-1)^3}}$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"M"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Chain Rule for Differentiation"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Given that $f(x)=\\left(3x + 4 \\right)^5,$ find $f'(x).$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Let $g(x)=3x+4$ and $f(g(x)) = g(x)^{5}.$\nDifferentiating, we have\n\\begin{align*}\nf'(g(x)) &amp; = ( g^{5})' = 5g^{4}, \\\\[5pt]\ng'(x) &amp; = (3x + 4)' = 3.\n\\end{align*}\nThen, using the chain rule,\n\\begin{align*}\nf'(x) &amp; = f'(g(x))\\cdot g'(x)\\\\[5pt]\n&amp; = 5g^{4} \\cdot 3\\\\[5pt]\n&amp; = 15(3x + 4)^4.\n\\end{align*}"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$15(3x + 4)^4$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$5(3x + 4)^4$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$3(3x + 4)^5$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$9(3x + 4)^4$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$20(3x + 4)^4$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"M"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Chain Rule for Differentiation"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Find $f'(x)$ for $f(x) = -\\dfrac{4}{\\sqrt{x- x^2}}.$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Let $g(x)=x - x^2$ and $f(g(x)) = -\\dfrac{4}{\\sqrt{g}} = -4g^{-1/2}.$\nDifferentiating, we have\n\\begin{align*}\nf'(g(x)) &amp; = -4( g^{-1/2})' = 4\\cdot \\dfrac{1}{2} g^{-3/2} = \\dfrac 2{g^{3/2}} , \\\\[5pt]\ng'(x) &amp; = (x - x^2)' = 1 - 2x.\n\\end{align*}\nThen, using the chain rule,\n\\begin{align*}\nf'(x) &amp; = f'(g(x))\\cdot g'(x)\\\\[3pt]\n&amp; = \\dfrac{2}{g^{3/2}} \\cdot (1 - 2x)\\\\[3pt]\n% &amp; = \\dfrac{2 - 4x}{g^{3/2} } \\\\[3pt]\n&amp; = \\dfrac{2 - 4x}{\\sqrt{(x - x^2)^3} }.\n\\end{align*}"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{2 - 4x}{\\sqrt{(x - x^2)^3} }$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{1- 2x}{\\sqrt{(x - x^2)^3} }$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$-\\dfrac{1 - 2x}{\\sqrt{(x - x^2)^3} }$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$-\\dfrac{2 + 4x}{\\sqrt{(x - x^2)^3} }$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{2 + 4x}{\\sqrt{x - x^2} }$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"H"</span>
      }
    ],
    <span class="hljs-attr">"set-theory"</span>: [
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Constructive Definition of a Set"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Consider the set $A,$ defined as\n\\[\nA = \\left\\{ \\dfrac{1}{n^2} : n \\in \\mathbb {N}\\right\\}.\n\\]\nWhich of the following sets is equivalent to $A?$"</span>,
