        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The elements of $A$ are all the terms of the sequence $a_n,$ given by\n\\[\na_n = \\dfrac{1}{n^2}, \\qquad n \\in \\mathbb N = \\{1,2,3,4,\\ldots\\}.\n\\]\nLet's write down the first few terms of our sequence:\n\\begin{align}\na_1 &amp; = \\dfrac{1}{1^2} = 1\\\\[5pt]\na_2 &amp; = \\dfrac{1}{2^2} = \\dfrac{1}{4}\\\\[5pt]\na_3 &amp; = \\dfrac{1}{3^2} = \\dfrac{1}{9}\\\\[5pt]\na_4 &amp; = \\dfrac{1}{4^2} = \\dfrac{1}{16}\\\\[5pt]\n&amp; \\vdots\n\\end{align}\nTherefore,\n\\[\nA = \\left\\{1,\\dfrac{1}{4},\\dfrac{1}{9},\\dfrac{1}{16},\\dfrac{1}{25},\\dfrac{1}{36},\\dfrac{1}{49}, ...\\right\\}.\n\\]"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\left\\{1,\\dfrac{1}{4},\\dfrac{1}{9},\\dfrac{1}{16},\\dfrac{1}{25},\\dfrac{1}{36},\\dfrac{1}{49}, ...\\right\\}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\left\\{1,\\dfrac{1}{4},\\dfrac{1}{8},\\dfrac{1}{4},\\dfrac{1}{5},\\dfrac{1}{6},\\dfrac{1}{7}, ...\\right\\}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\left\\{1,\\dfrac{1}{4},\\dfrac{1}{12},\\dfrac{1}{16},\\dfrac{1}{25},\\dfrac{1}{36},\\dfrac{1}{49}\\right\\}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\left\\{1,\\dfrac{1}{4},\\dfrac{1}{9},\\dfrac{1}{16},\\dfrac{1}{25},\\dfrac{1}{36},\\dfrac{1}{49}\\right\\}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\left\\{1,\\dfrac{1}{4},\\dfrac{1}{9},\\dfrac{1}{18},\\dfrac{1}{27},\\dfrac{1}{45},\\dfrac{1}{49}\\right\\}$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Constructive Definition of a Set"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Consider the set $A,$ defined as\n\\[\nA = \\left\\{\\dfrac{k}{2} : k \\in \\mathbb{Z}\\right\\}.\n\\]\nWhich of the following sets is equivalent to $A?$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The elements of $A$ are all the terms of the sequence $a_k,$ given by\n\\[\na_k = \\dfrac{k}{2}, \\qquad k \\in \\mathbb Z = \\{0, \\pm 1,\\pm 2,\\pm 3,\\ldots\\}.\n\\]\nLet's write down some terms of our sequence until the pattern becomes clear.\n\\begin{align*}\n&amp; \\vdots \\\\[5pt]\na_{-3} &amp; = \\dfrac{(-3)}{2} = -\\dfrac{3}{2} \\\\[5pt]\na_{-2} &amp; = \\dfrac{(-2)}{2} = -1 \\\\[5pt]\na_{-1} &amp; = \\dfrac{(-1)}{2} = -\\dfrac{1}{2}\\\\[5pt]\na_{0} &amp; = \\dfrac{0}{2} = 0\\\\[5pt]\na_{1} &amp; = \\dfrac{1}{2}\\\\[5pt]\na_{2} &amp; = \\dfrac{2}{2} = 1\\\\[5pt]\na_{3} &amp; = \\dfrac{3}{2}\\\\[5pt]\n&amp; \\vdots\n\\end{align*}\nSo, the set consists of the elements\n\\[\nA = \\left\\{0, \\pm \\dfrac{1}{2}, \\pm 1, \\pm \\dfrac{3}{2}, \\ldots \\right\\}.\n\\]"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\left\\{0, \\pm \\dfrac{1}{2}, \\pm 1, \\pm \\dfrac{3}{2}, \\ldots \\right\\}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\left\\{0, \\pm \\dfrac{1}{2}, \\pm \\dfrac{1}{3}, \\pm \\dfrac{1}{4} \\ldots \\right\\}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\left\\{0, \\pm \\dfrac{1}{2}, \\pm \\dfrac{3}{2}, \\pm \\dfrac{5}{2}, \\ldots \\right\\}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\left\\{0, \\dfrac{1}{2}, \\dfrac{3}{2}, \\dfrac{5}{2}, \\ldots \\right\\}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\left\\{0, \\pm \\dfrac{1}{2}, 1, \\pm \\dfrac{3}{2}, \\ldots \\right\\}$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"M"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Constructive Definition of a Set"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Consider the set $B,$ defined as\n\\[\nB = \\left \\{ n^3 -4 : n \\in \\mathbb Z,\\, -2 \\leq n \\leq 2 \\right \\}.\n\\]\nWhich of the following sets is equivalent to $B?$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The condition after the \"$:$\" symbol\n\\[\nn \\in \\mathbb Z,\\, n\\leq 4\n\\]\nstates that $n$ can be any integer number that satisfies the inequality $-2 \\leq n \\leq 2.$ In other words,\n\\[\nn = \\{-2,-1,0,1,2\\}.\n\\]\nTherefore, the elements of $B$ are all the terms of the sequence $b_n,$ given by\n\\[\nb_n = n^3 - 4, \\qquad n \\in \\{-2,-1,0,1,2\\}.\n\\]\nLet's write down all the terms of our sequence:\n\\begin{align*}\nb_{-2}\n&amp;=(-2)^3 -4 = -8-4= -12 \\\\[5pt]\nb_{-1}\n&amp;= (-1)^3 -4 = -1 -4= -5 \\\\[5pt]\nb_0\n&amp;= 0^3 - 4 = 0 -4 = -4 \\\\[5pt]\nb_1\n&amp;=1^3 - 4 = 1- 4 = -3 \\\\[5pt]\nb_2\n&amp;= 2^3 - 4 = 8 - 4= 4\n\\end{align*}\nTherefore,\n\\[\nB = \\left\\{-12,-5,-4,-3,4 \\right\\}.\n\\]"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\left\\{-12,-5,-4,-3,4 \\right\\}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\left\\{-12,-5,0,1,2 \\right\\}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\left\\{-12,-5,-4,-3,-2 \\right\\}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\left\\{-12,-5,4,5,12 \\right\\}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\left\\{-12,-5,-4,3,6 \\right\\}$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"M"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Constructive Definition of a Set"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Consider the set $A,$ defined as\n\\[\nA = \\left \\{ \\dfrac{1}{n + 4} : n \\in \\mathbb{N} \\right \\}.\n\\]\nWhich of the following sets is equivalent to $A?$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The elements of $A$ are all the terms of the sequence $a_n,$ given by\n\\[\na_n = \\dfrac{1}{n + 4}, \\qquad n \\in \\mathbb{N}.\n\\]\nLet's write down the first few terms of our sequence:\n\\begin{align*}\na_1\n&amp;=\\dfrac{1}{1+4} =\\dfrac{1}{5} \\\\[5pt]\na_2\n&amp;= \\dfrac{1}{2+4} = \\dfrac{1}{6} \\\\[5pt]\na_3\n&amp;= \\dfrac{1}{3+4} = \\dfrac{1}{7} \\\\[5pt]\na_4\n&amp;=\\dfrac{1}{4+4} = \\dfrac{1}{8} \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;\\vdots\n\\end{align*}\nTherefore,\n\\[\nA = \\left\\{\\dfrac{1}{5},\\dfrac{1}{6},\\dfrac{1}{7},\\dfrac{1}{8}, \\ldots \\right\\}.\n\\]"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\left\\{\\dfrac{1}{5},\\dfrac{1}{6},\\dfrac{1}{7},\\dfrac{1}{8}, \\ldots\\right\\}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\left\\{\\dfrac{1}{5},\\dfrac{1}{6},\\dfrac{1}{8},\\dfrac{1}{9}, \\ldots\\right\\}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\left\\{\\dfrac{1}{5},\\dfrac{1}{6},\\dfrac{1}{4},\\dfrac{1}{3}, \\ldots\\right\\}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\left\\{\\dfrac{1}{5},\\dfrac{1}{6},\\dfrac{1}{12},\\dfrac{1}{15}, \\ldots\\right\\}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\left\\{\\dfrac{1}{5},\\dfrac{1}{6},\\dfrac{1}{7},\\dfrac{1}{8} \\right\\}$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Constructive Definition of a Set"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Consider the set $A,$ defined as\n\\[\nA = \\big \\{ a_n\\,:\\,a_{n+2} = a_{n+1} + 2a_n \\,,\\, a_1 = 2,\\, a_2 =4,\\, n \\in\\mathbb N \\big\\}.\n\\]\nWhich of the following sets is equivalent to $A?$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The elements of $A$ are all the terms of the recursive sequence $a_n,$ given by\n\\[\na_{n+2} = a_{n+1} + 2a_n, \\quad a_1 = 2,\\quad a_2 = 4,\\qquad n \\in\\mathbb N.\n\\]\nLet's write down the first few terms of our sequence:\n\\begin{align*}\na_1 &amp; = 2\\\\[10pt]\na_2\n&amp;=4 \\\\[10pt]\na_3\n&amp;= a_2 + 2a_1 \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= 4+2(2)\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=8 \\\\[10pt]\na_4\n&amp;= a_3+2a_2\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= 8+2(4)\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=16 \\\\[10pt]\na_5\n&amp;= a_4+2a_3\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= 16+2(8)\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=32 \\\\[10pt]\n&amp;\\vdots\n\\end{align*}\nTherefore,\n\\[\nA = \\left\\{2,4,8,16,32, \\ldots \\right\\}.\n\\]"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\left\\{2,4,8,16,32, \\ldots \\right\\}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\left\\{2,4,8,12,24, \\ldots \\right\\}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\left\\{2,4,8,14,22, \\ldots \\right\\}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\left\\{2,4,8,12,16, \\ldots \\right\\}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\left\\{2,4,8,16,48, \\ldots \\right\\}$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"M"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Constructive Definition of a Set"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Consider the set $B,$ defined as\n\\[\nB = \\big \\{ b_n\\,:\\,b_{n+1} = 3b_{n} \\,,\\, b_1 =2,\\, n \\in \\mathbb N \\big\\}.\n\\]\nWhich of the following sets is equivalent to $B?$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The elements of $B$ are all the terms of the recursive sequence $b_n,$ given by\n\\[\nb_{n+1} = 3b_{n} , \\quad b_1 =2,\\qquad n \\in \\mathbb{N}.\n\\]\nLet's write down the first few terms of our sequence:\n\\begin{align*}\nb_1 &amp; = 2 \\\\[10pt]\nb_2\n&amp;= 3b_1\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= 3(2)\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= 6 \\\\[10pt]\nb_3\n&amp;= 3b_2 \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= 3(6)\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=18 \\\\[10pt]\nb_4\n&amp;= 3b_3\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= 3(18)\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=54 \\\\[10pt]\n&amp;\\vdots\n\\end{align*}\nTherefore,\n\\[\nB = \\left\\{2,6,18,54, \\ldots \\right\\}.\n\\]"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\left\\{2,6,18,54,\\ldots\\right\\}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\left\\{2,6,18, 24, \\ldots \\right\\}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\left\\{2,6,18,32, \\ldots \\right\\}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\left\\{2,6,18,22,\\ldots \\right\\}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\left\\{2,6,18, 58, \\ldots \\right\\}$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Equivalent Sets"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Which set is equivalent to $S = \\{5, 1, 1, 4, 1, 5\\}?$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Two sets are equivalent if they contain the same elements. It doesn't matter if the elements are listed differently or if one set has duplicates and the other does not.\nTo determine which of the given sets is equivalent to $S,$ we start by listing the elements of $S,$ ignoring any duplicates:\n\\[\n5, \\qquad 1, \\qquad 4\n\\]\nTherefore, the only set equivalent to $S$ is $\\{1,4,5\\}$ since it contains the same elements as $S.$\nThe other sets are not equivalent to $S{:}$\n$\\{ 1,5 \\}$ is missing the element $4.$\n$\\{ \\{5\\}, \\{1\\}, 4 \\}$ is missing the elements $5$ and $1.$ Note that $\\{5\\}\\neq 5$ and $\\{1\\} \\neq 1.$\n$\\{ 1, \\dots, 5 \\}$ means $\\{1,2,3,4,5\\}$ has the elements $2$ and $3,$ which are not in $S.$\n$\\{\\{4\\},1, 5\\}$ is missing the element $4.$ Note that $\\{4\\}\\neq 4.$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\{1,4,5\\}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\{ 1,5 \\}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\{ \\{5\\}, \\{1\\}, 4 \\}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\{ 1, \\dots, 5 \\}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\{\\{4\\},1, 5\\}$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Equivalent Sets"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Which set is equivalent to $S = \\{x, y, \\{z\\}, y, \\{x\\}\\}?$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Two sets are equivalent if they contain the same elements. It doesn't matter if the elements are listed differently or if one set has duplicates and the other does not.\nTo determine which of the given sets is equivalent to $S,$ we start by listing the elements of $S,$ ignoring any duplicates:\n\\[\nx, \\qquad y, \\qquad \\{z\\},\\qquad \\{x\\}\n\\]\nTherefore, the only set equivalent to $S$ is $\\{x,\\{x\\}, y, \\{z\\}\\}$ since it contains the same elements as $S.$\nThe other sets are not equivalent to $S{:}$\n$\\{ x,y,z \\}$ is missing the elements $\\{ x \\}$ and $\\{z\\}.$ Note that $\\{x\\}\\neq x$ and $\\{z\\} \\neq z.$\n$\\{ \\{x\\}, y, \\{z\\} \\}$ is missing the element $x.$ Note that $x\\neq \\{x\\}.$\n$\\{ \\{x,y\\}, \\{z\\}, x \\}$ is missing the elements $\\{x\\}$ and $y.$ Note that $\\{x\\}\\neq x.$\n$\\{ x, y, \\{z\\} \\}$ is missing the element $\\{ x\\}.$ Note that $\\{x\\}\\neq x.$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\{x,\\{x\\}, y, \\{z\\}\\}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\{ x,y,z \\}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\{ \\{x\\}, y, \\{z\\} \\}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\{ \\{x,y\\}, \\{z\\}, x \\}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\{ x, y, \\{z\\} \\}$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Equivalent Sets"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Which set is equivalent to $S = \\{\\{m,n\\}, \\{p\\}, o, \\{p\\},o\\}?$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Two sets are equivalent if they contain the same elements. It doesn't matter if the elements are listed differently or if one set has duplicates and the other does not.\nTo determine which of the given sets is equivalent to $S,$ we start by listing the elements of $S,$ ignoring any duplicates:\n\\[\n\\{m,n\\}, \\qquad \\{p\\}, \\qquad o\n\\]\nTherefore, the only set equivalent to $S$ is $\\{\\{m,n\\}, o, \\{p\\}\\}$ since it contains the same elements as $S.$\nThe other sets are not equivalent to $S{:}$\n$\\{ m, n, o , p \\}$ is missing the elements $\\{m,n\\}$ and $\\{p\\}.$ Note that $\\{p\\} \\neq p.$\n$\\{ \\{m, n\\}, p, o \\}$ is missing the element $\\{ p\\}.$ Note that $p\\neq \\{p\\}.$\n$\\{ \\{m,n\\}, o , \\{p\\}, q \\}$ has the element $q$ that is not in $S.$\n$\\{ m, n, o, \\{p\\}\\}$ is missing the element $\\{ m,n \\}.$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\{\\{m,n\\}, o, \\{p\\}\\}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\{ m, n, o , p \\}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\{ \\{m, n\\}, p, o \\}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\{ \\{m,n\\}, o , \\{p\\}, q \\}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\{ m, n, o, \\{p\\}\\}$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
      }
    ],
    <span class="hljs-attr">"distributions"</span>: [
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Modeling With the Poisson Distribution"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"It is known that, on average, $6$ cell phones per batch produced by a cellphone factory have defects. What is the probability that the next batch contains exactly $4$ phones with defects? Round your answer to $3$ decimal places. You may assume that defective phones occur independently and at a constant average rate."</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"A Poisson random variable $X$ with rate $\\lambda$ has the following probability mass function:\n\\[\nf(x) = \\dfrac{\\lambda^x e^{-\\lambda}}{x!}\n\\]\nLet $X$ be the number of cell phones with defects. Since the average rate is $6$ cell phones per batch, and events occur independently and at a constant average rate, $X$ can be modeled as a Poisson random variable with rate $\\lambda = 6,$ and we can write $X\\sim \\textrm{Po}(6).$\nSo, $X$ has the following probability mass function:\n\\[\nf(x) = \\dfrac{6^x e^{-6}}{x!}\n\\]\nTherefore, the probability of having $4$ cell phones with defects in the next batch is\n\\[\\begin{align*}\nP(X=4) &amp;= f(4) \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= \\dfrac{6^{4} e^{-6}}{4!} \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;\\approx 0.134\n\\end{align*}\\]\nrounded to $3$ decimal places."</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$0.134$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$0.141$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$0.099$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$0.118$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$0.165$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"M"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Modeling With the Poisson Distribution"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"An office secretary notes that, on average, $5$ calls are received per minute. What is the probability that she will receive $7$ calls in the next minute? Round your answer to $3$ decimal places. You may assume that calls occur independently and at a constant average rate."</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"A Poisson random variable $X$ with rate $\\lambda$ has the following probability mass function:\n\\[\nf(x) = \\dfrac{\\lambda^x e^{-\\lambda}}{x!}\n\\]\nLet $X$ be the number of calls per minute. Since the average rate is $5$ calls per minute, and events occur independently and at a constant average rate, the number of calls can be modeled as a Poisson random variable with rate $\\lambda = 5,$ and we can write $X\\sim \\textrm{Po}(5).$\nSo, $X$ has the following probability mass function:\n\\[\nf(x) = \\dfrac{5^x e^{-5}}{x!}\n\\]\nTherefore, the probability of having $7$ calls in the next minute is\n\\[\\begin{align*}\nP(X=7) &amp;= f(7) \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= \\dfrac{5^{7} e^{-5}}{7!} \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;\\approx 0.104\n\\end{align*}\\]\nrounded to $3$ decimal places."</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$0.104$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$0.133$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$0.091$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$0.146$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$0.192$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Poisson Distribution"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Given $X \\sim \\textrm{Po}(2),$ compute $P(X = 5),$ rounded to $4$ decimal places."</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"If $X \\sim \\textrm{Po}(\\lambda),$ then $X$ has the following probability mass function:\n\\[\nf(x) =\n\\dfrac{\\lambda^x e^{-\\lambda}}{x!}\n\\]\nHere, $X \\sim \\textrm{Po}(2),$ so the distribution of $X$ in this case is\n\\[\nf(x) =\n\\dfrac{2^x e^{-2}}{x!}.\n\\]\nTherefore,\n\\[\n\\begin{align*}\nP(X = 5) &amp;= f(5) \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= \\dfrac{2^5 e^{-2}}{5!} \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;\\approx 0.0361.\n\\end{align*}\n\\]"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$0.0361$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$0.0802$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$0.1115$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$0.1014$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$0.0842$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Poisson Distribution"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Given $X \\sim \\textrm{Po}(4),$ compute $P(X = 3),$ rounded to $4$ decimal places."</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"If $X \\sim \\textrm{Po}(\\lambda),$ then $X$ has the following probability mass function:\n\\[\nf(x) =\n\\dfrac{\\lambda^x e^{-\\lambda}}{x!}\n\\]\nHere, $X \\sim \\textrm{Po}(4),$ so the distribution of $X$ in this case is\n\\[\nf(x) =\n\\dfrac{4^x e^{-4}}{x!}.\n\\]\nTherefore,\n\\[\n\\begin{align*}\nP(X = 3) &amp;= f(3) \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= \\dfrac{4^3 e^{-4}}{3!} \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;\\approx 0.1954.\n\\end{align*}\n\\]"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$0.1954$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$0.2124$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$0.2506$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$0.1680$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$0.1204$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Poisson Distribution"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Given $X \\sim \\textrm{Po}(3),$ compute $P(X &lt; 2),$ rounded to $3$ decimal places."</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"If $X \\sim \\textrm{Po}(\\lambda),$ then $X$ has the following probability mass function:\n\\[\nf(x) = \\dfrac{\\lambda^x e^{-\\lambda}}{x!}\n\\]\nHere, $X \\sim \\textrm{Po}(3),$ so the probability distribution of $X$ in this case is\n\\[\nf(x) =\n\\dfrac{3^x e^{-3}}{x!}.\n\\]\nTherefore,\n\\[\n\\begin{align*}\nP(X &lt; 2) &amp;= P(X \\in \\left\\{ 0, 1 \\right\\}) \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= P(X = 0) + P(X = 1) \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= f(0) + f(1) \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= \\dfrac{3^0 e^{-3}}{0!} + \\dfrac{3^1 e^{-3}}{1!}\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;\\approx 0.199.\n\\end{align*}\n\\]"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$0.199$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$0.224$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$0.157$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$0.123$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$0.261$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"M"</span>
      }
    ],
    <span class="hljs-attr">"vectors-matrices"</span>: [
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Triangle Law for the Addition of Two Vectors"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The vectors $\\textbf{a}$ and $\\textbf{b}$ are shown in the diagram above. Which of the following could be $\\mathbf{a}+\\mathbf{b}?$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"First, we draw the vector that is equal to $\\mathbf{b}$ and has its tail placed at the head of $\\mathbf{a}.$\nNow, according to the triangle law of addition, the resultant\n\\[\n\\mathbf{a}+\\mathbf{b}\n\\]\nis the vector that goes from the tail of $\\textbf{a}$ to the head of $\\textbf{b}$, as shown below."</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">""</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">""</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">""</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">""</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">""</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"M"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Triangle Law for the Addition of Two Vectors"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Consider the diagram that shows the vector $\\mathbf{a}.$ Suppose we know that $\\mathbf{a}=\\mathbf{b}+\\mathbf{c}.$ Which of the following could be the vectors $\\mathbf{b}$ and $\\mathbf{c}?$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"First, we draw the vector that is equal to $\\mathbf{c}$ and has its tail placed at the head of $\\mathbf{b}.$\nNow, according to the triangle law of addition, the resultant\n\\[\n\\mathbf{a} = \\mathbf{b}+\\mathbf{c}\n\\]\nis the vector that goes from the tail of $\\textbf{b}$ to the head of $\\textbf{c}$, as shown below."</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">""</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">""</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">""</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">""</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">""</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"H"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Triangle Law for the Addition of Two Vectors"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Consider the vector $\\overrightarrow{CD},$ shown above. Which of the following could be $\\overrightarrow{CD}+\\overrightarrow{DC}?$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"According to the triangle law of addition, the resultant is the vector that goes from the tail of $\\overrightarrow{CD}$ to the head of $\\overrightarrow{DC}.$ Therefore, we obtain a new vector for which the tail and head is the same point, the tail of $\\overrightarrow{CD}.$\nThat vector is called the zero vector, and we write\n\\[\n\\overrightarrow{CD}+\\overrightarrow{DC} = \\mathbf{0}.\n\\]\nThe corresponding graph is shown below."</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">""</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">""</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">""</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">""</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">""</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Triangle Law for the Addition of Two Vectors"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Consider the vector $\\mathbf{c},$ shown above. Which of the following could be $\\mathbf{c}+(-\\mathbf{c})?$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"According to the triangle law of addition, the resultant is the vector that goes from the tail of $\\mathbf{c}$ to the head of $-\\mathbf{c}.$\nTherefore, we obtain a new vector for which the tail and head is the same point, the tail of $\\mathbf{c}.$\nThat vector is called the zero vector, and we write\n\\[\n\\mathbf{c}+(-\\mathbf{c}) = \\mathbf{0}.\n\\]\nThe corresponding graph is shown below."</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">""</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">""</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">""</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">""</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">""</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Triangle Law for the Addition of Two Vectors"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The vectors $\\textbf{b}$ and $\\textbf{c}$ are shown in the diagram above. Which of the following could be $\\mathbf{-b+c}.$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"We interpret $-\\mathbf{b}+\\mathbf{c}$ as the addition\n\\[\n(-\\mathbf{b})+\\mathbf{c},\n\\]\nwhere $-\\mathbf{b}$ is the vector that has the same magnitude as $\\mathbf{b}$ but goes in the direction opposite of $\\mathbf{b}.$\nFinally, we use the triangle law to add the vectors $-\\mathbf{b}$ and $\\mathbf{c}\\mathbin{:}$\nWe can start $\\mathbf{-b}$ from an arbitrary point on the plane.\nNext, we draw the vector that is equal to $\\mathbf{c}$ and has its tail placed at the head of $\\mathbf{-b}.$\nNow, according to the triangle law of addition, the resultant is the vector that goes from the tail of $-\\textbf{b}$ to the head of $\\textbf{c}$, as shown below."</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">""</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">""</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">""</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">""</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">""</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"H"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Triangle Law for the Addition of Two Vectors"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The diagram shows the vectors $\\mathbf{a}$ and $\\mathbf{b}.$ Which of the following diagrams illustrates the vector subtraction $\\mathbf{a-b}.$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"We interpret the subtraction\n$\n\\mathbf{a}-\\mathbf{b}\n$\nas the addition\n\\[\n\\mathbf{a} + (-\\mathbf{b}),\n\\]\nwhere $-\\mathbf{b}$ is the vector that has the same magnitude as $\\mathbf{b}$ but goes in the direction opposite of $\\mathbf{b}.$\nFinally, we use the triangle law to add the vectors $\\mathbf{a}$ and $-\\mathbf{b}\\mathbin{:}$\nWe can start $\\mathbf{a}$ from an arbitrary point on the plane.\nNext, we draw the vector that is equal to $\\mathbf{-b}$ and has its tail placed at the head of $\\mathbf{a}.$\nNow, according to the triangle law of addition, the resultant is the vector that goes from the tail of $\\textbf{a}$ to the head of $-\\textbf{b}$, as shown below."</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">""</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">""</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">""</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">""</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">""</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"M"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Triangle Law for the Addition of Two Vectors"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The diagram shows the vectors $\\mathbf{a}$ and $\\mathbf{b}.$ Which of the following diagrams illustrates the vector subtraction $-\\mathbf{a}+\\mathbf{b}.$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"We interpret $-\\mathbf{a}+\\mathbf{b}$ as the addition\n\\[\n(-\\mathbf{a})+\\mathbf{b},\n\\]\nwhere $-\\mathbf{a}$ is the vector that has the same magnitude as $\\mathbf{a}$ but goes in the direction opposite of $\\mathbf{a}.$\nFinally, we use the triangle law to add the vectors $-\\mathbf{a}$ and $\\mathbf{b}\\mathbin{:}$\nWe can start $\\mathbf{-a}$ from an arbitrary point on the plane.\nNext, we draw the vector that is equal to $\\mathbf{b}$ and has its tail placed at the head of $\\mathbf{-a}.$\nNow, according to the triangle law of addition, the resultant is the vector that goes from the tail of $-\\textbf{a}$ to the head of $\\textbf{b}$, as shown below."</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">""</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">""</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">""</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">""</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">""</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"H"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Triangle Law for the Addition of Two Vectors"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The vectors $\\textbf{a},$ $\\textbf{b},$ and $\\textbf{c}$ are shown in the diagram above. Which of the following diagrams shows the vector $\\mathbf{-a-b+c}.$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"First, we use the triangle law of addition to find\n\\[\n-\\mathbf{a}-\\mathbf{b} = (-\\mathbf{a}) + (-\\mathbf{b}).\n\\]\nThe resultant $-\\mathbf{a}-\\mathbf{b}$ is the vector that joins the tail of $-\\textbf{a}$ and the head of $-\\textbf{b}\\mathbin{:}$\nNow, we use the triangle law of addition to find\n\\[\n(-\\textbf{a}-\\textbf{b})+\\textbf{c}.\n\\]\nWe draw $\\textbf{c}$ from the head of $-\\textbf{a}-\\textbf{b}.$ So, we have the vector that goes from the tail of $-\\textbf{a}$ to the head of $\\textbf{c}\\mathbin{:}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">""</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">""</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">""</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">""</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">""</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"H"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Triangle Law for the Addition of Two Vectors"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The vectors $\\textbf{a},$ $\\textbf{b},$ and $\\textbf{c}$ are shown in the diagram above. Which of the following diagrams shows the vector $\\mathbf{-a-b+c}.$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"First, we use the triangle law of addition to find\n\\[\n-\\mathbf{a}-\\mathbf{b} = (-\\mathbf{a}) + (-\\mathbf{b}).\n\\]\nThe resultant $-\\mathbf{a}-\\mathbf{b}$ is the vector that joins the tail of $-\\textbf{a}$ and the head of $-\\textbf{b}\\mathbin{:}$\nNow, we use the triangle law of addition to find\n\\[\n(-\\textbf{a}-\\textbf{b})+\\textbf{c}.\n\\]\nWe draw $\\textbf{c}$ from the head of $-\\textbf{a}-\\textbf{b}.$ So, we have the vector that goes from the tail of $-\\textbf{a}$ to the head of $\\textbf{c}\\mathbin{:}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">""</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">""</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">""</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">""</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">""</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"H"</span>
      }
    ],
    <span class="hljs-attr">"exponentials"</span>: [
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Differentiating Exponential Functions"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Given that $f(x)=2e^x - \\ln 11$, find the slope of the tangent at $x=1.$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The derivative of $e^x$ is just $e^x$ itself. On the other hand, $-\\ln{11}$ is a constant, so its derivative is $0.$\nFirst, using the addition rule, we get\n\\begin{align}\nf'(x) &amp; = \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x} \\left( 2e^x-\\ln 11 \\right) \\\\[5pt]\n&amp; = \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(2e^x)+ \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(-\\ln{11}) \\\\[5pt]\n&amp; = 2\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(e^x)+0\\\\[5pt]\n&amp; = 2e^x.\n\\end{align}\nTherefore, the slope of the tangent at $x=1$ is\n\\[\n\\eqalign{\n\\left.\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}f} {\\textrm{d}x}\\right|_{x=1}\n&amp;=\\left.\\left(2e^x\\right)\\,\\right|_{x=1}\\\\[3pt]\n&amp;=2e^{1}\\\\[3pt]\n&amp;=2e.\n}\n\\]"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$2e$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$0$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$1$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$2$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$e^2$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Differentiating Exponential Functions"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Given that $f(x) =4 -3e^x-\\ln 6,$ find $f'(x).$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The derivative of $e^x$ is just $e^x$ itself. On the other hand, $4$ and $-\\ln{6}$ are constants, so their derivatives are $0.$\nUsing the addition rule, then, we have\n\\begin{eqnarray}\n\\eqalign{\nf'(x) &amp; = \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x} \\left(4-3e^x-\\ln 6\\right) \\\\[5pt]\n&amp; =\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(4)+\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(-3e^x)+ \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(-\\ln{6}) \\\\[5pt]\n&amp; =0-3\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(e^x)+0\\\\[5pt]\n&amp; = -3e^x.\n}\n\\end{eqnarray}"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$f'(x)=-3e^x$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$f'(x)=-3e^x-\\dfrac 1 6$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$f'(x)=0$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$f'(x)=4-3e^x-\\ln 6$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$f'(x)=e^x$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Differentiating Exponential Functions"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Given that $f(x)=10 - 2x^4 - 7e^x$, find the slope of the tangent at $x=-1.$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"First, using the addition and the power rules, we get\n\\begin{align}\nf'(x) &amp; = \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}( 10 - 2x^4 - 7e^x) \\\\[5pt]\n&amp; = \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(10 ) + \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(- 2x^4)+ \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(-7e^x) \\\\[5pt]\n&amp; = \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(10 ) - 2\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(x^4) - 7\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(e^x) \\\\[5pt]\n&amp; = - 8x^3 - 7e^x.\n\\end{align}\nTherefore, the slope of the tangent at $x=-1$ is\n\\[\n\\eqalign{\n\\left.\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}f} {\\textrm{d}x}\\right|_{x=-1}\n&amp;=\\left.\\left(- 8x^3 - 7e^x\\right)\\,\\right|_{x=-1}\\\\[3pt]\n&amp;=-8\\cdot (-1)^3 - 7\\cdot e^{-1}\\\\[3pt]\n&amp;=-8 \\cdot (-1) - 7\\cdot \\dfrac{1}{e}\\\\[3pt]\n&amp;=8 -\\dfrac{7}{e}.\n}\n\\]"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$8- \\dfrac{7}{e}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$-7$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$7e - 8$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$8 +e$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{7}{e}$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Differentiating Exponential Functions"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Given that $f(x)=7e^{x} + 5x^2 + e^2$, find the slope of the tangent at $x=-1.$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"First, using the addition and the power rules, we get\n\\begin{align}\nf'(x) &amp; = \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}( 7e^{x} + 5x^2 + e^2) \\\\[5pt]\n&amp; = \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(7e^{x}) + \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(5x^2)+ \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(e^2) \\\\[5pt]\n&amp; = 7\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(e^{x} ) + 5\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(x^2) +0 \\\\[5pt]\n&amp; = 7e^x +10x.\n\\end{align}\nTherefore, the slope of the tangent at $x=-1$ is\n\\[\n\\eqalign{\n\\left.\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}f} {\\textrm{d}x}\\right|_{x=-1}\n&amp;=\\left.\\left( 7e^x +10x\\right)\\,\\right|_{x=-1}\\\\[3pt]\n&amp;=7 \\cdot e^{-1} + 10\\cdot (-1) \\\\[3pt]\n&amp;=7\\cdot \\dfrac{1}{e} - 10\\\\[3pt]\n&amp;=\\dfrac{7}{e} - 10.\n}\n\\]"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{7}{e} - 10$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$7e + 10$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$7e$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{7}{e}$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{7}{e} + 5$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Differentiating Exponential Functions"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x} \\left(-4^{x} + 7e^x\\right) = $"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Applying the formula, we get\n\\begin{eqnarray}\n\\eqalign{\nf'(x)&amp;=\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}} {\\textrm{d}x} \\left(-4^{x} + 7e^x\\right)\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}} {\\textrm{d}x} \\left(-4^{x}\\right)+\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}} {\\textrm{d}x} \\left(7e^x\\right)\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}} {\\textrm{d}x} \\left(-4^{x}\\right)+7\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}} {\\textrm{d}x} \\left(e^x\\right)\\\\[5pt]\n&amp; = -4^x \\cdot \\ln 4 + 7e^x \\\\[5pt]\n&amp; = -4^x\\ \\ln 4 + 7e^x.\n}\n\\end{eqnarray}"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$-4^x\\ \\ln 4 + 7e^x$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$3e^x$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$-4^x + e^x$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$6\\ln 4\\cdot 4^x$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$-4^x + 7e^x$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
      },
      {
        <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Differentiating Exponential Functions"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Given that $f(x)=3\\cdot 2^x-3x^4-4$, find the instantaneous rate of change at $x=0.$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Applying the formula, we get\n\\begin{align*}\nf'(x)&amp;= \\dfrac{\\textrm d}{\\textrm d x}\\left(3\\cdot 2^x-3x^4-4\\right)\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= \\dfrac{\\textrm d}{\\textrm d x}\\left(3\\cdot 2^x\\right)-\\dfrac{\\textrm d}{\\textrm d x}\\left(3x^4\\right)-\\dfrac{\\textrm d}{\\textrm d x}\\left(4\\right)\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= 3\\dfrac{\\textrm d}{\\textrm d x}\\left(2^x\\right)-3\\dfrac{\\textrm d}{\\textrm d x}\\left(x^4\\right)-\\dfrac{\\textrm d}{\\textrm d x}\\left(4\\right)\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=3 \\ln 2 \\cdot 2^{x} - 3 \\cdot 4x^3-0\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=3 \\ln 2 \\cdot 2^{x}-12x^3.\n\\end{align*}\nTherefore, the slope at $x=0$ is\n\\begin{align*}\n\\left.\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}f} {\\textrm{d}x}\\right|_{x=0}\n&amp;=\\left.\\left( 3 \\ln 2 \\cdot 2^{x}-12x^3\\right)\\right|_{x=0}\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=3 \\ln 2 \\cdot 2^{0}-12(0)^3\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=3 \\ln 2.\n\\end{align*}"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
          <span class="hljs-string">"$3 \\ln 2$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$6 \\ln 2$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$3 \\ln 2 - 4$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$3 \\ln 2 - 12$"</span>,
          <span class="hljs-string">"$\\ln 6$"</span>
        ],
        <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
        <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"M"</span>
      }
    ]
  },
  <span class="hljs-attr">"all_questions"</span>: [
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Product Rule for Logarithms"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Express $\\log_{4}\\big(k^2 t\\big) + \\log_{4}\\big(t^2\\big) + \\log_4 k$ as a single logarithm."</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"First, let's recall the product rule for logarithms:\n\\[\n\\log_n a + \\log_n b = \\log_n(ab)\n\\]\nUsing the product rule for logarithms, we can combine the first two terms as follows:\n\\begin{align}\n\\log_{4}\\big(k^2 t\\big) + \\log_{4}\\big(t^2\\big) + \\log_4 k\n&amp; = \\bigl( \\log_{4}\\big(k^2 t\\big) + \\log_{4}\\big(t^2\\big) \\bigr) + \\log_4 k \\\\[5pt]\n&amp; = \\log_{4}\\big(k^2 t t^2\\big) + \\log_4 k\\\\[5pt]\n\\end{align}\nUsing the product rule for logarithms once more, we obtain\n\\[\n\\log_{4}\\big(k^2 t t^2\\big) + \\log_4 k = \\log_{4}\\big(k^2 t t^2 k\\big).\n\\]\nFinally, if we use the commutative property of real numbers and the addition law for exponents, we get\n\\begin{align}\n\\log_{4}\\big(k^2 t t^2 k \\big) &amp; = \\log_{4}\\big(k^2 k t t^2\\big)\\\\[5pt]\n&amp; = \\log_{4}\\big(k^{2+1} t^{1+2}\\big)\\\\[5pt]\n&amp; = \\log_{4}\\big(k^3 t^3\\big).\n\\end{align}\nTherefore,\n\\[\n\\log_{4}(k^2 t) + \\log_{4}(t^2) + \\log_4(k) = \\log_{4}\\big(k^3 t^3\\big).\n\\]"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\log_{4}\\left(k^3 t^3\\right)$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\log_{12}\\left(k^2 t + t^2 + k\\right)$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\log_{4}\\left(k^2 t + t^2 + k\\right)$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\log_{12}\\left(k^2 t^2\\right)$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\log_{4}\\left(k^2 t^2\\right)$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"H"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Product Rule for Logarithms"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Express $\\log_{3}\\big(p^3\\big) + \\log_{3}\\big(q^2\\big) + \\log_3(pq)$ as a single logarithm."</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"First, let's recall the product rule for logarithms:\n\\[\n\\log_n a + \\log_n b = \\log_n(ab)\n\\]\nUsing the product rule for logarithms, we can combine the first two terms as follows:\n\\begin{align}\n\\log_{3}\\big(p^3\\big) + \\log_{3}\\big(q^2\\big) + \\log_3(pq)\n&amp; = \\bigl( \\log_{3}\\big(p^3\\big) + \\log_{3}\\big(q^2\\big) \\bigr) + \\log_3(pq)\\\\[5pt]\n&amp; = \\log_{3}\\big(p^3 q^2\\big) + \\log_3(pq)\\\\[5pt]\n\\end{align}\nUsing the product rule for logarithms once more, we obtain\n\\[\n\\log_{3}\\big(p^3 q^2\\big) + \\log_3(pq)= \\log_{3}\\big(p^3 q^2 pq\\big).\n\\]\nFinally, if we use the commutative property of real numbers and the addition law for exponents, we get\n\\begin{align}\n\\log_{3} \\big(p^3 q^2 pq\\big)\n&amp; = \\log_{3} \\big(p^3p q^2 q \\big)\\\\[5pt]\n&amp; = \\log_{3} \\big(p^{3+1} q^{2+1} \\big)\\\\[5pt]\n&amp; = \\log_{3}\\big(p^4 q^3\\big).\n\\end{align}\nTherefore,\n\\[\n\\log_{3}\\big(p^3\\big) + \\log_{3}\\big(q^2\\big) + \\log_3(pq) = \\log_{3}\\big(p^4 q^3\\big).\n\\]"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\log_{3}\\big(p^4 q^3\\big)$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\log_{27}\\big(p^4 q^3\\big)$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\log_{9}\\big(p^3+q^2+pq\\big)$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\log_{3}\\big(p^3 q^2\\big)$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\log_{3}\\big(p^3+q^2+pq \\big)$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"H"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Product Rule for Logarithms"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Express $\\log(x+2) + \\log(5x)$ as a single logarithm."</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"First, let's recall the product rule for logarithms:\n\\[\n\\log_n a + \\log_n b = \\log_n(ab)\n\\]\nUsing the product rule for logarithms, the given expression can be rewritten as\n\\begin{align}\n\\log(x+2) + \\log(5x) &amp; = \\log\\big( (x+2)\\cdot 5x \\big)\\\\[5pt]\n&amp; = \\log \\left(5x^2 + 10x \\right).\n\\end{align}"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\log \\left(5x^2 + 10x \\right)$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\log \\left(5x^2 + 10 \\right)$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\log \\left(6x + 2 \\right)$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\log \\left(7x+2 \\right)$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\log \\left(5x^2 + 5x \\right)$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"M"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Product Rule for Logarithms"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Express $4\\log 2+4\\log 4 + 4\\log 8$ as a single logarithm."</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"First, let's recall the product rule for logarithms:\n\\[\n\\log_n a + \\log_n b = \\log_n(ab)\n\\]\nIf we factor the given expression and use the product rule for logarithms, we get\n\\begin{align}\n4\\log 2+4\\log 4+4 \\log 8\n&amp;= 4 \\left(\\log 2+4\\log 4 + \\log 8\\right) \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= 4\\log (2 \\cdot 4 \\cdot 8) \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= 4\\log 64.\n\\end{align}\nTherefore,\n\\[\n4\\log 2+4\\log 4 + 4\\log 8 = 4\\log 64.\n\\]"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"$4\\log 64$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$4\\log 14$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\log 64$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$12\\log 14$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$64\\log 64$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"M"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Product Rule for Logarithms"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"$\\log_2(8 p) = $"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"First, let's recall the product rule for logarithms:\n\\[\n\\log_n a + \\log_n b = \\log_n(ab)\n\\]\nUsing the product rule, the given expression can be rewritten as\n\\begin{align}\n\\log_2(8 p) = \\log_2 8 + \\log_2 p.\n\\end{align}\nSince $8 = 2^3,$ we have\n\\[\n\\log_2 8 = \\log_2\\left( 2^3 \\right) = 3.\n\\]\nTherefore,\n\\begin{align}\n\\log_2(8 p) &amp; = \\log_2 8 + \\log_2 p\\\\[5pt]\n&amp; = 3 + \\log_2 p.\n\\end{align}"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"$3 + \\log_2 p$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$8 + \\log_2 p$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$3\\log_2 p$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$1 + \\log_2(2p)$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$3 \\log_2(4p)$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"H"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Product Rule for Logarithms"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"$\\log_8(3z)=$"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"First, let's recall the product rule for logarithms:\n\\[\n\\log_n a + \\log_n b = \\log_n(ab)\n\\]\nUsing the product rule, the given expression can be rewritten as\n\\begin{align}\n\\log_8 (3z )\n&amp; = \\log_8 (3 \\cdot z )\\\\[5pt]\n&amp; = \\log_8 3 + \\log_8 z.\n\\end{align}"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\log_8 3+\\log_8 z$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\log_8 3\\cdot\\log_8 z$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\log_8 3-\\log_8 z$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{\\log_8 3}{\\log_8 z}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$3\\log_8 z$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Product Rule for Logarithms"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"If $\\log 16 = x$ and $\\log 4=y$, then $2\\log 64=$"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"First, let's recall the product rule for logarithms:\n\\[\n\\log_n a + \\log_n b= \\log_n(ab)\n\\]\nUsing the product rule for logarithms and the information given, we get\n\\begin{align}\n2\\log 64 &amp; = 2\\log(16\\cdot 4)\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=2\\bigl( \\log 16 + \\log 4 \\bigr) \\\\[5pt]\n&amp; = 2(x + y).\n\\end{align}"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"$2(x+y)$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$2xy$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$4(x+y)$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x^2 + y^2$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x+y+2xy$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"H"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Product Rule for Logarithms"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"If $\\ln x =10$ and $\\ln y = 2$, then $\\ln (xy)=$"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"First, let's recall the product rule for logarithms:\n\\[\n\\log_n a + \\log_n b= \\log_n(ab)\n\\]\nUsing the product rule, we have\n\\begin{align}\n\\ln (xy) &amp; = \\ln x + \\ln y\\\\[5pt]\n&amp; = 10 + 2\\\\[5pt]\n&amp; =12.\n\\end{align}"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"$12$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$20$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$10x+2y$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$20xy$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x^{10}y^2$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"M"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Solving Logarithmic Equations Containing the Natural Logarithm"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"If $\\ln \\left( {3-7x} \\right) = 6$, then $x=$"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"To remove the logarithm, we exponentiate both sides of the equation. The logarithm is a natural logarithm, which means its base is $e.$ So, we use a base of $e$ for the exponentiation as well:\n\\begin{align*}\n\\ln \\left( {3-7x} \\right) &amp;= 6 \\\\[3pt]\ne^{\\color{blue}\\ln \\left( {3-7x} \\right)} &amp;=e^{\\color{blue}6}\n\\end{align*}\nSince exponentiation is the inverse function of the logarithm, we get $e^{\\ln(3-7x)} = 3-7x.$\nTherefore,\n\\begin{align*}\n3-7x &amp; = e^6 \\\\[3pt]\n- 7x &amp;= e^6 - 3 \\\\[3pt]\nx &amp;= \\dfrac{e^6 - 3}{ - 7} \\\\[3pt]\nx &amp;= \\dfrac{3 -e^6}{7}.\n\\end{align*}"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{3 -e^6}{7}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{3 +e^6}{7}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{3 -e^7}{6}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{6 -e^6}{7}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{6+e^7}{7}$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"M"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Solving Logarithmic Equations Containing the Natural Logarithm"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Find the exact solution to $\n\\ln \\left( {4x} \\right) = 2\n.$"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"To remove the logarithm, we exponentiate both sides of the equation. The logarithm is a natural logarithm, which means its base is $e.$ So, we use a base of $e$ for the exponentiation as well:\n\\begin{align*}\n\\ln\\left( {4x} \\right) &amp;= 2 \\\\[3pt]\ne^{\\color{blue}\\ln\\left( {4x} \\right)} &amp;=e^{\\color{blue}2}\n\\end{align*}\nSince exponentiation is the inverse function of the logarithm, we get $e^{\\ln (4x)} = 4x.$\nTherefore,\n\\begin{align*}\n4x &amp;= e^{2} \\\\[5pt]\nx &amp;= \\dfrac{1}{4}e^{2}\\\\[5pt]\nx &amp;= \\dfrac{e^{2}}{4}.\n\\end{align*}"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x = \\dfrac{e^2}{4}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x =\\dfrac{2-e^{2}}{4}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x = \\dfrac{{4 - e}}{2}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x = \\dfrac{{ {e^4}}}{2}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x =\\dfrac{1}{2}$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Solving Logarithmic Equations Containing the Natural Logarithm"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Find the exact solution to $5\\ln (7-2x) +13 = 38.$"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"First, we rearrange our equation to isolate the logarithmic term, as follows:\n\\begin{align*}\n5\\ln (7-2x) +13 &amp;= 38 \\\\[3pt]\n5\\ln (7-2x) &amp;= 25 \\\\[3pt]\n\\ln (7-2x) &amp;= 5\n\\end{align*}\nTo remove the logarithm, we exponentiate both sides of the equation. The logarithm is a natural logarithm, which means its base is $e.$ So, we use a base of $e$ for the exponentiation as well:\n\\begin{align*}\n\\ln{(7-2x)} &amp;= 5 \\\\[3pt]\ne^{\\color{blue}\\ln{(7-2x)}} &amp;=e^{\\color{blue}5}\n\\end{align*}\nSince exponentiation is the inverse function of the logarithm, we get $e^{\\ln (7-2x)} = 7-2x.$\nTherefore,\n\\begin{align*}\n7-2x &amp;= e^5 \\\\[5pt]\n-2x &amp;= e^5-7 \\\\[3pt]\nx &amp;= \\dfrac{e^5-7}{-2} \\\\[3pt]\nx &amp;= \\dfrac{7-e^5}{2}.\n\\end{align*}"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{7-e^5}{2}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{7-e^2}{2}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{7+e^5}{5}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{7-e^2}{5}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{2+e^5}{7}$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"M"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Solving Logarithmic Equations Containing the Natural Logarithm"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Find the exact solution to $2\\ln (1-5x) +3 = 9.$"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"First, we rearrange our equation to isolate the logarithmic term, as follows:\n\\begin{align*}\n2\\ln (1-5x) +3 &amp;= 9 \\\\[3pt]\n2\\ln (1-5x) &amp;= 6 \\\\[3pt]\n\\ln (1-5x) &amp;= 3\n\\end{align*}\nTo remove the logarithm, we exponentiate both sides of the equation. The logarithm is a natural logarithm, which means its base is $e.$ So, we use a base of $e$ for the exponentiation as well:\n\\begin{align*}\n\\ln{(1-5x)} &amp;= 3 \\\\[3pt]\ne^{\\color{blue}\\ln{(1-5x)}} &amp;=e^{\\color{blue}3}\n\\end{align*}\nSince exponentiation is the inverse function of the logarithm, we get $e^{\\ln (1-5x)} = 1-5x.$\nTherefore,\n\\begin{align*}\n1-5x &amp;= e^3 \\\\[5pt]\n-5x &amp;= e^3-1 \\\\[3pt]\nx &amp;= \\dfrac{e^3-1}{-5} \\\\[3pt]\nx &amp;= \\dfrac{1-e^3}{5}.\n\\end{align*}"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{1-e^3}{5}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{e^3+1}{5}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{1+e^3}{3}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{2-e^3}{3}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{3+e^3}{5}$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"M"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Solving Logarithmic Equations Containing the Natural Logarithm"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Find the values of $x$ such that $\\ln(x-1) - 3\\ln(x-1) = 2.$"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"First, we simplify our equation by collecting the like logarithmic terms, and isolating the logarithm, as follows:\n\\begin{align*}\n\\ln(x-1) - 3\\ln(x-1) &amp;= 2 \\\\[5pt]\n(1-3)\\ln(x-1) &amp;=2 \\\\[5pt]\n-2\\ln(x-1) &amp;= 2 \\\\[5pt]\n\\ln(x-1) &amp;= -1\n\\end{align*}\nTo remove the logarithm, we exponentiate both sides of the equation. The logarithm is a natural logarithm, which means its base is $e.$ So, we use a base of $e$ for the exponentiation as well:\n\\begin{align*}\n\\ln(x-1) &amp;= -1 \\\\[5pt]\ne^{\\color{blue}\\ln(x-1)} &amp;= e^{\\color{blue}-1}\n\\end{align*}\nSince exponentiation is the inverse function of the logarithm, we get $e^{\\ln (x-1)} = x-1.$\nTherefore,\n\\begin{align*}\nx-1 &amp;= e^{-1} \\\\[5pt]\nx-1 &amp;= \\dfrac{1}{e} \\\\[5pt]\nx &amp; = \\dfrac{1}{e} + 1.\n\\end{align*}"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"$ \\dfrac{1}{e} + 1$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$e + 1$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$ e^4 + 1$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$ \\dfrac{1}{e^4} + 1$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\sqrt{e}+1$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"H"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Solving Logarithmic Equations Containing the Natural Logarithm"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"If $2\\ln{x} + 3\\ln{x}=20$, then what is the value of $x?$"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"First, we simplify our equation by collecting the like logarithmic terms, and isolating the logarithm, as follows:\n\\begin{align*}\n2\\ln{x} + 3\\ln{x} &amp;= 20 \\\\[5pt]\n(2+3)\\ln x &amp;= 20 \\\\[5pt]\n5\\ln x &amp;= 20 \\\\[5pt]\n\\ln x &amp;= \\dfrac{20}{5}\\\\[5pt]\n\\ln x &amp;=4.\n\\end{align*}\nTo remove the logarithm, we exponentiate both sides of the equation. The logarithm is a natural logarithm, which means its base is $e.$ So, we use a base of $e$ for the exponentiation as well:\n\\begin{align*}\n\\ln x &amp;= 4 \\\\[5pt]\ne^{\\color{blue}\\ln x} &amp;= e^{\\color{blue}4}\n\\end{align*}\nSince exponentiation is the inverse function of the logarithm, we get $e^{\\ln x} = x.$\nTherefore, $ x = e^{4}. $"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"$e^4$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{e^{2}}{4}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\sqrt e$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\sqrt[3]e$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{e^{20}}{5}$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"M"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Average of the Roots Formula"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Find the axis of symmetry for the parabola sketched above."</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"From the graph, we deduce that the roots are $x_1=-5$ and $x_2=1.$\nTo find the axis of symmetry, we take the average of the roots, as follows:\n\\begin{align*}\nx &amp;= \\dfrac{x_1+x_2}{2}\n= \\dfrac{(-5)+1}{2}\n= \\dfrac{-4}{2}\n=-2.\n\\end{align*}\nTherefore, the axis of symmetry is $x=-2.$"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x=-2$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x=2$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x=1$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x=-5$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x=-1$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Average of the Roots Formula"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Find the axis of symmetry for the parabola sketched above."</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"From the graph, we deduce that the roots are $x_1=-3$ and $x_2=1.$\nTo find the axis of symmetry, we take the average of the roots, as follows:\n\\begin{align*}\nx &amp;= \\dfrac{x_1+x_2}{2}\n= \\dfrac{(-3)+1}{2}\n= \\dfrac{-2}{2}.\n\\end{align*}\nTherefore, the axis of symmetry is $x= -1.$"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x=-1$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x=1$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x=-2$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x=-1.5$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x=0$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Average of the Roots Formula"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Find the axis of symmetry for the parabola $y=(x-2)(x+6).$"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"From the equation $y=(x-2)(x+6),$ we find that the roots of the parabola are $x_1=2$ and $x_2=-6.$\nTo find the axis of symmetry, we take the average of the roots, as follows:\n\\begin{align*}\nx &amp;= \\dfrac{x_1+x_2}{2}\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= \\dfrac{2+(-6)}{2} \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= -2.\n\\end{align*}\nTherefore, the axis of symmetry is $x=-2.$"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x=-2$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x=-4$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x=-3$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x=-\\dfrac32$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x=-\\dfrac52$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Average of the Roots Formula"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Find the axis of symmetry of the parabola $y= (2x-1)\\left(4x+7\\right).$"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"From the equation $y= (2x-1)\\left(4x+7\\right),$ we find that the roots of the parabola are $x_1=\\dfrac{1}{2}$ and $x_2=-\\dfrac{7}{4}.$\nTo find the axis of symmetry, we take the average of the roots, as follows:\n\\begin{align*}\nx\n&amp;= \\dfrac{x_1+x_2}{2}\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= \\dfrac{\\left(\\dfrac12 - \\dfrac74\\right)}{2}\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= \\dfrac{\\left(\\dfrac{2}{4} - \\dfrac74\\right)}{2}\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= \\dfrac{\\left( -\\dfrac54\\right)}{2}\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=-\\dfrac{5}{8}\n\\end{align*}\nTherefore, the axis of symmetry is $x=-\\dfrac{5}{8}.$"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x=-\\dfrac{5}{8}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x=-\\dfrac{3}{8}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x=-\\dfrac{7}{8}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x=\\dfrac{3}{8}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x=\\dfrac{5}{4}$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"M"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The Average of the Roots Formula"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The axis of symmetry of $y=\\dfrac 34(x+4k)(x+3k)$ is $x=1.$ The value of $k=$"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"First, we calculate the axis of symmetry in terms of $k.$ Then, since we know that the axis of symmetry is $x=1,$ we will set our result equal to $1.$\nFrom the equation $y=\\dfrac 34(x+4k)(x+3k),$ we find that the roots of the parabola are $x_1=-4k$ and $x_2=-3k.$\nTo find the axis of symmetry, we take the average of the roots, as follows:\n\\begin{align*}\nx\n&amp;= \\dfrac{x_1+x_2}{2}\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= \\dfrac{(-4k) + (-3k)}{2}\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= \\dfrac{-7k}{2}\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=-\\dfrac{7k}{2}.\n\\end{align*}\nWe know that the axis of symmetry is $x=1.$ Therefore, we have\n\\begin{align*}\n-\\dfrac{7k}{2} &amp;= 1 \\\\[5pt]\nk &amp;=-\\dfrac{2}{7}.\n\\end{align*}"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"$-\\dfrac{2}{7}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$-\\dfrac{1}{7}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$-\\dfrac{3}{7}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{3}{7}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{5}{7}$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"M"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Equations of Circles Centered at the Origin"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"What is the center and radius of the circle with the equation $x^2 + y^2 = 256?$"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The general equation of a circle with radius $r$ centered at the origin is\n\\[\nx^2 + y^2 = r^2.\n\\]\nSo, the equation $x^2 + y^2 = 256$ represents a circle that is centered at the origin, with radius\n\\begin{align*}\nr^2 &amp; = 256 \\\\[5pt]\nr &amp; = \\sqrt{256} \\\\[5pt]\nr &amp; = 16.\n\\end{align*}\nNote that we disregarded the negative solution for the radius since the radius of a circle must be positive.\nTherefore, the circle is centered at $(0,0)$ and its radius is $16.$"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"The center is $(0,0)$ and the radius is $16$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"The center is $(0,0)$ and the radius is $4$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"The center is $(0,0)$ and the radius is $256$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"The center is $(0,0)$ and the radius is $512$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"The center is $(0,0)$ and the radius is $32$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"M"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Equations of Circles Centered at the Origin"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"What is the center and the radius of the circle with equation $x^2 + y^2 =64 ?$"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The general equation of a circle with radius $r$ centered at the origin is\n\\[\nx^2 + y^2 = r^2.\n\\]\nSo, the equation $x^2 + y^2 = 64$ represents a circle that is centered at the origin, with radius\n\\begin{align*}\nr^2 &amp; = 64 \\\\[5pt]\nr &amp;= \\sqrt{64}\\\\[5pt]\nr &amp;= 8.\n\\end{align*}\nNote that we disregarded the negative solution for the radius since the radius of a circle must be positive.\nTherefore, the circle is centered at $(0,0)$ and its radius is $8.$"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"The center is $(0,0)$ and the radius is $8$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"The center is $(0,0)$ and the radius is $\\sqrt{8}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"The center is $(0,0)$ and the radius is $64$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"The center is $(1,1)$ and the radius is $\\sqrt{8}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"The center is $(1,1)$ and the radius is $8$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Equations of Circles Centered at the Origin"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"What is the equation of the circle given above?"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The general equation of a circle with radius $r$ centered at the origin is\n\\[\nx^2 + y^2 = r^2.\n\\]\nFrom the picture, we see that our circle is centered at $(0,0)$ and has radius $r = 13.$ So, the equation is\n\\begin{align*}\nx^2 + y^2 &amp; = r^2 \\\\[5pt]\nx^2 + y^2 &amp; = 13^2 \\\\[5pt]\nx^2 + y^2 &amp;= 169.\n\\end{align*}"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"$ x^2 + y^2 =169$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$ x^2 + y^2 =\\sqrt{13}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$ x^2 + y^2 = 13$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$ \\left(x-13\\right)^2 + \\left(y-13\\right)^2 = \\sqrt{13}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$ \\left(x-\\sqrt{13}\\right)^2 + \\left(y-\\sqrt{13}\\right)^2 = 13$"</span>
      ],
