Describe it in terms of $a_{ij}.$\n\\[\nA =\n\\begin{bmatrix}\n3 &amp; 5 &amp; 10 &amp; 6 \\\\\n6 &amp; 14 &amp; 8 &amp; 1\\\\\n9 &amp; 17 &amp; 9 &amp; {\\bbox[lightgray, 2pt]{0}}\n\\end{bmatrix}\n\\]"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The entry lies at the intersection of the $3$rd row and $4$th column. Hence, we have $a_{34}={\\bbox[lightgray, 2pt]{0}}.$"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"$a_{34}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$a_{21}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$a_{43}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$a_{13}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$a_{22}$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Equations of Circles Centered at the Origin"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"What is the center and the radius of the circle with equation $x^2 + y^2 = 6 ?$"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The general equation of a circle with radius $r$ centered at the origin is\n\\[\nx^2 + y^2 = r^2.\n\\]\nSo, the equation $x^2 + y^2 =6$ represents a circle that is centered at the origin, with radius\n\\begin{align*}\nr^2 &amp;= 6 \\\\[5pt]\nr &amp;= \\sqrt{6} .\n\\end{align*}\nNote that we disregarded the negative solution for the radius since the radius of a circle must be positive.\nTherefore, the circle is centered at $(0,0)$ and its radius is $\\sqrt{6}.$"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"The center is $(0,0)$ and the radius is $\\sqrt{6}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"The center is $(0,0)$ and the radius is $6$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"The center is $(0,0)$ and the radius is $36$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"The center is $(6,6)$ and the radius is $\\sqrt{6}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"The center is $(6,6)$ and the radius is $6$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"H"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Equations of Circles Centered at the Origin"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"What is the center and radius of the circle with equation $x^2 + y^2 = 7?$"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The general equation of a circle with radius $r$ centered at the origin is\n\\[\nx^2 + y^2 = r^2.\n\\]\nSo, the equation $x^2 + y^2 = 7$ represents a circle that is centered at the origin, with radius\n\\begin{align*}\nr^2 &amp; = 7 \\\\[5pt]\nr &amp; = \\sqrt{7} .\n\\end{align*}\nNote that we disregarded the negative solution for the radius since the radius of a circle must be positive.\nTherefore, the circle is centered at $(0,0)$ and its radius is $\\sqrt{7}.$"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"The center is $(0,0)$ and the radius is $\\sqrt{7}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"The center is $(0,0)$ and the radius is $14$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"The center is $(0,0)$ and the radius is $7$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"The center is $(7,7)$ and the radius is $1$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"The center is $(7,7)$ and the radius is $\\sqrt{7}$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"H"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Equations of Circles Centered at the Origin"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"What is the equation of the circle given above?"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The general equation of a circle with radius $r$ centered at the origin is\n\\[\nx^2 + y^2 = r^2.\n\\]\nFrom the picture, we see that our circle is centered at $(0,0)$ and has radius $r = \\sqrt{2}.$ So, the equation is\n\\begin{align*}\nx^2 + y^2 &amp; = r^2 \\\\[5pt]\nx^2 + y^2 &amp; = \\left(\\sqrt{2}\\right)^2 \\\\[5pt]\nx^2 + y^2 &amp;= 2.\n\\end{align*}"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x^2 + y^2 = 2$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x^2 + y^2 = \\sqrt{2}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x^2 + y^2 = 4$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\left(x- \\sqrt{2}\\right)^2 + \\left(y- \\sqrt{2}\\right)^2 =2$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\left(x- 2\\right)^2 + \\left(y-2\\right)^2 =2$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Equations of Circles Centered at the Origin"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"What is the equation of the circle given above?"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The general equation of a circle with radius $r$ centered at the origin is\n\\[\nx^2 + y^2 = r^2.\n\\]\nFrom the picture, we see that our circle is centered at $(0,0)$ and has radius $r = 9.$ So, the equation is\n\\begin{align*}\nx^2 + y^2 &amp; = r^2 \\\\[5pt]\nx^2 + y^2 &amp; = 9^2 \\\\[5pt]\nx^2 + y^2 &amp; = 81.\n\\end{align*}"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x^2 + y^2 = 81$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x^2 + y^2 = 9$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x^2 + y^2 = 3$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x^2 + y^2 = 18$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$(x-9)^2 + (y-9)^2 = 1$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"M"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Equations of Circles Centered at the Origin"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Which equation describes the set of all points $(x,y)$ in the coordinate plane that are a distance of $13$ units from the origin?"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The set of all points $(x,y)$ that are a distance of $13$ units from the origin defines a circle with radius $r = 13$ centered at the origin, as shown below.\nSo, the required equation is\n\\begin{align*}\nx^2 + y^2 &amp;= r^2 \\\\[5pt]\nx^2 + y^2 &amp;= 13^2 \\\\[5pt]\nx^2 + y^2 &amp;= 169.\n\\end{align*}"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x^2 + y^2 = 169$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x^2 + y^2 +169 = 0$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x^2 + y^2 = 13$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x^2 + y^2 = \\sqrt{13}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x^2 + y^2 +13 = 0$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"M"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Equations of Circles Centered at the Origin"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Which equation describes the set of all points $(x,y)$ in the coordinate plane that are a distance of $15$ units from the origin?"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The set of all points $(x,y)$ that are a distance of $15$ units from the origin defines a circle with radius $r = 15$ centered at the origin, as shown below.\nSo, the required equation is\n\\begin{align*}\nx^2 + y^2 &amp;= r^2 \\\\[5pt]\nx^2 + y^2 &amp;= 15^2 \\\\[5pt]\nx^2 + y^2 &amp;= 225.\n\\end{align*}"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x^2 + y^2 = 225$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x^2 + y^2 + 225 =0$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x^2 + y^2 = 15$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x^2 + y^2 = \\sqrt{15}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x^2 + y^2 +15 = 0$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"M"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Solving Elementary Quadratic Inequalities"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Solve the inequality $x^2 \\gt 16$ using a graphical method."</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Bringing all the terms to the left-hand side, we have\n\\[\n\\eqalign{\nx^2 &amp; \\gt 16 \\\\[3pt]\nx^2-16 &amp; \\gt 0.\n}\n\\]\nNow, we consider the corresponding parabola given by $\\, y = x^2-16 \\, $ and find its roots:\n\\[\n\\eqalign{\nx^2-16 &amp;= 0\\\\[3pt]\nx^2-4^2 &amp;= 0\\\\[3pt]\n(x-4)(x+4) &amp;= 0\n}\n\\]\nSo the roots are $x= \\pm 4.$\nSolving the inequality $x^2-16 \\gt 0$ is equivalent to finding the values of $x$ for which the parabola $y=x^2-16$ is above the $x$-axis (where ${\\color{red}y \\gt 0}$).\nSince the leading coefficient of the quadratic expression is positive, the parabola opens upward. Therefore, the solution set lies outside the roots (but it does not include them), as shown below.\nTherefore, the solution is $x \\in \\color{blue}(-\\infty, -4) \\cup (4, \\infty).$"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x \\in (-\\infty, -4) \\cup (4, \\infty)$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x \\in (-\\infty, -8) \\cup (8, \\infty)$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x \\in (-\\infty,\\infty)$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x \\in (-\\infty, -4) \\cup (8, \\infty)$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x \\in (16, \\infty)$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Solving Elementary Quadratic Inequalities"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Solve the inequality $x^2 \\geq 4$ using a graphical method."</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Bringing all the terms to the left-hand side, we have\n\\[\n\\eqalign{\nx^2 &amp;\\geq 4 \\\\\nx^2-4 &amp;\\geq 0.}\n\\]\nNow, we consider the corresponding parabola given by $\\, y = x^2-4 \\, $ and find its roots:\n\\[\n\\eqalign{\nx^2-4 &amp;= 0\\\\\nx^2-2^2 &amp;= 0\\\\\n(x-2)(x+2) &amp;= 0\n}\n\\]\nSo the roots are $x= \\pm 2.$\nSolving the inequality $x^2-4 \\geq 0$ is equivalent to finding the values of $x$ for which the parabola $y=x^2-4$ is above or intersects the $x$-axis (where ${\\color{red}y \\geq 0}$).\nSince the leading coefficient of the quadratic expression is positive, the parabola opens upward. Therefore, the solution set lies outside the roots (including them), as shown below.\nTherefore, the solution is $x \\in \\color{blue}(-\\infty, -2] \\cup [2, \\infty).$"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x \\in (-\\infty,-2] \\cup [2,\\infty)$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x \\in [-2, 2]$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x \\in (-\\infty,-4] \\cup [4,\\infty)$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x \\in [-4,4]$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x \\in [-2,\\infty)$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Solving Elementary Quadratic Inequalities"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Solve the inequality $x^2-15 \\lt 5$ using the absolute value method."</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Bringing the constant term to the right-hand side, we have\n\\begin{align*}\nx^2 -15&amp; \\lt 5\\\\[3pt]\nx^2 &amp; \\lt 20.\n\\end{align*}\nThen, taking the square root of both sides of the inequality, we get\n\\[\n\\eqalign{\nx^2 &amp;\\lt 20\\\\[3pt]\n\\sqrt{x^2} &amp;\\lt \\sqrt{20}\\\\[3pt]\n|x| &amp; \\lt 2\\sqrt{5}.\n}\n\\]\nNow, using the definition of absolute value, we must have\n\\[\n-2\\sqrt{5} \\lt x \\lt 2\\sqrt{5}.\n\\]\nThe solution is $x \\in (-2\\sqrt 5,2\\sqrt 5).$"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x \\in (-2\\sqrt 5,2\\sqrt 5)$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x \\in (-2\\sqrt 5,\\infty)$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x \\in (-\\infty,2\\sqrt 5)$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x \\in (-\\infty, \\infty)$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x \\in (-\\infty, -2\\sqrt 5) \\cup (2\\sqrt 5, \\infty)$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"M"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Solving Elementary Quadratic Inequalities"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Solve the inequality $x^2-1&lt; -\\sqrt{2}\\,$ using the absolute value method."</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Bringing the constant $-1$ to the right-hand side, we have\n\\begin{align*}\nx^2-1&amp;&lt; -\\sqrt{2} \\\\\nx^2 &amp;&lt; 1-\\sqrt{2}.\n\\end{align*}\nNotice that $1-\\sqrt{2}&lt; 0$ and that $x^2$, being a square, must be a positive number or zero.\nHence, $x^2$ cannot be less than $1-\\sqrt{2}.$\nTherefore, there is no solution."</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"There is no solution"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$-2 &lt; x &lt; \\sqrt{2}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$-\\sqrt{2} &lt; x &lt; 1$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$-\\sqrt{2} &lt; x &lt; \\sqrt{2}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x &lt; \\sqrt{2}$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"M"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Solving Elementary Quadratic Inequalities"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Solve $6x^2&lt; 9.$"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"First, let's simplify the inequality:\n\\begin{align*}\n6x^2 &amp;&lt; 9 \\\\\nx^2 &amp;&lt; \\dfrac{9}{6}\\\\\nx^2 &amp;&lt; \\dfrac{3}{2}\\\\\n\\end{align*}\nNow, we can use either of two methods to solve the inequality.\nMethod 1 - Solving an Absolute Value Equation\nTaking the square root of both sides of the inequality, we get\n\\[\n\\eqalign{\n\\sqrt{x^2} &amp;&lt; \\sqrt{\\dfrac{3}{2}}\\\\\n|x|&amp;&lt; \\sqrt{\\dfrac{3}{2}}\n.}\n\\]\nNow, using the definition of absolute value, we must have $-\\sqrt{\\dfrac{3}{2}}&lt; x&lt; \\sqrt{\\dfrac{3}{2}}.$\nSo, the solution is $x\\in \\left(-\\sqrt{\\dfrac{3}{2}},\\sqrt{\\dfrac{3}{2}}\\right).$\nMethod 2 - Picturing a Quadratic Curve\nStarting from $x^2 &lt; \\dfrac{3}{2}$ and moving all terms to the left-hand side, we reach\n\\[\nx^2-\\dfrac 32 &lt; 0 .\n\\]\nNow, we consider the corresponding parabola given by $\\, y = x^2-\\dfrac{3}{2} \\, $ and find its roots:\n\\[\n\\eqalign{\nx^2-\\dfrac{3}{2} &amp;= 0\\\\\nx^2-\\left(\\sqrt{\\dfrac{3}{2}}\\right)^2 &amp;= 0\\\\\n\\left(x-\\sqrt{\\dfrac{3}{2}}\\right)\\left(x+\\sqrt{\\dfrac{3}{2}}\\right) &amp;= 0,\n}\n\\]\nhence $x= \\pm \\sqrt{\\dfrac{3}{2}}.$\nSince the leading coefficient of $x^2-\\sqrt{\\dfrac{3}{2}}$ is positive, the parabola opens upward. Therefore, the solution set to the given inequality ($y &lt; 0$) lies between the roots (but it does not include them):\nTherefore, the solution is $x\\in \\left(-\\sqrt{\\dfrac{3}{2}},\\sqrt{\\dfrac{3}{2}}\\right).$"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x\\in \\left(-\\sqrt{\\dfrac{3}{2}},\\sqrt{\\dfrac{3}{2}}\\right)$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x \\in \\left(-\\infty, -\\dfrac{9}{6}\\right) \\cup \\left(\\dfrac{9}{6}, \\infty\\right)$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x \\in \\left(\\infty, -\\sqrt{\\dfrac{3}{2}}\\right)\\cup \\left(\\sqrt{\\dfrac{3}{2}}, \\infty\\right)$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x \\in \\left(-\\infty, -\\dfrac{1}{2}\\right)\\cup \\left(\\dfrac{1}{2}, \\infty\\right)$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x \\in \\left(-\\dfrac{9}{4}, \\dfrac{9}{4}\\right)$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"M"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Solving Elementary Quadratic Inequalities"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Solve $4x^2&gt; 9.$"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"First, let's simplify the inequality:\n\\begin{align*}\n4x^2 &amp;&gt;9\\\\[3pt]\nx^2 &amp;&gt; \\dfrac{9}{4}\\\\\n\\end{align*}\nNow, we can use either of two methods to solve the inequality.\nMethod 1 - Solving an Absolute Value Equation\nTaking the square root of both sides of the inequality, we get\n\\[\n\\eqalign{\n\\sqrt{x^2} &amp;&gt; \\sqrt{ \\dfrac{9}{4}}\\\\\n|x| &amp;&gt; \\dfrac{3}{2}.\\\\\n}\n\\]\nNow, using the definition of absolute value, we must have\n\\[\nx&lt; -\\dfrac{3}{2}\\quad\\textrm{or}\\quad x&gt;\\dfrac{3}{2}.\n\\]\nSo, the solution is $x \\in \\left(-\\infty, -\\dfrac{3}{2}\\right) \\cup\\left ( \\dfrac{3}{2}, +\\infty\\right).$\nMethod 2 - Picturing a Quadratic Curve\nStarting from $x^2&gt; \\dfrac{9}{4} $ and moving all terms to the left-hand side, we reach\n\\[\nx^2- \\dfrac{9}{4} &gt; 0 .\n\\]\nNow, we consider the corresponding parabola given by $\\, y = x^2-\\dfrac{9}{4} \\, $ and find its roots:\n\\[\n\\eqalign{\nx^2-\\dfrac{9}{4} &amp;= 0\\\\\nx^2-\\left(\\dfrac{3}{2}\\right)^2 &amp;= 0\\\\\n\\left(x-\\dfrac{3}{2}\\right)\\left(x+\\dfrac{3}{2}\\right) &amp;= 0,\n}\n\\]\nso the roots are $x= \\pm \\dfrac{3}{2}.$\nSince the leading coefficient of $x^2-\\dfrac{9}{4}$ is positive, the parabola opens upward. Hence, the solution set to the given inequality ($y &gt; 0$) lies outside the roots and does not include them:\nTherefore, the solution is $x \\in \\left(-\\infty, -\\dfrac{3}{2}\\right) \\cup \\left(\\dfrac{3}{2}, \\infty\\right).$"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x \\in \\left(-\\infty, -\\dfrac{3}{2}\\right) \\cup \\left(\\dfrac{3}{2}, \\infty\\right)$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x \\in \\left(-\\infty, -\\dfrac{9}{4}\\right) \\cup \\left(\\dfrac{9}{4}, \\infty\\right)$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x \\in \\left(\\infty, -\\dfrac{3}{2}\\right)\\cap \\left(-\\dfrac{9}{2}, \\infty\\right)$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x \\in \\left(-\\infty, -\\dfrac{1}{2}\\right)\\cup \\left(\\dfrac{1}{2}, \\infty\\right)$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x \\in \\left(\\dfrac{9}{4}, \\infty\\right)$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"M"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Solving Elementary Quadratic Inequalities"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Solve the inequality $5x^2+2x \\gt 2x-3.$"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"First, let's simplify the inequality:\n\\begin{align*}\n5x^2+2x&amp;&gt; 2x-3\\\\[3pt]\n5x^2+2x-2x &amp;&gt; 2x-3-2x\\\\[3pt]\n5x^2&amp;&gt; -3.\n\\end{align*}\nNow, we can use either of two methods to solve the inequality.\nMethod 1 - Solving an Absolute Value Inequality\nWe know that the square of any real number is greater than or equal to zero.\nSo, the inequality is true for every real number. Therefore the solution is $x \\in (-\\infty,\\infty).$\nMethod 2 - Picturing a Quadratic Curve\nStarting from $5x^2 \\gt -3$ and moving all terms to the left-hand side, we reach\n\\[\n5x^2+3 \\gt 0.\n\\]\nNow, we consider the corresponding parabola given by $\\, y = 5x^2+3. $ :\nSince the leading coefficient of $ 5x^2+3$ is positive, the parabola opens upward. The desired inequality $(y \\gt 0)$ is always satisfied. Therefore, the solution set to the given inequality includes all real numbers. That is, $x \\in (-\\infty,\\infty).$"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x \\in (-\\infty, \\infty)$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x \\in (-3, \\infty)$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x \\in (-\\infty, 3)$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x \\in (-3, 3)$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x \\in (3, \\infty)$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"H"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Solving Elementary Quadratic Inequalities"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Solve the inequality $3x^2-6\\leq 5x^2.$"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"First, let's simplify the inequality:\n\\begin{align*}\n3x^2-6&amp;\\leq 5x^2 \\\\\n3x^2-5x^2 &amp;\\leq6\\\\\n-2x^2 &amp;\\leq 6\\\\\nx^2&amp;\\geq -\\dfrac{ 6}{2}\\\\\nx^2&amp;\\geq -3.\n\\end{align*}\nNow, we can use either of two methods to solve the inequality.\nMethod 1 - Solving an Absolute Value Inequality\nWe know that the square of any real number is greater than or equal to zero.\nSo, the inequality is true for every real number. Therefore the solution is $x \\in (-\\infty,\\infty).$\nMethod 2 - Picturing a Quadratic Curve\nStarting from $-2x^2 \\leq 6$ and moving all terms to the left-hand side, we reach\n\\[\n-2x^2-6 \\leq 0 .\n\\]\nNow, we consider the corresponding parabola given by $y = -2x^2-6$:\nSince the leading coefficient of $ -2x^2-6$ is negative, the parabola opens downward (like $\\frown$). The desired inequality $(y\\leq 0)$ is always satisfied. Therefore, the solution set to the given inequality includes all real numbers. That is, $x \\in (-\\infty,\\infty).$"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\, x\\in (-\\infty, \\infty)$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\, x\\in [3 , \\infty)$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\, x \\in (-\\infty , -3]\\cup [3,\\infty)$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\, x \\in (-\\infty , -3]$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\, x \\in (-\\infty , -5] \\cup [5, \\infty)$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"H"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Solving Elementary Quadratic Inequalities"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Solve the inequality $x^2 \\lt -{2}.$"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"We can solve this inequality using either of the two methods described below.\nMethod 1 - Solving an Absolute Value Inequality\nWe know that the square of any real number is greater than or equal to zero. Therefore, it is impossible for the square of a real number to be smaller than $-2.$\nSo, the inequality $x^2 \\lt -2$ has no solutions.\nMethod 2 - Picturing a Quadratic Curve\nLet's bring all the terms to the left-hand side,\n\\[\nx^2+ 2 &lt; 0,\n\\]\nand then plot the parabola $y=x^2+ 2.$ The desired inequality ($y&lt; 0$) is never satisfied, so there are no solutions."</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"The inequality has no solution"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$- 2 \\lt x \\lt 2$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$- 2 \\lt x \\lt 0$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$- \\sqrt{2} \\lt x \\lt \\sqrt{2}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$x \\lt -\\sqrt{2}$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"M"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Circles in the Coordinate Plane"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Find the coordinates $(h,k)$ of the center of the circle above given that the points $(0,2)$ and $(8,4)$ lie on a diameter of the circle."</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The center of the circle is located at the midpoint of the diameter. Therefore, we can calculate it using the midpoint formula:\n\\[\n\\eqalign{\n(h,k) &amp;= \\left( \\dfrac{x_1+x_2}{2}, \\dfrac {y_1+y_2}{2}\\right) \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= \\left(\\dfrac{0+8}2,\\dfrac{2+4}2\\right) \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= \\left(\\dfrac{8}2,\\dfrac{6}2\\right) \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= (4, 3).\n}\n\\]"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"$(4,3)$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$(3,1)$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$(3,3)$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$(3,4)$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$(4,1)$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Circles in the Coordinate Plane"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Find the coordinates $(h,k)$ of the center of the circle above given that the points $(-4,3)$ and $(2,1)$ lie on a diameter of the circle."</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The center of the circle is located at the midpoint of the diameter. Therefore, we can calculate it using the midpoint formula:\n\\[\n\\eqalign{\n(h,k) &amp;= \\left( \\dfrac{x_1+x_2}{2}, \\dfrac {y_1+y_2}{2}\\right) \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= \\left(\\dfrac{-4+2}2,\\dfrac{3+1}2\\right) \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= \\left(\\dfrac{-2}2,\\dfrac{4}2\\right) \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= (-1, 2).\n}\n\\]"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"$(-1,2)$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$(-1,3)$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$(-2,2)$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$(-1.5,2)$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$(-3,1)$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Circles in the Coordinate Plane"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The points $A$ and $B$ have coordinates $(-6,3)$ and $(0,1)$, respectively, and the segment $\\overline{AB}$ is a diameter of a circle. Find the radius of the circle."</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The diameter can be calculated using the distance formula:\n\\[\n\\eqalign{\nd &amp;= \\sqrt{{(x_1 - x_2)}^2+{(y_1-y_2)}^2} \\\\[3pt]\n&amp;= \\sqrt{\\left(-6 - 0\\right)^2 + \\left(3 -1\\right)^2} \\\\[3pt]\n&amp;= \\sqrt{(-6)^2+(2)^2} \\\\[3pt]\n&amp;= \\sqrt{36+4} \\\\[3pt]\n&amp;= \\sqrt{40} \\\\[3pt]\n&amp;= 2\\sqrt{10}.\n}\n\\]\nThe radius is half the diameter:\n\\[\nr = \\dfrac{d}{2} = \\dfrac{2\\sqrt{10}}{2} =\\sqrt{10}\n\\]"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\sqrt{10}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$4\\sqrt{10}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{\\sqrt{10}}{2}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\sqrt{5}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$2\\sqrt{10}$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"M"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Circles in the Coordinate Plane"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The points $A$ and $B$ have coordinates $(6,-4)$ and $(0,-2)$, respectively, and the segment $\\overline{AB}$ is a diameter of a circle. Find the radius of the circle."</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The diameter can be calculated using the distance formula:\n\\begin{align*}\nd &amp;= \\sqrt{{(x_1 - x_2)}^2+{(y_1-y_2)}^2} \\\\[3pt]\n&amp;= \\sqrt{(6-0)^2 + (-4 - (-2))^2}\\\\[3pt]\n&amp;= \\sqrt{(6)^2+(-2)^2} \\\\[3pt]\n&amp;= \\sqrt{36 +4} \\\\[3pt]\n&amp;= \\sqrt{40}\\\\[3pt]\n&amp;= 2\\sqrt{10}\n\\end{align*}\nThe radius is half the diameter:\n\\[\nr = \\dfrac{d}{2} = 2\\dfrac{\\sqrt{10}}{2} = \\sqrt{10}\n\\]"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\sqrt{10}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$2\\sqrt{10}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$3\\sqrt{2}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\sqrt{8}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$3\\sqrt{3}$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"M"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Circles in the Coordinate Plane"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Find the coordinates $(h,k)$ of the center of the circle above and its radius given that the points $(2,-3)$ and $(10,5)$ lie on a diameter of the circle."</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The center of the circle is located at the midpoint of the diameter. So, we can calculate the coordinates of the center using the midpoint formula:\n\\[\n\\eqalign{\n(h,k) &amp;= \\left( \\dfrac{x_1+x_2}{2}, \\dfrac {y_1+y_2}{2}\\right) \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= \\left(\\dfrac{2+10}2,\\dfrac{-3+5}2\\right) \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= \\left(\\dfrac{12}2,\\dfrac{2}2\\right) \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= (6, 1).\n}\n\\]\nThe radius can be found by calculating the distance between the center $\\left(6,1\\right)$ and a point on the circle, such as $A(2,-3)\\mathbin{:}$\n\\[\n\\eqalign{\nr&amp;=\\sqrt{{(x_1-x_2)}^2+{(y_1-y_2)}^2}\\\\[3pt]\n&amp;= \\sqrt{(6 - 2)^2 + (1 - (-3))^2} \\\\[3pt]\n&amp;= \\sqrt{(4)^2+(4)^2} \\\\[3pt]\n&amp;= \\sqrt{16+16} \\\\[3pt]\n&amp;= \\sqrt{32}\\\\[3pt]\n&amp;= 4\\sqrt{2}.\n}\n\\]"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"The center is $(6,1)$ and the radius is $4\\sqrt{2}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"The center is $(6,1)$ and the radius is $5\\sqrt{2}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"The center is $(6,1)$ and the radius is $3\\sqrt{2}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"The center is $(5,1)$ and the radius is $4\\sqrt{2}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"The center is $(5,1)$ and the radius is $5\\sqrt{2}$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Circles in the Coordinate Plane"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The segment $\\overline{AB}$ is the diameter of a circle where the points $A$ and $B$ have coordinates $(3b,6b)$ and $(-3b,-2b)$, respectively, with $b&gt;0.$ Find the coordinates of the center and the radius of the circle."</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The center of the circle is located at the midpoint of the diameter. So, we can calculate the coordinates of the center using the midpoint formula:\n\\[\n\\eqalign{\n(h,k) &amp;= \\left( \\dfrac{x_1+x_2}{2}, \\dfrac {y_1+y_2}{2}\\right) \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= \\left(\\dfrac{3b+(-3b)}2,\\dfrac{6b+(-2b)}2\\right) \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= \\left(\\dfrac{0}2,\\dfrac{4b}2\\right) \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= (0, 2b).\n}\n\\]\nThe radius can be found by calculating the distance between the center $\\left(0,2b\\right)$ and a point on the circle, such as $A(3b,6b)\\mathbin{:}$\n\\[\n\\eqalign{\nr&amp;=\\sqrt{{(x_1-x_2)}^2+{(y_1-y_2)}^2}\\\\[3pt]\n&amp;= \\sqrt{(0 - 3b)^2 + (2b - 6b)^2}\\\\[3pt]\n&amp;= \\sqrt{(-3b)^2+(-4b)^2} \\\\[3pt]\n&amp;= \\sqrt{9b^2 +16b^2} \\\\[3pt]\n&amp;= \\sqrt{25b^2}\\\\[3pt]\n&amp;= 5|b|.\n}\n\\]\nFinally, since $b &gt; 0,$ we have that\n\\[\nr = 5\\vert b \\vert = 5b.\n\\]\nTherefore, the center of the circle is at $(0, 2b)$ and the radius is $5b.$"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"The center is $(0,2b)$ and the radius is $5b$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"The center is $(b,b)$ and the radius is $5|b|$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"The center is $(b,2b)$ and the radius is $4b$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"The center is $(0,b)$ and the radius is $3b$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"The center is $(b,3b)$ and the radius is $3|b|$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"H"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Modeling With the Poisson Distribution"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"It is known that, on average, $6$ cell phones per batch produced by a cellphone factory have defects. What is the probability that the next batch contains exactly $4$ phones with defects? Round your answer to $3$ decimal places. You may assume that defective phones occur independently and at a constant average rate."</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"A Poisson random variable $X$ with rate $\\lambda$ has the following probability mass function:\n\\[\nf(x) = \\dfrac{\\lambda^x e^{-\\lambda}}{x!}\n\\]\nLet $X$ be the number of cell phones with defects. Since the average rate is $6$ cell phones per batch, and events occur independently and at a constant average rate, $X$ can be modeled as a Poisson random variable with rate $\\lambda = 6,$ and we can write $X\\sim \\textrm{Po}(6).$\nSo, $X$ has the following probability mass function:\n\\[\nf(x) = \\dfrac{6^x e^{-6}}{x!}\n\\]\nTherefore, the probability of having $4$ cell phones with defects in the next batch is\n\\[\\begin{align*}\nP(X=4) &amp;= f(4) \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= \\dfrac{6^{4} e^{-6}}{4!} \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;\\approx 0.134\n\\end{align*}\\]\nrounded to $3$ decimal places."</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"$0.134$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$0.141$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$0.099$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$0.118$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$0.165$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"M"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Modeling With the Poisson Distribution"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"An office secretary notes that, on average, $5$ calls are received per minute. What is the probability that she will receive $7$ calls in the next minute? Round your answer to $3$ decimal places. You may assume that calls occur independently and at a constant average rate."</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"A Poisson random variable $X$ with rate $\\lambda$ has the following probability mass function:\n\\[\nf(x) = \\dfrac{\\lambda^x e^{-\\lambda}}{x!}\n\\]\nLet $X$ be the number of calls per minute. Since the average rate is $5$ calls per minute, and events occur independently and at a constant average rate, the number of calls can be modeled as a Poisson random variable with rate $\\lambda = 5,$ and we can write $X\\sim \\textrm{Po}(5).$\nSo, $X$ has the following probability mass function:\n\\[\nf(x) = \\dfrac{5^x e^{-5}}{x!}\n\\]\nTherefore, the probability of having $7$ calls in the next minute is\n\\[\\begin{align*}\nP(X=7) &amp;= f(7) \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= \\dfrac{5^{7} e^{-5}}{7!} \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;\\approx 0.104\n\\end{align*}\\]\nrounded to $3$ decimal places."</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"$0.104$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$0.133$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$0.091$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$0.146$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$0.192$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Finding the Nth Term of an Arithmetic Sequence Given Two Terms"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Find the general formula for the $n$th term of an arithmetic sequence if the $15$th term is $40$ and the first term is $-2.$"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The formula for the $n$th term of an arithmetic sequence is given by\n\\[ a_n = a_1 + (n-1)d. \\]\nWe already know that the first term is $a_1=-2,$ so we just need to figure out the common difference $d.$\nTo find the common difference, we can find the total difference between the terms $a_1=-2$ and $a_{15}=40$ and divide it by the number of jumps, as follows:\n\\begin{align*}\nd &amp;= \\dfrac{a_{15} - a_1}{15-1} \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= \\dfrac{40 - (-2)}{14} \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= \\dfrac{42}{14} \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= 3\n\\end{align*}\nSo, the formula for the $n$th term of our arithmetic sequence is\\[\n\\eqalign{\na_n&amp;= a_{1}+(n-1)d\\\\[3pt]\n&amp; = -2 + (n-1) (3) \\\\[3pt]\n&amp; = -2 + 3n - 3\\\\[3pt]\n&amp; = 3n - 5.\n}\n\\]"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"$ a_n = 3n - 5$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$ a_n = 2n + 10$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$ a_n = 4n - 20$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$ a_n = 4n - 10$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$ a_n = 3n + 15$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"H"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Finding the Nth Term of an Arithmetic Sequence Given Two Terms"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Find the formula for the $n$th term of an arithmetic sequence if the $7$th term is $45$ and the first term is $3.$"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The formula for the $n$th term of an arithmetic sequence is given by\n\\[ a_n = a_1 + (n-1)d. \\]\nWe already know that the first term is $a_1=3,$ so we just need to figure out the common difference $d.$\nTo find the common difference, we can find the total difference between the terms $a_1=3$ and $a_{7}=45$ and divide it by the number of jumps, as follows:\n\\begin{align*}\nd &amp;= \\dfrac{a_{7} - a_1}{7-1} \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= \\dfrac{45 - 3}{6} \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= \\dfrac{42}{6} \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= 7\n\\end{align*}\nSo, the formula for the $n$th term of our arithmetic sequence is\n\\[\n\\eqalign{\na_n&amp;= a_{1}+(n-1)d\\\\[3pt]\n&amp; = 3 + (n-1) (7) \\\\[3pt]\n&amp; = 3 + 7n - 7\\\\[3pt]\n&amp; = 7n - 4.\n}\n\\]"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"$a_n = 7n - 4$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$a_n = 6n + 3$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$a_n = 5n + 10$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$a_n = 7n + 1$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$a_n = 6n + 5$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Finding the Nth Term of an Arithmetic Sequence Given Two Terms"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The second and third terms of an arithmetic sequence are $45$ and $80,$ respectively. Determine the formula for the $n$th term of the sequence."</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The formula for the $n$th term is given by\n\\[ a_n = a_1 + (n-1)d, \\]\nso we need to figure out the first term $a_1$ and the common difference $d.$\nSince the terms $a_2=45$ and $a_3=80$ are consecutive, we can subtract them to find the common difference:\n\\begin{align*}\nd &amp;= a_3-a_2 \\\\[3pt]\n&amp;= 80- 45\\\\[3pt]\n&amp;= 35\n\\end{align*}\nNow, we can use one of our known values, say, $a_2=45,$ to determine the first term of the sequence. Since $a_2$ is $1$ jump after $a_1,$ we have:\n\\[\n\\eqalign{\na_2 &amp;= a_1 + 1\\cdot d \\\\[3pt]\n45 &amp;= a_1 +35\\\\[3pt]\n45-35 &amp;= a_1\\\\[3pt]\na_1&amp;= 10\n}\n\\]\nSo the formula for the $n$th term of this arithmetic sequence is:\n\\[\n\\eqalign{\na_n&amp;= a_{1}+(n-1)d \\\\[3pt]\n&amp; = 10 + (n-1) \\cdot 35\\\\[3pt]\n&amp; =10 + 35n -35 \\\\[3pt]\n&amp; = 35n -25\n}\n\\]"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"$a_n= 35n -25$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$a_n= 5n +25$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$a_n= 9n +27$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$a_n= 15n +15$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$a_n= 20n +5$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Finding the Nth Term of an Arithmetic Sequence Given Two Terms"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The $4$th and $5$th terms of an arithmetic sequence are $3$ and $-6,$ respectively. Determine the formula for the $n$th term of the sequence."</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The formula for the $n$th term is given by\n\\[\na_n = a_1 + (n-1)d,\n\\]\nso we need to figure out the first term $a_1$ and the common difference $d.$\nSince the terms $a_{4} = 3$ and $a_{5} = -6$ are consecutive, we can subtract them to find the common difference:\n\\begin{align*}\nd &amp; = a_{5} - a_{4}\\\\[5pt]\n&amp; = -6 - 3\\\\[5pt]\n&amp; = -9\n\\end{align*}\nNow, we can use one of our known values, say, $a_{4} = 3,$ to determine the first term of the sequence. Since $a_{4}$ is $3$ jumps after $a_1,$ we have\n\\begin{align*}\na_{4} &amp; = a_1 + 3d \\\\[5pt]\n3 &amp; = a_1 + 3(-9) \\\\[5pt]\n3 &amp; = a_1 - 27\\\\[5pt]\n3 + 27 &amp; = a_1\\\\[5pt]\na_1 &amp; = 30.\n\\end{align*}\nSo, the formula for the $n$th term of this arithmetic sequence is\n\\begin{align*}\na_n &amp; = a_{1} + (n-1)d \\\\[5pt]\n&amp; = 30 + (n-1)(-9) \\\\[5pt]\n&amp; = 30 - 9n + 9\\\\[5pt]\n&amp; = 39 - 9n.\n\\end{align*}"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"$a_n = 39 - 9n$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$a_n = 30 - 9n$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$a_n = 35 - 8n$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$a_n = 26 - 8n$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$a_n = 31 - 7n$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"H"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Finding the Nth Term of an Arithmetic Sequence Given Two Terms"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Find the $14$th term of an arithmetic sequence if the $3$rd term is $40$ and the $23$rd term is $-20.$"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The formula for the $n$th term is given by\\[\na_n = a_1 + (n-1)d,\n\\]\nso we need to figure out the first term $a_1$ and the common difference $d.$\nTo find the common difference, we can find the total difference between the terms $a_3 = 40$ and $a_{23} = -20$ and divide it by the number of jumps, as follows:\n\\begin{align*}\nd &amp; = \\dfrac{a_{23} - a_3}{23 - 3} \\\\[5pt]\n&amp; = \\dfrac{-20 - 40}{23 - 3} \\\\[5pt]\n&amp; = \\dfrac{(-60)}{20} \\\\[5pt]\n&amp; = -3\n\\end{align*}\nNow, we can use one of our known values, say, $a_3 = 40,$ to determine the first term of the sequence. Since $a_3$ is $2$ jumps after $a_1,$ we have\n\\begin{align*}\na_3 &amp; = a_1 + 2d \\\\[5pt]\n40 &amp; = a_1 + 2(-3) \\\\[5pt]\n40 &amp; = a_1 - 6 \\\\[5pt]\n40 + 6 &amp; = a_1\\\\[5pt]\na_1 &amp; = 46.\n\\end{align*}\nSo the formula for the $n$th term is:\n\\[\n\\begin{align}\na_n &amp;= 46 + (n-1)(-3)\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= 46-3n+3\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= 49-3n\n\\end{align}\n\\]\nFinally, we can find the $14$th term by substituting $n = 14$ into the above:\n\\begin{align*}\na_{14} &amp; = 49-3(14)\\\\[5pt]\n&amp; = 49-42 \\\\[5pt]\n&amp; = 7\n\\end{align*}"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"$7$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$4$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$1$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$-2$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$-5$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"H"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Finding the Nth Term of an Arithmetic Sequence Given Two Terms"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Find the $11$th term of an arithmetic sequence if the $19$th term is $99$ and the $4$th term is $-6.$"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The formula for the $n$th term is given by\n\\[ a_n = a_1 + (n-1)d, \\]\nso we need to figure out the first term $a_1$ and the common difference $d.$\nTo find the common difference, we can find the total difference between the terms $a_{19}=99$ and $a_{4}=-6$ and divide it by the number of jumps, as follows:\n\\begin{align*}\nd &amp;= \\dfrac{a_{19} -a_{4}}{19-4} \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= \\dfrac {99-(-6)}{19-4} \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= \\dfrac {105}{15} \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= 7\n\\end{align*}\nNow, we can use one of our known values, say, $a_{4}=-6,$ to determine the first term of the sequence. Since $a_{4}$ is $3$ jumps after $a_1,$ we have:\n\\begin{align*}\na_{4} &amp;= a_1 + 3d \\\\[3pt]\n-6 &amp;= a_1 + 3\\cdot 7 \\\\[3pt]\n-6 &amp;= a_1 + 21 \\\\[3pt]\na_1 &amp;= -27\n\\end{align*}\nSo the formula for the $n$th term is:\n\\[\n\\begin{align}\na_n &amp;= -27 + (n-1)\\cdot 7\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= 7n-34\n\\end{align}\n\\]\nFinally, we can find the $11$th term by substituting $n=11$ into the above:\n\\begin{align*}\na_{11}\n&amp;= 7(11)-34 \\\\[3pt]\n&amp;= 77-34 \\\\[3pt]\n&amp;= 43\n\\end{align*}"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"$43$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$37$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$39$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$47$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$49$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"H"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Perfect Square Quadratic Equations with One or No Solutions"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Which value of $q$ gives precisely one solution to the equation $x^2+q+3=7?$"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"First, we solve for $x$ as follows:\n\\begin{align*}\nx^2+q+3 &amp;= 7 \\\\[3pt]\nx^2 &amp;= 7-q-3 \\\\[3pt]\nx^2 &amp;= 4-q \\\\[3pt]\n\\sqrt{x^2} &amp;= \\sqrt{4-q} \\\\[3pt]\n\\vert x\\vert &amp;= \\sqrt{4-q}\n\\end{align*}\nThe equation has only one solution if the right-hand side is equal to zero, and the right-hand side is zero only if the expression underneath the radical is zero. So, we solve for the value of $q$ that makes the expression under the radical equal to zero:\n\\begin{align*}\n4-q &amp;= 0 \\\\[3pt]\nq &amp;= 4\n\\end{align*}\nTherefore, $q=4$ will give only one solution to the equation."</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"$4$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$-3$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$7$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$-4$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$10$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Vertical Reflections of Functions"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The function $y=f(x)$ is shown above. Which of the following gives the plot of $y=-f(x)?$"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"To obtain the graph of $y=-f(x)$ from $y=f(x),$ we need to reflect the graph over the $x$ axis. The resulting graph is shown below."</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">""</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">""</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">""</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">""</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">""</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Vertical Reflections of Functions"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The function $y = f(x)$ is shown above. Which of the following gives the plot of $y=-f(x)?$"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"To obtain the graph of $y=\u2212f(x)$ from $y=f(x)$, we need to reflect the graph over the $x$-axis. The resulting graph is shown below."</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">""</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">""</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">""</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">""</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">""</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"M"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Vertical Reflections of Functions"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The point $P$ with coordinates $(8,-6)$ lies on the curve $y = f(x).$ Which point must lie on $y = -f(x)?$"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The transformation $y=-f(x)$ reflects the curve $y=f(x)$ in the $x$-axis.\nFirst, notice that the point $P(8,-6)$ lies $6$ units below the $x$-axis. So, under the action of the transformation, this point will be reflected $6$ units above the $x$-axis.\nWe conclude that the point $(8, 6)$ must lie on the graph of $y=-f(x).$"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"$(8, 6)$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$(8, -6)$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$(-8, 6)$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$(-8, -6)$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$(6, 8)$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Vertical Reflections of Functions"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The point $P$ with coordinates $(3,-1)$ lies on the curve $y = f(x).$ Which point must lie on $y = -f(x)?$"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The transformation $y=-f(x)$ reflects the curve $y=f(x)$ in the $x$-axis.\nFirst, notice that the point $P(3,-1)$ lies $1$ unit below the $x$-axis. So, under the action of the transformation, this point will be reflected $1$ unit above the $x$-axis.\nWe conclude that the point $(3, 1)$ must lie on the graph of $y=-f(x).$"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"$(3, 1)$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$(-3, -1)$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$(-3, 1)$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$(-1, 3)$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$(1, -3)$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Problem Solving Using Vector Diagrams"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Consider the diagram shown above, where $\\overrightarrow{YX}=\\mathbf{a},$ $\\overrightarrow{XV}=\\mathbf{b},$ $\\overrightarrow{VZ}=\\mathbf{c},$ and $\\overrightarrow{YW}=\\mathbf{d}.$ Find $\\overrightarrow{XW}$ in terms of $\\mathbf{a},\\mathbf{b}, \\mathbf{c},$ and $\\mathbf{d}.$"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"We want to find a \"path\" from $X$ to $W$ consisting only of the vectors $\\mathbf a, \\mathbf b, \\mathbf c,$ and $\\mathbf d.$\nAccording to the triangle law of addition,\n\\[\n{\\color{red}{\\overrightarrow{XW}}} = {\\color{blue}{\\overrightarrow{XY}}} + {\\color{blue}{\\overrightarrow{YW}}},\n\\]\nas shown below.\nNow, from the original diagram, we have that\n\\[\n{\\color{blue}\\overrightarrow{XY}} = -\\mathbf{a}, \\qquad {\\color{blue}{\\overrightarrow{YW}}} = \\mathbf{d}.\n\\]\nLet's add these to our diagram.\nTherefore, we obtain\n\\begin{align*}\n{\\color{red}{\\overrightarrow{XW}}} &amp;= {\\color{blue}{\\overrightarrow{XY}}} + {\\color{blue}{\\overrightarrow{YW}}}\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= -\\mathbf{a}+\\mathbf{d} \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=\\mathbf{d}-\\mathbf{a}.\n\\end{align*}"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"$ \\overrightarrow{XW}=\\mathbf{d}-\\mathbf{a}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$ \\overrightarrow{XW}=\\mathbf{c}-\\mathbf{a}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$ \\overrightarrow{XW}=\\mathbf{a-b-c}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$ \\overrightarrow{XW}=\\mathbf{d}+\\mathbf{a}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$ \\overrightarrow{XW}=\\mathbf{a+b-c}$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"M"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Problem Solving Using Vector Diagrams"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Consider the diagram shown above, where $\\overrightarrow{PS}=\\mathbf{a},$ $\\overrightarrow{SQ}=\\mathbf{b},$ and $\\overrightarrow{PT}=\\mathbf{c}.$ Find $\\overrightarrow{PQ}$ in terms of $\\mathbf{a},\\mathbf{b}$ and $\\mathbf{c}.$"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"We want to find a \"path\" from $P$ to $Q$ consisting only of the vectors $\\mathbf a, \\mathbf b,$ and $\\mathbf c.$\nAccording to the triangle law of addition,\n\\[\n{\\color{red}{\\overrightarrow{PQ}}} = {\\color{blue}{\\overrightarrow{PS}}} + {\\color{blue}{\\overrightarrow{SQ}}}\n\\]\nas shown below.\nNow, from the original diagram, we have that\n\\[\n{\\color{blue}\\overrightarrow{PS}} = \\mathbf{a}, \\qquad {\\color{blue}\\overrightarrow{SQ}} = \\mathbf{b}.\n\\]\nLet's add these to our diagram.\nTherefore, we obtain\n\\begin{align*}\n\\overrightarrow{PQ}\n&amp;= {\\color{blue}{\\overrightarrow{PS}}} + {\\color{blue}{\\overrightarrow{SQ}}} \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=\\mathbf{a}+\\mathbf{b}\n\\end{align*}"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\overrightarrow{PQ}= \\mathbf{a}+\\mathbf{b}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\overrightarrow{PQ}= \\mathbf{a}-\\mathbf{b}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\overrightarrow{PQ}= \\mathbf{b}-\\mathbf{a}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\overrightarrow{PQ}= \\mathbf{a}+\\mathbf{c}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\overrightarrow{PQ}= \\mathbf{c}-\\mathbf{b}$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Problem Solving Using Vector Diagrams"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Consider the diagram shown above, where $\\overrightarrow{QP}=\\mathbf{a},$ $\\overrightarrow{RQ}=\\mathbf{b},$ $\\overrightarrow{SQ}=\\mathbf{c},$ and $\\overrightarrow{RT}=\\mathbf{d}.$ Find $\\overrightarrow{TP}$ in terms of $\\mathbf{a},\\mathbf{b}, \\mathbf{c},$ and $\\mathbf{d}.$"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"We want to find a \"path\" from $T$ to $P$ consisting only of the vectors $\\mathbf{a},\\mathbf{b}, \\mathbf{c},$ and $\\mathbf{d}.$\nAccording to the triangle law of addition,\n\\[\n{\\color{red}{\\overrightarrow{TP}}} = {\\color{blue}{\\overrightarrow{TR}}} + {\\color{blue}{\\overrightarrow{RQ}}} + {\\color{blue}{\\overrightarrow{QP}}}\n\\]\nas shown below.\nNow, from the original diagram, we have that\n\\[\n{\\color{blue}\\overrightarrow{TR}} = -\\mathbf{d}, \\qquad {\\color{blue}\\overrightarrow{RQ}} = \\mathbf{b}, \\qquad {\\color{blue}\\overrightarrow{QP}} = \\mathbf{a}.\n\\]\nLet's add these to our diagram.\nTherefore, we obtain\n\\begin{align*}\n\\overrightarrow{TP}\n&amp;= {\\color{blue}{\\overrightarrow{TR}}} + {\\color{blue}{\\overrightarrow{RQ}}} + {\\color{blue}{\\overrightarrow{QP}}} \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= -\\mathbf{d}+\\mathbf{b}+\\mathbf{a} \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= \\mathbf{a}+\\mathbf{b}-\\mathbf{d}.\n\\end{align*}"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\overrightarrow{TP} = \\mathbf{a}+\\mathbf{b}-\\mathbf{d}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\overrightarrow{TP} = \\mathbf{a}- \\mathbf{b}+\\mathbf{d}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\overrightarrow{TP} = \\mathbf{b}-\\mathbf{c}-\\mathbf{d}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\overrightarrow{TP} = \\mathbf{a}+\\mathbf{b}-\\mathbf{c}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\overrightarrow{TP} = \\mathbf{a}-\\mathbf{c}+\\mathbf{d}$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"M"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Problem Solving Using Vector Diagrams"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Consider the diagram shown above, where $\\overrightarrow{QT}=\\mathbf{a},$ $\\overrightarrow{RS}=\\mathbf{b},$ $\\overrightarrow{TR}=\\mathbf{c},$ and $\\overrightarrow{RP}=\\mathbf{d}.$ Find $\\overrightarrow{QP}$ in terms of $\\mathbf{a},\\mathbf{b}, \\mathbf{c},$ and $\\mathbf{d}.$"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"We want to find a \"path\" from $Q$ to $P$ consisting only of the vectors $\\mathbf a, \\mathbf b, \\mathbf c,$ and $\\mathbf d.$\nAccording to the triangle law of addition,\n\\[\n{\\color{red}{\\overrightarrow{QP}}} = {\\color{blue}{\\overrightarrow{QT}}} + {\\color{blue}{\\overrightarrow{TR}}} + {\\color{blue}{\\overrightarrow{RP}}}\n\\]\nas shown below.\nNow, from the original diagram, we have that\n\\[\n{\\color{blue}\\overrightarrow{QT}} = \\mathbf{a}, \\qquad {\\color{blue}\\overrightarrow{TR}} = \\mathbf{c}, \\qquad {\\color{blue}\\overrightarrow{RP}} = \\mathbf{d}.\n\\]\nLet's add these to our diagram.\nTherefore, we obtain\n\\begin{align*}\n{\\color{red}{\\overrightarrow{QP}}}\n&amp;= {\\color{blue}{\\overrightarrow{QT}}} + {\\color{blue}{\\overrightarrow{TR}}} + {\\color{blue}{\\overrightarrow{RP}}} \\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=\\mathbf a +\\mathbf c +\\mathbf d.\n\\end{align*}"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\overrightarrow{QP}=\\mathbf a +\\mathbf c +\\mathbf d$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\overrightarrow{QP}=\\mathbf a -\\mathbf b +\\mathbf d$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\overrightarrow{QP}=\\mathbf b +\\mathbf c +\\mathbf d$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\overrightarrow{QP}=\\mathbf b +\\mathbf c -\\mathbf d$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\overrightarrow{QP}=\\mathbf b -\\mathbf a +\\mathbf c$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Problem Solving Using Vector Diagrams"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"In figure $ABCD$ shown above, which of the following are equal to the vector $\\overrightarrow{DB}?$\n$\\overrightarrow{DA}+\\overrightarrow{AC}+\\overrightarrow{CB}$\n$\\overrightarrow{DA}+\\overrightarrow{AB}$\n$\\overrightarrow{BA}-\\overrightarrow{CA}+\\overrightarrow{CD}$"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"For the $1$st expression, we have:\n\\begin{align*}\n\\overrightarrow{DA}+\\overrightarrow{AC}+\\overrightarrow{CB}\n&amp;= (\\overrightarrow{DA}+\\overrightarrow{AC})+\\overrightarrow{CB} \\\\[3pt]\n&amp;= \\overrightarrow{DC}+\\overrightarrow{CB} \\\\[3pt]\n&amp;= \\overrightarrow{DB} \\quad {\\color{green}\\checkmark}\n\\end{align*}\nFor the $2$nd expression, we have:\n\\begin{align*}\n\\overrightarrow{DA}+\\overrightarrow{AB}\n&amp;= \\overrightarrow{DB} \\quad {\\color{green}\\checkmark}\n\\end{align*}\nFor the $3$rd expression, we have:\n\\begin{align*}\n\\overrightarrow{BA}-\\overrightarrow{CA}+\\overrightarrow{CD}\n&amp;= (\\overrightarrow{BA}-\\overrightarrow{CA})+\\overrightarrow{CD} \\\\[3pt]\n&amp;= (\\overrightarrow{BA}+\\overrightarrow{AC})+\\overrightarrow{CD} \\\\[3pt]\n&amp;= \\overrightarrow{BC}+\\overrightarrow{CD} \\\\[3pt]\n&amp;= \\overrightarrow{BD} \\\\[3pt]\n&amp;\\neq \\overrightarrow{DB} \\quad {\\color{red}\\times}\n\\end{align*}\nTherefore, the correct answer is \"I and II only\"."</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"I and II only"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"I and III only"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"II and III only"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"I only"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"II only"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"M"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Problem Solving Using Vector Diagrams"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"In figure $ABCDE$ shown above, which of the following equalities are true?\n$\\overrightarrow{ED}-\\overrightarrow{CD} = \\overrightarrow{CE}$\n$\\overrightarrow{AE}-\\overrightarrow{DE}+\\overrightarrow{DC}=\\overrightarrow{AC}$\n$\\overrightarrow{CB}-\\overrightarrow{AB}-\\overrightarrow{EA}=\\overrightarrow{CE}$"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Equality I is false. Indeed,\n\\begin{align*}\n\\overrightarrow{ED}-\\overrightarrow{CD} &amp;= \\overrightarrow{ED}+\\overrightarrow{DC}\\\\[3pt]\n&amp;= \\overrightarrow{EC}\\\\[3pt]\n&amp;\\ne \\overrightarrow{CE}.\n\\end{align*}\nEquality II is true. Indeed,\n\\begin{align*}\n\\overrightarrow{AE}-\\overrightarrow{DE}+\\overrightarrow{DC}&amp;=(\\overrightarrow{AE}+\\overrightarrow{ED})+\\overrightarrow{DC} \\\\[3pt]\n&amp;= \\overrightarrow{AD}+\\overrightarrow{DC} \\\\[3pt]\n&amp;= \\overrightarrow{AC}.\n\\end{align*}\nEquality III is true. Indeed,\n\\begin{align*}\n\\overrightarrow{CB}-\\overrightarrow{AB}-\\overrightarrow{EA} &amp;=\n(\\overrightarrow{CB}+\\overrightarrow{BA})-\\overrightarrow{EA}\\\\[3pt]\n&amp;=\\overrightarrow{CA}+\\overrightarrow{AE}\\\\[3pt]\n&amp; =\\overrightarrow{CE}.\n\\end{align*}\nTherefore, the correct answer is \"II and III only\"."</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"II and III only"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"I and III only"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"I and II only"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"I, II, and III"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"II only"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"M"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Problem Solving Using Vector Diagrams"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"In figure $ABCDEFG$ shown above, which of the following equalities are true?\n$\\overrightarrow{DC}-\\overrightarrow{BC} = \\overrightarrow{BD}$\n$\\overrightarrow{GF}-\\overrightarrow{EF}+\\overrightarrow{ED}=\\overrightarrow{GD}$\n$\\overrightarrow{BA}-\\overrightarrow{GA}-\\overrightarrow{FG}=\\overrightarrow{FB}$"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Equality I is false. Indeed,\n\\begin{align*}\n\\overrightarrow{DC}-\\overrightarrow{BC} &amp;= \\overrightarrow{DC}+\\overrightarrow{CB}\\\\[3pt]\n&amp;= \\overrightarrow{DB}\\\\[3pt]\n&amp;\\ne \\overrightarrow{BD}.\n\\end{align*}\nEquality II is true. Indeed,\n\\begin{align*}\n\\overrightarrow{GF}-\\overrightarrow{EF}+\\overrightarrow{ED}&amp;=(\\overrightarrow{GF}+\\overrightarrow{FE})+\\overrightarrow{ED} \\\\[3pt]\n&amp;= \\overrightarrow{GE}+\\overrightarrow{ED} \\\\[3pt]\n&amp;= \\overrightarrow{GD}.\n\\end{align*}\nEquality III is false. Indeed,\n\\begin{align*}\n\\overrightarrow{BA}-\\overrightarrow{GA}-\\overrightarrow{FG} &amp;=\n(\\overrightarrow{BA}+\\overrightarrow{AG})-\\overrightarrow{FG}\\\\[3pt]\n&amp;=\\overrightarrow{BG}+\\overrightarrow{GF}\\\\[3pt]\n&amp; =\\overrightarrow{BF}\\\\[3pt]\n&amp;\\ne \\overrightarrow{FB}.\n\\end{align*}\nTherefore, the correct answer is \"II only\"."</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"II only"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"I only"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"III only"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"I and II only"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"II and III only"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"M"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Differentiating Logarithmic Functions"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Given that $y=4\\pi\\ln x$, find $\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}y}{\\textrm{d}x}.$"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The derivative of the natural logarithm is the reciprocal function:\n\\[\n\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{ \\textrm{d} x}\\left( \\ln x \\right)= \\dfrac{1}{x}\n\\]\nWe apply our formula and get\n\\begin{align}\n\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}y}{\\textrm{d}x}&amp;=\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}\\left(4\\pi\\ln x\\right)\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=4\\pi\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}\\left(\\ln x\\right)\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=4\\pi\\cdot\\dfrac{1}{x}\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=\\dfrac{4\\pi}{x}.\n\\end{align}"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{4\\pi}{x}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{4}{\\pi x}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{\\pi}{4x}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{4}{x+\\pi}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{x+\\pi}{4}$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Differentiating Logarithmic Functions"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Given that $y=7\\pi\\ln x$, find $\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}y}{\\textrm{d}x}.$"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"The derivative of the natural logarithm is the reciprocal function:\n\\[\n\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{ \\textrm{d} x}\\left( \\ln x \\right)= \\dfrac{1}{x}\n\\]\nWe apply our formula and get\n\\begin{align}\n\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}y}{\\textrm{d}x}&amp;=\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}\\left(7\\pi\\ln x\\right)\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=7\\pi\\cdot \\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}\\left(\\ln x\\right)\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=7\\pi\\cdot\\dfrac{1}{x}\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=\\dfrac{7\\pi}{x}.\n\\end{align}"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{7\\pi}{x}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{7}{\\pi x}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{\\pi}{7x}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{7}{x+\\pi}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{x+\\pi}{7}$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Differentiating Logarithmic Functions"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Given that $y=\\ln x +e^x$, find the instantaneous rate of change at $x=1.$"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"To find the instantaneous rate of change at a given point, we have to find $\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}y} {\\textrm{d}x}$ at that point.\nTaking the derivative, we have\n\\begin{align}\n\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}y}{\\textrm{d}x}&amp;=\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}\\left(\\ln x +e^x\\right)\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=\\dfrac{1}{x}+e^x \\,.\n\\end{align}\nTherefore, the instantaneous rate of change at $x=1$ is\n\\[\n\\eqalign{\n\\left.\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}y} {\\textrm{d}x}\\right|_{x=1}\n&amp;=\\left.\\left(\\dfrac{1}{x}+e^x \\right)\\,\\right|_{x=1}\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=\\dfrac{1}{1}+e^1\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=1+e \\,.\n}\n\\]"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"$1+e$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$e$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$1$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{e}{1+e}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{1+e}{e}$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Differentiating Logarithmic Functions"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Find $f'(x)$ for $f(x) = 2\\ln x - 5e^x.$"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Taking the derivative, we have\n\\begin{eqnarray}\n\\eqalign{\nf'(x) &amp; = \\dfrac {\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x} \\left(2\\ln x - 5e^x\\right) \\\\[5pt]\n&amp; = 2\\left(\\dfrac {1}{x}\\right) - 5e^x \\\\[5pt]\n&amp; = \\dfrac {2}{x} - 5e^x.\n}\n\\end{eqnarray}"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac {2}{x} - 5e^x $"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac {2}{x} - e^x$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac {1}{x} - 5e^x$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac {2x}{3}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$ 5e^x$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Differentiating Logarithmic Functions"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Given that $y=5\\log_7 x -5x+1$, find $\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}y}{\\textrm{d}x}.$"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"To differentiate a logarithm with base $a,$ we use the formula\n\\[\n\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{ \\textrm{d} x}\\left( \\log_a x \\right)= \\dfrac{1}{x\\ln a}.\n\\]\nTherefore,\n\\[\n\\eqalign{\n\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}y}{\\textrm{d}x}&amp;=\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}\\left(5\\log_7 x -5x+1\\right)\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=5\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}\\left(\\log_7 x\\right)-5\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(x)-\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x}(1)\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=5\\cdot\\dfrac{1}{x \\ln 7}-5\\cdot 1+0\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=\\dfrac{5}{x \\ln 7}-5.\n}\n\\]"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{5}{x \\ln 7}-5$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{1}{x \\ln 7}-5$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{5}{x \\ln 7}$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{5}{x \\ln 7}-5x+1$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac{1}{x \\ln 7}-5x+1$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Differentiating Logarithmic Functions"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"$\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}} {\\textrm{d}x} \\left(2^{x} + \\log_2 x\\right) = $"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"To differentiate a logarithm with base $a,$ we use the formula\n\\[\n\\dfrac{\\textrm{d}}{ \\textrm{d} x}\\left( \\log_a x \\right)= \\dfrac{1}{x\\ln a}.\n\\]\nTherefore,\n\\begin{eqnarray}\n\\eqalign{\n\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}} {\\textrm{d}x} \\left(2^{x} + \\log_2 x\\right)\n&amp; = 2^x \\cdot \\ln 2 + \\dfrac {1}{x \\cdot \\ln 2} \\\\[5pt]\n&amp; = 2^x \\ln 2 + \\dfrac {1}{x \\cdot \\ln 2}.\n}\n\\end{eqnarray}"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"$2^x\\ \\ln 2 + \\dfrac {1}{x\\ln 2} $"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$2^x + \\dfrac {1}{x\\ln 2} $"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$2^x + \\dfrac {1}{x} $"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$2^x\\ \\ln 2 + \\dfrac {1}{x} $"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$2^x\\ \\ln 2 + \\dfrac {1}{\\ln 2} $"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
