      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Differentiating Exponential Functions"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Given that $f(x)=3\\cdot 2^x-3x^4-4$, find the instantaneous rate of change at $x=0.$"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Applying the formula, we get\n\\begin{align*}\nf'(x)&amp;= \\dfrac{\\textrm d}{\\textrm d x}\\left(3\\cdot 2^x-3x^4-4\\right)\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= \\dfrac{\\textrm d}{\\textrm d x}\\left(3\\cdot 2^x\\right)-\\dfrac{\\textrm d}{\\textrm d x}\\left(3x^4\\right)-\\dfrac{\\textrm d}{\\textrm d x}\\left(4\\right)\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;= 3\\dfrac{\\textrm d}{\\textrm d x}\\left(2^x\\right)-3\\dfrac{\\textrm d}{\\textrm d x}\\left(x^4\\right)-\\dfrac{\\textrm d}{\\textrm d x}\\left(4\\right)\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=3 \\ln 2 \\cdot 2^{x} - 3 \\cdot 4x^3-0\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=3 \\ln 2 \\cdot 2^{x}-12x^3.\n\\end{align*}\nTherefore, the slope at $x=0$ is\n\\begin{align*}\n\\left.\\dfrac {\\textrm{d}f} {\\textrm{d}x}\\right|_{x=0}\n&amp;=\\left.\\left( 3 \\ln 2 \\cdot 2^{x}-12x^3\\right)\\right|_{x=0}\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=3 \\ln 2 \\cdot 2^{0}-12(0)^3\\\\[5pt]\n&amp;=3 \\ln 2.\n\\end{align*}"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"$3 \\ln 2$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$6 \\ln 2$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$3 \\ln 2 - 4$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$3 \\ln 2 - 12$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\ln 6$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"M"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Calculating the Equation of a Tangent Line Using Differentiation"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Find the equation of the tangent to the curve $y=x^2+5x$ at the point $(-1,-4).$"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Let $f(x)=x^2+5x.$ Computing the derivative $f'(x)$ gives\n\\begin{align*}\nf'(x) = \\dfrac {\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x} \\left(x^2 + 5x\\right)= 2x+5.\n\\end{align*}\nWe then compute $f'(-1)$, which gives\n\\begin{align*}\nf'(-1) = 2(-1)+5 = 3 \\,.\n\\end{align*}\nFinally, we use the point-slope form with $m=3$ and the coordinates of our point $(-1, -4),$ and get\n\\begin{align*}\ny - y_1 &amp;= m(x-x_1) \\\\\ny - (-4) &amp;= 3(x-(-1)) \\\\\ny +4 &amp;= 3(x+1) \\\\\ny &amp; = 3x +3 -4\\\\\ny &amp;= 3x-1.\n\\end{align*}"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"$y=3x-1$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$y=-3x+1$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$y=-3x-1$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$y=2x-3$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$y=3x-6$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Calculating the Equation of a Tangent Line Using Differentiation"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Find the equation of the tangent to the curve $y = x^2 - 3x$ at the point $(-2,10).$"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Let $f(x)=x^2-3x.$ Computing the derivative $f'(x)$ gives\n\\begin{align*}\nf'(x) = \\dfrac {\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x} \\left(x^2 - 3x\\right)= 2x-3.\n\\end{align*}\nWe then compute $f'(-2)$, which gives\n\\begin{align*}\nf'(-2) = 2(-2)-3 = -7.\n\\end{align*}\nFinally, we use the point-slope form with $m=-7$ and the coordinates of our point $(-2, 10),$ and get\n\\begin{align*}\ny - y_1 &amp;= m(x-x_1) \\\\\ny - (10) &amp;= -7(x-(-2)) \\\\\ny - 10 &amp;= -7(x+2) \\\\\ny &amp; = -7x - 14 +10\\\\\ny &amp;= -7x -4.\n\\end{align*}"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"$y = -7x -4$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$y = -7x +4$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$y = 6x -2$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$y = -x -2$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$y = 6x +2$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Calculating the Equation of a Tangent Line Using Differentiation"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Find where the tangent to the curve $y = 3x^2+ 5x$ at the point $(-1,-2)$ intersects the $y$-axis."</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Let $f(x)=3x^2+ 5x.$ Let's first calculate the derivative:\n\\begin{align*}\nf'(x) &amp; = \\dfrac {\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x} \\left(3x^2+ 5x\\right)\n= 6x+5\\\\\n\\end{align*}\nSo, the slope of the tangent at our point is\n\\[\nm=f'(-1) = 6(-1) +5 = -1 \\,.\n\\]\nTherefore, the equation of the tangent is\n\\begin{align}\ny - y_1 &amp;= m(x-x_1) \\\\\ny + 2 &amp;= -(x+1) \\\\\ny &amp; = -x-1-2\\\\\ny &amp;= -x-3.\n\\end{align}\nTo find where the tangent line intercepts the $y$-axis, we set $x=0$ and solve:\n\\begin{align*}\ny &amp; = -0-3\\\\\ny &amp; = -3\n\\end{align*}\nTherefore, the point of intersection is $\\left(0,-3\\right).$"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\left(0,-3\\right)$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\left(0,5\\right)$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\left(0,2\\right)$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\left(0,-1\\right)$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\left(0,0\\right)$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
    },
    {
      <span class="hljs-attr">"topic"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Calculating the Equation of a Tangent Line Using Differentiation"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"question"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Find where the tangent to the curve $y = x^4-5$ at the point $(-1,-4)$ intersects the $x$-axis."</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"explanation"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"Let $f(x)=x^4-5.$ Let's first calculate the derivative:\n\\begin{align*}\nf'(x) &amp; = \\dfrac {\\textrm{d}}{\\textrm{d}x} \\left(x^4-5\\right)\n= 4x^3\n\\end{align*}\nSo, the slope of the tangent at our point is\n\\[\nm=f'(-1) = 4(-1)^3 = -4.\n\\]\nTherefore, the equation of the tangent is\n\\begin{align}\ny - y_1 &amp;= m(x-x_1) \\\\\ny - (-4) &amp;= -4(x+1) \\\\\ny &amp; = -4x-4-4\\\\\ny &amp;= -4x-8.\n\\end{align}\nTo find where the tangent line intercepts the $x$-axis, we set $y=0$ and solve:\n\\begin{align*}\n0 &amp; = -4x-8\\\\\nx &amp; = -2\n\\end{align*}\nTherefore, the point of intersection is $\\left(-2, 0\\right).$"</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"choices"</span>: [
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\left(-2, 0\\right)$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\left(0, 1\\right)$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\left(0, 0\\right)$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\left(-5, 0\\right)$"</span>,
        <span class="hljs-string">"$\\left(4, 0\\right)$"</span>
      ],
      <span class="hljs-attr">"correctAnswer"</span>: <span class="hljs-number">0</span>,
      <span class="hljs-attr">"difficulty"</span>: <span class="hljs-string">"E"</span>
    }
  ]
}
</pre>
        </td>
      </tr>
    
  </tbody>
</table>

  </div>
</body>
</html>
